Rotherham.label: The Emphasised Bible
Rotherham.overlayPolicy: mismatch chapters stored under translationOverlays.Rotherham without altering target verse sequence
Rotherham.emptySourceRowsPolicy: empty Rotherham source rows are governed omissions and are not imported as blank text

KJV.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

NABRE.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

text.NRSV: In the first book, Theophilus, I wrote about all that Jesus did and taught from the beginning
text.Rotherham: The first narrative, indeed, made I, concerning all things, O Theophilus, which Jesus began both to do and teach,—
text.KJV: The former treatise have I made, O Theophilus, of all that Jesus began both to do and teach,
text.NABRE: In the first book, Theophilus, I dealt with all that Jesus did and taught
text.DRB: The first treatise I made of all things, O Theophilus, which Jesus began to do and to teach,

text.NRSV: until the day when he was taken up to heaven, after giving instructions through the Holy Spirit to the apostles whom he had chosen.
text.Rotherham: Until the day when, having given command unto the apostles, whom through Holy Spirit he had chosen, he was taken up;
text.KJV: Until the day in which he was taken up, after that he through the Holy Ghost had given commandments unto the apostles whom he had chosen:
text.NABRE: until the day he was taken up, after giving instructions through the holy Spirit to the apostles whom he had chosen.
text.DRB: until the day wherein giving commandment by the Holy Ghost to the Apostles whom he chose, he was assumpted.

text.NRSV: After his suffering he presented himself alive to them by many convincing proofs, appearing to them during forty days and speaking about the kingdom of God.
text.Rotherham: Unto whom he also presented himself alive, after he had suffered, by many sure tokens, throughout forty days making himself visible unto them, and speaking the things concerning the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: To whom also he shewed himself alive after his passion by many infallible proofs, being seen of them forty days, and speaking of the things pertaining to the kingdom of God:
text.NABRE: He presented himself alive to them by many proofs after he had suffered, appearing to them during forty days and speaking about the kingdom of God.
text.DRB: to whom he shewed also himself alive after his passion in many arguments, for forty days appearing to them, and speaking of the Kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: While staying with them, he ordered them not to leave Jerusalem, but to wait there for the promise of the Father. “This,” he said, “is what you have heard from me;
text.Rotherham: And being in company with them he charged them, from Jerusalem, not to absent themselves, but—To abide around the promise of the Father which ye have heard of me,
text.KJV: And, being assembled together with them, commanded them that they should not depart from Jerusalem, but wait for the promise of the Father, which, saith he, ye have heard of me.
text.NABRE: While meeting with them, he enjoined them not to depart from Jerusalem, but to wait for “the promise of the Father about which you have heard me speak;
text.DRB: And eating with them, he commanded them, that they should not depart from Jerusalem, but should expect the promise of the Father, which you have heard (saith he) by my mouth:

text.NRSV: for John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit not many days from now.”
text.Rotherham: Because, John, indeed, immersed with water; but, ye, in Holy Spirit shall be immersed,—after not many of these days.
text.KJV: For John truly baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost not many days hence.
text.NABRE: for John baptized with water, but in a few days you will be baptized with the holy Spirit.”
text.DRB: for John indeed baptized with water, but you shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost after these few days.

text.NRSV: So when they had come together, they asked him, “Lord, is this the time when you will restore the kingdom to Israel?”
text.Rotherham: They, therefore, having come together, began to question him, saying—Lord! art thou at this time duly establishing the kingdom unto Israel?
text.KJV: When they therefore were come together, they asked of him, saying, Lord, wilt thou at this time restore again the kingdom to Israel?
text.NABRE: The Ascension of Jesus. When they had gathered together they asked him, “Lord, are you at this time going to restore the kingdom to Israel?”
text.DRB: They therefore that were assembled, asked him, saying: Lord, whether at this time wilt thou restore the Kingdom to Israel?

text.NRSV: He replied, “It is not for you to know the times or periods that the Father has set by his own authority.
text.Rotherham: He said unto them—It is not yours to get to know times or seasons which the Father hath put in his own authority;
text.KJV: And he said unto them, It is not for you to know the times or the seasons, which the Father hath put in his own power.
text.NABRE: He answered them, “It is not for you to know the times or seasons that the Father has established by his own authority.
text.DRB: but he said to them: It is not for you to know times or moments, which the Father hath put in his own power:

text.NRSV: But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you; and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth.”
text.Rotherham: But ye shall receive power when the Holy Spirit cometh upon you, and ye shall be my witnesses, both in Jerusalem and [in] all Judaea and Samaria and as far as the uttermost part of the land.
text.KJV: But ye shall receive power, after that the Holy Ghost is come upon you: and ye shall be witnesses unto me both in Jerusalem, and in all Judea, and in Samaria, and unto the uttermost part of the earth.
text.NABRE: But you will receive power when the holy Spirit comes upon you, and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, throughout Judea and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth.”
text.DRB: but you shall receive the virtue of the Holy Ghost coming upon you, and you shall be witnesses unto me in Jerusalem, and in all Jewry, and Samaria, and even to the utmost of the earth.

text.NRSV: When he had said this, as they were watching, he was lifted up, and a cloud took him out of their sight.
text.Rotherham: And having said these things, as they were beholding, he was lifted up, and a cloud caught him away from their eyes.
text.KJV: And when he had spoken these things, while they beheld, he was taken up; and a cloud received him out of their sight.
text.NABRE: When he had said this, as they were looking on, he was lifted up, and a cloud took him from their sight.
text.DRB: And when he had said these things, in their sight he was elevated: & a cloud received him out of their sight.

text.NRSV: While he was going and they were gazing up toward heaven, suddenly two men in white robes stood by them.
text.Rotherham: And, while they were looking steadfastly into heaven as he was going his way, then lo! two men had taken their stand beside them, in white garments,—
text.KJV: And while they looked stedfastly toward heaven as he went up, behold, two men stood by them in white apparel;
text.NABRE: While they were looking intently at the sky as he was going, suddenly two men dressed in white garments stood beside them.
text.DRB: And when they beheld him going into Heaven, behold two men stood beside them in white garments,

text.NRSV: They said, “Men of Galilee, why do you stand looking up toward heaven? This Jesus, who has been taken up from you into heaven, will come in the same way as you saw him go into heaven.”
text.Rotherham: Who also said—Men of Galilee! why stand ye looking into heaven? This Jesus who hath been taken up from you into heaven, shall so come, in like manner as ye yourselves have gazed upon him going into heaven.
text.KJV: Which also said, Ye men of Galilee, why stand ye gazing up into heaven? this same Jesus, which is taken up from you into heaven, shall so come in like manner as ye have seen him go into heaven.
text.NABRE: They said, “Men of Galilee, why are you standing there looking at the sky? This Jesus who has been taken up from you into heaven will return in the same way as you have seen him going into heaven.”
text.DRB: who also said: Ye men of Galilee, why stand you looking into Heaven? This Jesus which is assumpted from you into Heaven, shall so come as you have seen him going into Heaven.

text.NRSV: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the mount called Olivet, which is near Jerusalem, a sabbath day’s journey away.
text.Rotherham: Then returned they into Jerusalem, from a mountain called Olivet, which is nigh unto Jerusalem a Sabbath day’s journey.
text.KJV: Then returned they unto Jerusalem from the mount called Olivet, which is from Jerusalem a sabbath day’s journey.
text.NABRE: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the mount called Olivet, which is near Jerusalem, a sabbath day’s journey away.
text.DRB: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the mount that is called Olivet, which is by Jerusalem, distant a Sabboth's journey.

text.NRSV: When they had entered the city, they went to the room upstairs where they were staying, Peter, and John, and James, and Andrew, Philip and Thomas, Bartholomew and Matthew, James son of Alphaeus, and Simon the Zealot, and Judas son of James.
text.Rotherham: And, when they had entered, into the upper-story went they up, where remained behind—both Peter and John and James and Andrew, Philip and Thomas, Bartholomew and Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus and Simon the zealot, and Judas the son of James.
text.KJV: And when they were come in, they went up into an upper room, where abode both Peter, and James, and John, and Andrew, Philip, and Thomas, Bartholomew, and Matthew, James the son of Alpheus, and Simon Zelotes, and Judas the brother of James.
text.NABRE: The First Community in Jerusalem. When they entered the city they went to the upper room where they were staying, Peter and John and James and Andrew, Philip and Thomas, Bartholomew and Matthew, James son of Alphaeus, Simon the Zealot, and Judas son of James.
text.DRB: And when they were entered in, they went up into an upper chamber, where abode Peter & John, James and Andrew, Philippe and Thomas, Bartholomew and Matthew, James of Alphæus and Simon Zelotes, and Jude of James.

text.NRSV: All these were constantly devoting themselves to prayer, together with certain women, including Mary the mother of Jesus, as well as his brothers.
text.Rotherham: These all were giving constant attention with one accord unto the prayer, with [certain] women, and Mary the mother of Jesus, and with his brethren.
text.KJV: These all continued with one accord in prayer and supplication, with the women, and Mary the mother of Jesus, and with his brethren.
text.NABRE: All these devoted themselves with one accord to prayer, together with some women, and Mary the mother of Jesus, and his brothers.
text.DRB: All these were persevering with one mind in prayer with the women and Mary the mother of Jesus, and his brethren.

text.NRSV: In those days Peter stood up among the believers (together the crowd numbered about one hundred twenty persons) and said,
text.Rotherham: And, in these days, Peter, standing up in the midst of the brethren, said,—and there was a multitude of names, of one accord, about a hundred and twenty,—
text.KJV: And in those days Peter stood up in the midst of the disciples, and said, (the number of names together were about an hundred and twenty,)
text.NABRE: The Choice of Judas’s Successor. During those days Peter stood up in the midst of the brothers (there was a group of about one hundred and twenty persons in the one place). He said,
text.DRB: In those days Peter rising up in the midst of the Brethren, said: (and the multitude of persons together, was almost an hundred and twenty.)

text.NRSV: “Friends, the scripture had to be fulfilled, which the Holy Spirit through David foretold concerning Judas, who became a guide for those who arrested Jesus—
text.Rotherham: Brethren! It was needful for the Scripture to be fulfilled which the Holy Spirit spake beforehand through the mouth of David, concerning Judas,—who became guide unto them who apprehended Jesus;
text.KJV: Men and brethren, this scripture must needs have been fulfilled, which the Holy Ghost by the mouth of David spake before concerning Judas, which was guide to them that took Jesus.
text.NABRE: “My brothers, the scripture had to be fulfilled which the holy Spirit spoke beforehand through the mouth of David, concerning Judas, who was the guide for those who arrested Jesus.
text.DRB: You men, brethren, the scripture must be fulfilled which the Holy Ghost spake before by the mouth of David concerning Judas, who was the captain of them that apprehended Jesus:

text.NRSV: for he was numbered among us and was allotted his share in this ministry.”
text.Rotherham: For that he had come to be reckoned among us and obtained the lot of this ministry.—
text.KJV: For he was numbered with us, and had obtained part of this ministry.
text.NABRE: He was numbered among us and was allotted a share in this ministry.
text.DRB: who was numbered among us and obtained the lot of this ministry.

text.NRSV: (Now this man acquired a field with the reward of his wickedness; and falling headlong, he burst open in the middle and all his bowels gushed out.
text.Rotherham: This man therefore had possessed himself of a field out of the reward of unrighteousness,—and falling headlong burst asunder in the midst, and forth gushed all his bowels;
text.KJV: Now this man purchased a field with the reward of iniquity; and falling headlong, he burst asunder in the midst, and all his bowels gushed out.
text.NABRE: He bought a parcel of land with the wages of his iniquity, and falling headlong, he burst open in the middle, and all his insides spilled out.
text.DRB: And he indeed hath possessed a field of the reward of iniquity, and being hanged he burst in the midst, and all his bowels gushed out.

text.NRSV: This became known to all the residents of Jerusalem, so that the field was called in their language Hakeldama, that is, Field of Blood.)
text.Rotherham: And it became known unto all them who were dwelling in Jerusalem, so that that field was called, in their language, Akeldama, that is, Field of Blood.—
text.KJV: And it was known unto all the dwellers at Jerusalem; insomuch as that field is called in their proper tongue, Aceldama, that is to say, The field of blood.
text.NABRE: This became known to everyone who lived in Jerusalem, so that the parcel of land was called in their language ‘Akeldama,’ that is, Field of Blood.
text.DRB: And it was made notorious to all the inhabitants of Jerusalem: so that the same field was called in their tongue, Hacel-dema, that is to say, the field of blood.

text.NRSV: “For it is written in the book of Psalms, ‘Let his homestead become desolate, and let there be no one to live in it’; and ‘Let another take his position of overseer.’
text.Rotherham: For it is written in the book of Psalms: Let his encampment become desolate, and let there be none to dwell therein! And—his overseership, let a different man take!
text.KJV: For it is written in the book of Psalms, Let his habitation be desolate, and let no man dwell therein: and his bishoprick let another take.
text.NABRE: For it is written in the Book of Psalms: ‘Let his encampment become desolate, and may no one dwell in it.’ And: ‘May another take his office.’
text.DRB: For it is written in the book of Psalms: Be their habitation made desert, & be there none to dwell in it. And his Bishopric let another take.

text.NRSV: So one of the men who have accompanied us during all the time that the Lord Jesus went in and out among us,
text.Rotherham: It is needful then that, of the men who companied with us during all the time in which the Lord Jesus came in and went out over us,
text.KJV: Wherefore of these men which have companied with us all the time that the Lord Jesus went in and out among us,
text.NABRE: Therefore, it is necessary that one of the men who accompanied us the whole time the Lord Jesus came and went among us,
text.DRB: Therefore, of these men that have assembled with us, all the time that our Lord Jesus went in and went out among us,

text.NRSV: beginning from the baptism of John until the day when he was taken up from us—one of these must become a witness with us to his resurrection.”
text.Rotherham: Beginning from the immersion by John until the day when he was taken up from us, that, a witness of his resurrection along with us, should one of these become.
text.KJV: Beginning from the baptism of John, unto that same day that he was taken up from us, must one be ordained to be a witness with us of his resurrection.
text.NABRE: beginning from the baptism of John until the day on which he was taken up from us, become with us a witness to his resurrection.”
text.DRB: beginning from the Baptism of John until the day wherein he was assumpted from us, there must one of these be made a witness with us of his resurrection.

text.NRSV: So they proposed two, Joseph called Barsabbas, who was also known as Justus, and Matthias.
text.Rotherham: And they appointed two,—Joseph, called Barsabbas, who was surnamed Justus, and Matthias.
text.KJV: And they appointed two, Joseph called Barsabas, who was surnamed Justus, and Matthias.
text.NABRE: So they proposed two, Joseph called Barsabbas, who was also known as Justus, and Matthias.
text.DRB: And they appointed two, Joseph, who was called Barsabas, who was surnamed Justus: and Matthias.

text.NRSV: Then they prayed and said, “Lord, you know everyone’s heart. Show us which one of these two you have chosen
text.Rotherham: And, praying, they said—Thou Lord! observer of the hearts of all men! shew forth whom thou hast chosen,—of these two, one;—
text.KJV: And they prayed, and said, Thou, Lord, which knowest the hearts of all men, shew whether of these two thou hast chosen,
text.NABRE: Then they prayed, “You, Lord, who know the hearts of all, show which one of these two you have chosen
text.DRB: And praying they said: Thou Lord that knowest the hearts of all men, shew of these two, one, whom thou hast chosen,

text.NRSV: to take the place in this ministry and apostleship from which Judas turned aside to go to his own place.”
text.Rotherham: To take the place of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas went aside, to go his way unto his own place.
text.KJV: That he may take part of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas by transgression fell, that he might go to his own place.
text.NABRE: to take the place in this apostolic ministry from which Judas turned away to go to his own place.”
text.DRB: to take the place of this ministry & Apostleship, from the which Judas hath prevaricated that he might go to his own place.

text.NRSV: And they cast lots for them, and the lot fell on Matthias; and he was added to the eleven apostles.
text.Rotherham: And they gave lots for them; and the lot fell upon Matthias, and he was numbered with the eleven apostles.
text.KJV: And they gave forth their lots; and the lot fell upon Matthias; and he was numbered with the eleven apostles.
text.NABRE: Then they gave lots to them, and the lot fell upon Matthias, and he was counted with the eleven apostles.
text.DRB: And they gave them lots, and the lot fell upon Matthias, and he was numbered with the eleven Apostles.

text.NRSV: When the day of Pentecost had come, they were all together in one place.
text.Rotherham: And, when the day of pentecost was filling up [the number of days] they were all together with one intent;—
text.KJV: And when the day of Pentecost was fully come, they were all with one accord in one place.
text.NABRE: When the time for Pentecost was fulfilled, they were all in one place together.
text.DRB: And when the days of Pentecost were accomplished, they were all together in one place:

text.NRSV: And suddenly from heaven there came a sound like the rush of a violent wind, and it filled the entire house where they were sitting.
text.Rotherham: When there came suddenly out of heaven a sound, just as of a mighty rushing wind,—and it filled all the house where they were sitting;
text.KJV: And suddenly there came a sound from heaven as of a rushing mighty wind, and it filled all the house where they were sitting.
text.NABRE: And suddenly there came from the sky a noise like a strong driving wind, and it filled the entire house in which they were.
text.DRB: and suddenly there was made a sound from Heaven, as of a vehement wind coming, & it filled the whole house where they were sitting.

text.NRSV: Divided tongues, as of fire, appeared among them, and a tongue rested on each of them.
text.Rotherham: And there appeared unto them—parting asunder—tongues like as of fire, and it sat upon each one of them;
text.KJV: And there appeared unto them cloven tongues like as of fire, and it sat upon each of them.
text.NABRE: Then there appeared to them tongues as of fire, which parted and came to rest on each one of them.
text.DRB: And there appeared to them parted tongues as it were of fire, and it sat upon every one of them:

text.NRSV: All of them were filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other languages, as the Spirit gave them ability.
text.Rotherham: And they were all filled with Holy Spirit, and began to be speaking with other kinds of tongues just as the Spirit was giving unto them to be sounding forth.
text.KJV: And they were all filled with the Holy Ghost, and began to speak with other tongues, as the Spirit gave them utterance.
text.NABRE: And they were all filled with the holy Spirit and began to speak in different tongues, as the Spirit enabled them to proclaim.
text.DRB: and they were all replenished with the Holy Ghost, and they began to speak with diverse tongues, according as the Holy Ghost gave them to speak.

text.NRSV: Now there were devout Jews from every nation under heaven living in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Now there were in Jerusalem sojourning Jews, reverent men from every nation who were under heaven;
text.KJV: And there were dwelling at Jerusalem Jews, devout men, out of every nation under heaven.
text.NABRE: Now there were devout Jews from every nation under heaven staying in Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And there were dwelling at Jerusalem Jews, devout men of every nation that is under Heaven.

text.NRSV: And at this sound the crowd gathered and was bewildered, because each one heard them speaking in the native language of each.
text.Rotherham: And this sound occurring the throng came together and was thrown into confusion, because each one severally heard in his own language them who were speaking;
text.KJV: Now when this was noised abroad, the multitude came together, and were confounded, because that every man heard them speak in his own language.
text.NABRE: At this sound, they gathered in a large crowd, but they were confused because each one heard them speaking in his own language.
text.DRB: And when this voice was made, the multitude came together, and was astonished in mind, because every man heard them speak in his own tongue.

text.NRSV: Amazed and astonished, they asked, “Are not all these who are speaking Galileans?
text.Rotherham: Yea they were beside themselves and did marvel, saying—Lo! are not all these who are speaking Galilaeans?
text.KJV: And they were all amazed and marvelled, saying one to another, Behold, are not all these which speak Galileans?
text.NABRE: They were astounded, and in amazement they asked, “Are not all these people who are speaking Galileans?
text.DRB: And they were all amazed, and marveled saying: Are not, lo, all these that speak, Galilæans,

text.NRSV: And how is it that we hear, each of us, in our own native language?
text.Rotherham: How then do, we, hear each one in our own language in which we were born?
text.KJV: And how hear we every man in our own tongue, wherein we were born?
text.NABRE: Then how does each of us hear them in his own native language?
text.DRB: and how have we heard, each man our own tongue wherein we were born?

text.NRSV: Parthians, Medes, Elamites, and residents of Mesopotamia, Judea and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia,
text.Rotherham: Parthians and Medes and Elamites, and those dwelling in Mesopotamia, Judaea also and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia,
text.KJV: Parthians, and Medes, and Elamites, and the dwellers in Mesopotamia, and in Judea, and Cappadocia, in Pontus, and Asia,
text.NABRE: We are Parthians, Medes, and Elamites, inhabitants of Mesopotamia, Judea and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia,
text.DRB: Parthians, & Medians, & Elamites, & that inhabit Mesopotamia, Jewry, & Cappadocia, Pontus, and Asia,

text.NRSV: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the parts of Libya belonging to Cyrene, and visitors from Rome, both Jews and proselytes,
text.Rotherham: Phrygia also and Pamphylia, Egypt and the parts of the Libya that is towards Cyrene, and the sojourning Romans,—both Jews and proselytes,
text.KJV: Phrygia, and Pamphylia, in Egypt, and in the parts of Libya about Cyrene, and strangers of Rome, Jews and proselytes,
text.NABRE: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the districts of Libya near Cyrene, as well as travelers from Rome,
text.DRB: Phrygia, and Pamphilia, Ægypt & the parts of Libya that is about Cyrenee, & strangers of Rome,

text.NRSV: Cretans and Arabs—in our own languages we hear them speaking about God’s deeds of power.”
text.Rotherham: Cretans and Arabians, we do hear them speaking in our own tongues the magnificent things of God.
text.KJV: Cretes and Arabians, we do hear them speak in our tongues the wonderful works of God.
text.NABRE: both Jews and converts to Judaism, Cretans and Arabs, yet we hear them speaking in our own tongues of the mighty acts of God.”
text.DRB: Jews also, and Proselytes, Cretensians, and Arabians: we have heard them speak in our own tongues the great works of God.

text.NRSV: All were amazed and perplexed, saying to one another, “What does this mean?”
text.Rotherham: But they were all beside themselves and were utterly at a loss, saying one to another—What doth this please to be?
text.KJV: And they were all amazed, and were in doubt, saying one to another, What meaneth this?
text.NABRE: They were all astounded and bewildered, and said to one another, “What does this mean?”
text.DRB: And they were all astonished, and marveled, saying one to another: What meaneth this?

text.NRSV: But others sneered and said, “They are filled with new wine.”
text.Rotherham: While, others, in mockery were saying—With sweet wine, are they drunken!
text.KJV: Others mocking said, These men are full of new wine.
text.NABRE: But others said, scoffing, “They have had too much new wine.”
text.DRB: But others deriding said: That these are full of new wine.

text.NRSV: But Peter, standing with the eleven, raised his voice and addressed them, “Men of Judea and all who live in Jerusalem, let this be known to you, and listen to what I say.
text.Rotherham: But, taking his stand, Peter with the eleven lifted up his voice, and sounded out unto them—Ye men of Judaea, and all ye who are sojourning in Jerusalem! Let, this, unto you be known, and give ear unto my declarations;—
text.KJV: But Peter, standing up with the eleven, lifted up his voice, and said unto them, Ye men of Judea, and all ye that dwell at Jerusalem, be this known unto you, and hearken to my words:
text.NABRE: Then Peter stood up with the Eleven, raised his voice, and proclaimed to them, “You who are Jews, indeed all of you staying in Jerusalem. Let this be known to you, and listen to my words.
text.DRB: But Peter standing with the Eleven, lifted up his voice; and spake to them: Ye men, Jews, and all you that dwell in Jerusalem, be this known to you, and with your ears receive my words.

text.NRSV: Indeed, these are not drunk, as you suppose, for it is only nine o’clock in the morning.
text.Rotherham: For not, as, ye, suppose, are these men drunken, for it is the third hour of the day;—
text.KJV: For these are not drunken, as ye suppose, seeing it is but the third hour of the day.
text.NABRE: These people are not drunk, as you suppose, for it is only nine o’clock in the morning.
text.DRB: For these are not drunk, as you suppose, whereas it is the third hour of the day:

text.NRSV: No, this is what was spoken through the prophet Joel:
text.Rotherham: But this is that which hath been spoken through the prophet Joel—
text.KJV: But this is that which was spoken by the prophet Joel;
text.NABRE: No, this is what was spoken through the prophet Joel:
text.DRB: But this is it that was said by the Prophet Joel:

text.NRSV: ‘In the last days it will be, God declares, that I will pour out my Spirit upon all flesh, and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, and your young men shall see visions, and your old men shall dream dreams.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be, in the last days saith God, I will pour out of my Spirit upon all flesh; and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, and, your young men, visions shall see and, your elders, in dreams shall dream,—
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass in the last days, saith God, I will pour out of my Spirit upon all flesh: and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, and your young men shall see visions, and your old men shall dream dreams:
text.NABRE: ‘It will come to pass in the last days,’ God says, ‘that I will pour out a portion of my spirit upon all flesh. Your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, your young men shall see visions, your old men shall dream dreams.
text.DRB: And it shall be, in the last days (saith our Lord) of my Spirit I will pour out upon all flesh: and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, and your young men shall see visions, and your Ancients shall dream dreams.

text.NRSV: Even upon my slaves, both men and women, in those days I will pour out my Spirit; and they shall prophesy.
text.Rotherham: And, even upon my men-servants and upon my maid-servants in those days, will I pour out of my Spirit, and they shall prophesy;
text.KJV: And on my servants and on my handmaidens I will pour out in those days of my Spirit; and they shall prophesy:
text.NABRE: Indeed, upon my servants and my handmaids I will pour out a portion of my spirit in those days, and they shall prophesy.
text.DRB: and upon my servants truly, and upon my handmaids will I pour out in those days, of my Spirit, and they shall prophesy:

text.NRSV: And I will show portents in the heaven above and signs on the earth below, blood, and fire, and smoky mist.
text.Rotherham: And I will set forth wonders in the heaven above, and signs upon the earth beneath,—blood and fire and vapour of smoke:
text.KJV: And I will shew wonders in heaven above, and signs in the earth beneath; blood, and fire, and vapour of smoke:
text.NABRE: And I will work wonders in the heavens above and signs on the earth below: blood, fire, and a cloud of smoke.
text.DRB: and I will give wonders in the Heaven above, and signs in the earth beneath, blood and fire, and vapour of smoke.

text.NRSV: The sun shall be turned to darkness and the moon to blood, before the coming of the Lord’s great and glorious day.
text.Rotherham: The sun, shall be turned into darkness and, the moon, into blood,—before the coming of the day of the Lord, the great and manifest [day];
text.KJV: The sun shall be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood, before that great and notable day of the Lord come:
text.NABRE: The sun shall be turned to darkness, and the moon to blood, before the coming of the great and splendid day of the Lord,
text.DRB: The sun shall be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood, before the great and manifest day of our Lord doth come.

text.NRSV: Then everyone who calls on the name of the Lord shall be saved.’
text.Rotherham: And it shall be—Whosoever shall call upon the name of the Lord, shall be saved.
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass, that whosoever shall call on the name of the Lord shall be saved.
text.NABRE: and it shall be that everyone shall be saved who calls on the name of the Lord.’
text.DRB: And it shall be, every one whosoever calleth upon the name of our Lord, shall be saved.

text.NRSV: “You that are Israelites, listen to what I have to say: Jesus of Nazareth, a man attested to you by God with deeds of power, wonders, and signs that God did through him among you, as you yourselves know—
text.Rotherham: Ye men of Israel! hear these words:—Jesus the Nazarene, a man pointed out of God unto you by mighty works and wonders and signs, which God did through him in your midst, just as ye yourselves know,
text.KJV: Ye men of Israel, hear these words; Jesus of Nazareth, a man approved of God among you by miracles and wonders and signs, which God did by him in the midst of you, as ye yourselves also know:
text.NABRE: You who are Israelites, hear these words. Jesus the Nazorean was a man commended to you by God with mighty deeds, wonders, and signs, which God worked through him in your midst, as you yourselves know.
text.DRB: Ye men of Israel hear these words: Jesus of Nazareth a man approved of God among you, by miracles & wonders and signs which God did by him in the midst of you, as you also know;

text.NRSV: this man, handed over to you according to the definite plan and foreknowledge of God, you crucified and killed by the hands of those outside the law.
text.Rotherham: The same, by the marked out counsel and foreknowledge of God given up, through the hands of lawless men, suspending, ye slew,
text.KJV: Him, being delivered by the determinate counsel and foreknowledge of God, ye have taken, and by wicked hands have crucified and slain:
text.NABRE: This man, delivered up by the set plan and foreknowledge of God, you killed, using lawless men to crucify him.
text.DRB: this same, by the determinate counsel & prescience of God being delivered, you by the hands of wicked men have crucified and slain.

text.NRSV: But God raised him up, having freed him from death, because it was impossible for him to be held in its power.
text.Rotherham: Whom, God, raised up, loosing the pangs of death, inasmuch as it was, not possible, for him to continue held fast by it.
text.KJV: Whom God hath raised up, having loosed the pains of death: because it was not possible that he should be holden of it.
text.NABRE: But God raised him up, releasing him from the throes of death, because it was impossible for him to be held by it.
text.DRB: Whom God hath raised up loosing the sorrows of hell, according as it was impossible that he should be holden of it.

text.NRSV: For David says concerning him, ‘I saw the Lord always before me, for he is at my right hand so that I will not be shaken;
text.Rotherham: For, David, saith concerning him—I foresaw the Lord before me continually, because he is, on my right hand, that I may not be shaken;
text.KJV: For David speaketh concerning him, I foresaw the Lord always before my face, for he is on my right hand, that I should not be moved:
text.NABRE: For David says of him: ‘I saw the Lord ever before me, with him at my right hand I shall not be disturbed.
text.DRB: For David saith concerning him: I foresaw the Lord in my sight always: because he is at my right hand that I be not moved.

text.NRSV: therefore my heart was glad, and my tongue rejoiced; moreover my flesh will live in hope.
text.Rotherham: For this reason, was my heart made glad and my tongue exulted,—ye further, even my flesh, shall encamp on hope:
text.KJV: Therefore did my heart rejoice, and my tongue was glad; moreover also my flesh shall rest in hope:
text.NABRE: Therefore my heart has been glad and my tongue has exulted; my flesh, too, will dwell in hope,
text.DRB: For this, my heart hath been glad and my tongue hath rejoiced: moreover my flesh also shall rest in hope.

text.NRSV: For you will not abandon my soul to Hades, or let your Holy One experience corruption.
text.Rotherham: Because thou wilt not abandon my soul unto hades, neither wilt thou give thy man of lovingkindness to see corruption;
text.KJV: Because thou wilt not leave my soul in hell, neither wilt thou suffer thine Holy One to see corruption.
text.NABRE: because you will not abandon my soul to the netherworld, nor will you suffer your holy one to see corruption.
text.DRB: Because thou wilt not leave my soul in hell, nor give thy Holy one to see corruption.

text.NRSV: You have made known to me the ways of life; you will make me full of gladness with your presence.’
text.Rotherham: Thou madest known unto me paths of life, thou wilt make me full of gladness with thy countenance.
text.KJV: Thou hast made known to me the ways of life; thou shalt make me full of joy with thy countenance.
text.NABRE: You have made known to me the paths of life; you will fill me with joy in your presence.’
text.DRB: Thou hast made known to me the ways of life: thou shalt make me full of joyfulness with thy face.

text.NRSV: “Fellow Israelites, I may say to you confidently of our ancestor David that he both died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this day.
text.Rotherham: Brethren! it is, allowable, to say with freedom of speech unto you, concerning the patriarch David,—that he both died and was buried, and, his tomb, is among us until this day.
text.KJV: Men and brethren, let me freely speak unto you of the patriarch David, that he is both dead and buried, and his sepulchre is with us unto this day.
text.NABRE: My brothers, one can confidently say to you about the patriarch David that he died and was buried, and his tomb is in our midst to this day.
text.DRB: Ye men, Brethren, let me boldly speak to you of the Patriarch David: that he died, and was buried; and his sepulchre is with us until this present day.

text.NRSV: Since he was a prophet, he knew that God had sworn with an oath to him that he would put one of his descendants on his throne.
text.Rotherham: Being then, a prophet, and knowing that, with an oath, God had sworn unto him, of the fruit of his loins, to seat on his throne,
text.KJV: Therefore being a prophet, and knowing that God had sworn with an oath to him, that of the fruit of his loins, according to the flesh, he would raise up Christ to sit on his throne;
text.NABRE: But since he was a prophet and knew that God had sworn an oath to him that he would set one of his descendants upon his throne,
text.DRB: Whereas therefore he was a Prophet, and knew that by an oath God hath sworn to him, that of the fruit of his loins there should sit upon his seat;

text.NRSV: Foreseeing this, David spoke of the resurrection of the Messiah, saying, ‘He was not abandoned to Hades, nor did his flesh experience corruption.’
text.Rotherham: With foresight, spake he concerning the resurrection of the Christ—that neither was he abandoned unto hades, nor did his flesh see corruption.
text.KJV: He seeing this before spake of the resurrection of Christ, that his soul was not left in hell, neither his flesh did see corruption.
text.NABRE: he foresaw and spoke of the resurrection of the Messiah, that neither was he abandoned to the netherworld nor did his flesh see corruption.
text.DRB: foreseeing, he spake of the resurrection of Christ, for neither was he left in hell, neither did his flesh see corruption.

text.NRSV: This Jesus God raised up, and of that all of us are witnesses.
text.Rotherham: The same Jesus, hath God raised up, whereof, all we, are witnesses!
text.KJV: This Jesus hath God raised up, whereof we all are witnesses.
text.NABRE: God raised this Jesus; of this we are all witnesses.
text.DRB: This Jesus hath God raised again, whereof all we are witnesses.

text.NRSV: Being therefore exalted at the right hand of God, and having received from the Father the promise of the Holy Spirit, he has poured out this that you both see and hear.
text.Rotherham: By the right hand of God, therefore, having been exalted, also, the promise of the Holy Spirit, having received from the Father, He hath poured out this which, yourselves, do see and hear.
text.KJV: Therefore being by the right hand of God exalted, and having received of the Father the promise of the Holy Ghost, he hath shed forth this, which ye now see and hear.
text.NABRE: Exalted at the right hand of God, he received the promise of the holy Spirit from the Father and poured it forth, as you [both] see and hear.
text.DRB: Being exalted therefore by the right hand of God, and having received of his Father the promise of the Holy Ghost, he hath poured out this whom you see and hear.

text.NRSV: For David did not ascend into the heavens, but he himself says, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand,
text.Rotherham: For, David, hath not ascended into the heavens; but he saith, himself,—Said the Lord unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand,
text.KJV: For David is not ascended into the heavens: but he saith himself, The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand,
text.NABRE: For David did not go up into heaven, but he himself said: ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand
text.DRB: For David ascended not into Heaven; but he saith: Our Lord hath said to my Lord, sit on my right hand.

text.NRSV: until I make your enemies your footstool.” ’
text.Rotherham: Until I make thy foes thy footstool.
text.KJV: Until I make thy foes thy footstool.
text.NABRE: until I make your enemies your footstool.”’
text.DRB: until I make thine enemies the foot-stool of thy feet.

text.NRSV: Therefore let the entire house of Israel know with certainty that God has made him both Lord and Messiah, this Jesus whom you crucified.”
text.Rotherham: Assuredly, then, let all the house of Israel know: that, both Lord and Christ, hath God made him, even the same Jesus whom, ye, crucified!
text.KJV: Therefore let all the house of Israel know assuredly, that God hath made that same Jesus, whom ye have crucified, both Lord and Christ.
text.NABRE: Therefore let the whole house of Israel know for certain that God has made him both Lord and Messiah, this Jesus whom you crucified.”
text.DRB: Therefore let all the house of Israel know most certainly that God hath made him both Lord, & Christ, this Jesus, whom you have crucified.

text.NRSV: Now when they heard this, they were cut to the heart and said to Peter and to the other apostles, “Brothers, what should we do?”
text.Rotherham: And, when they heard this, they were pricked to the heart, and said unto Peter and the rest of the apostles—What are we to do, brethren?
text.KJV: Now when they heard this, they were pricked in their heart, and said unto Peter and to the rest of the apostles, Men and brethren, what shall we do?
text.NABRE: Now when they heard this, they were cut to the heart, and they asked Peter and the other apostles, “What are we to do, my brothers?”
text.DRB: And hearing these things, they were compunct in heart, and said to Peter and to the rest of the Apostles: What shall we do men, brethren.

text.NRSV: Peter said to them, “Repent, and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ so that your sins may be forgiven; and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: And Peter [said] unto them—Repent ye, and let each one of you be immersed, in the name of Jesus Christ, into the remission of your sins,—and ye shall receive the free-gift of the Holy Spirit;
text.KJV: Then Peter said unto them, Repent, and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ for the remission of sins, and ye shall receive the gift of the Holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: Peter [said] to them, “Repent and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins; and you will receive the gift of the holy Spirit.
text.DRB: But Peter said to them, do penance, & be every one of you baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for remission of your sins; and you shall receive the gift of the Holy Ghost.

text.NRSV: For the promise is for you, for your children, and for all who are far away, everyone whom the Lord our God calls to him.”
text.Rotherham: For, unto you, is the promise, and unto your children,—and unto all them who are afar off: as many soever as the Lord our God shall call unto him.
text.KJV: For the promise is unto you, and to your children, and to all that are afar off, even as many as the Lord our God shall call.
text.NABRE: For the promise is made to you and to your children and to all those far off, whomever the Lord our God will call.”
text.DRB: For to you is the promise, and to your children, and to all that are far off, whomsoever the Lord our God shall call.

text.NRSV: And he testified with many other arguments and exhorted them, saying, “Save yourselves from this corrupt generation.”
text.Rotherham: And, with many different words, bare he frail witness, and went on exhorting them saying—Be saved from this perverse generation!
text.KJV: And with many other words did he testify and exhort, saying, Save yourselves from this untoward generation.
text.NABRE: He testified with many other arguments, and was exhorting them, “Save yourselves from this corrupt generation.”
text.DRB: With very many other words also did he testify and exhorted them, saying: Save yourselves from this perverse Generation.

text.NRSV: So those who welcomed his message were baptized, and that day about three thousand persons were added.
text.Rotherham: They, therefore, who welcomed his word, were immersed; and there were added, on that day, about three thousand souls.
text.KJV: Then they that gladly received his word were baptized: and the same day there were added unto them about three thousand souls.
text.NABRE: Those who accepted his message were baptized, and about three thousand persons were added that day. Communal Life.
text.DRB: They therefore that received his word, were baptized: & there were added in that day about three thousand souls.

text.NRSV: They devoted themselves to the apostles’ teaching and fellowship, to the breaking of bread and the prayers.
text.Rotherham: And they went on to give constant attention—unto the teaching of the apostles, and unto the fellowship, unto the breaking of bread, and unto the prayers.
text.KJV: And they continued stedfastly in the apostles’ doctrine and fellowship, and in breaking of bread, and in prayers.
text.NABRE: They devoted themselves to the teaching of the apostles and to the communal life, to the breaking of the bread and to the prayers.
text.DRB: And they were persevering in the doctrine of the Apostles, and in the communication of the breaking of bread, and prayers.

text.NRSV: Awe came upon everyone, because many wonders and signs were being done by the apostles.
text.Rotherham: And there came on every soul, fear, and, many wonders and signs, through means of the apostles, were coming to pass.
text.KJV: And fear came upon every soul: and many wonders and signs were done by the apostles.
text.NABRE: Awe came upon everyone, and many wonders and signs were done through the apostles.
text.DRB: And fear came upon every soul; many wonders also and signs were done by the Apostles in Jerusalem, and there was great fear in all.

text.NRSV: All who believed were together and had all things in common;
text.Rotherham: And, all who believed, with one accord, began to hold all things common;
text.KJV: And all that believed were together, and had all things common;
text.NABRE: All who believed were together and had all things in common;
text.DRB: All they also that believed, were together, & had all things common.

text.NRSV: they would sell their possessions and goods and distribute the proceeds to all, as any had need.
text.Rotherham: and, their possessions and goods, were they selling and distributing them unto all, in so far as anyone had need.
text.KJV: And sold their possessions and goods, and parted them to all men, as every man had need.
text.NABRE: they would sell their property and possessions and divide them among all according to each one’s need.
text.DRB: Their possessions and substance they sold, and divided them to all, according as every one had need.

text.NRSV: Day by day, as they spent much time together in the temple, they broke bread at home and ate their food with glad and generous hearts,
text.Rotherham: And, daily giving attendance with one intent in the temple and, at home, breaking bread, they were partaking of food with exultation and singleness of heart;
text.KJV: And they, continuing daily with one accord in the temple, and breaking bread from house to house, did eat their meat with gladness and singleness of heart,
text.NABRE: Every day they devoted themselves to meeting together in the temple area and to breaking bread in their homes. They ate their meals with exultation and sincerity of heart,
text.DRB: Daily also continuing with one accord in the temple, and breaking bread from house to house, they took their meat with joy and simplicity of heart:

text.NRSV: praising God and having the goodwill of all the people. And day by day the Lord added to their number those who were being saved.
text.Rotherham: Praising God, and having favour with all the people. And, the Lord, was adding them who were being saved, daily, together.
text.KJV: Praising God, and having favour with all the people. And the Lord added to the church daily such as should be saved.
text.NABRE: praising God and enjoying favor with all the people. And every day the Lord added to their number those who were being saved.
text.DRB: praising God, and having grace with all the people. And our Lord increased them that should be saved, daily together.

text.NRSV: One day Peter and John were going up to the temple at the hour of prayer, at three o’clock in the afternoon.
text.Rotherham: Now, Peter and John, were going up into the temple, for the hour of prayer—the ninth;
text.KJV: Now Peter and John went up together into the temple at the hour of prayer, being the ninth hour.
text.NABRE: Now Peter and John were going up to the temple area for the three o’clock hour of prayer.
text.DRB: And Peter and John went up into the temple, at the ninth hour of prayer.

text.NRSV: And a man lame from birth was being carried in. People would lay him daily at the gate of the temple called the Beautiful Gate so that he could ask for alms from those entering the temple.
text.Rotherham: And, a certain man, who had been, lame from his mother’s womb, was being carried,—whom they used to lay daily at the door of the temple—the [door] called Beautiful, that he might ask alms of them who were entering into the temple:
text.KJV: And a certain man lame from his mother’s womb was carried, whom they laid daily at the gate of the temple which is called Beautiful, to ask alms of them that entered into the temple;
text.NABRE: And a man crippled from birth was carried and placed at the gate of the temple called “the Beautiful Gate” every day to beg for alms from the people who entered the temple.
text.DRB: And a certain man that was lame from his mother's womb, was carried; whom they laid every day at the gate of the temple, that is called Specious, that he might ask alms of them that went into the temple.

text.NRSV: When he saw Peter and John about to go into the temple, he asked them for alms.
text.Rotherham: Who, seeing Peter and John about to enter into the temple, was requesting to receive, an alms.
text.KJV: Who seeing Peter and John about to go into the temple asked an alms.
text.NABRE: When he saw Peter and John about to go into the temple, he asked for alms.
text.DRB: He, when he had seen Peter and John about to enter into the temple, asked to receive an alms.

text.NRSV: Peter looked intently at him, as did John, and said, “Look at us.”
text.Rotherham: But Peter looking steadfastly at him—with John—said: Look on us!
text.KJV: And Peter, fastening his eyes upon him with John, said, Look on us.
text.NABRE: But Peter looked intently at him, as did John, and said, “Look at us.”
text.DRB: But Peter with John looking upon him, said: Look upon us.

text.NRSV: And he fixed his attention on them, expecting to receive something from them.
text.Rotherham: And, he, gave heed unto them, expecting to receive, something from them.
text.KJV: And he gave heed unto them, expecting to receive something of them.
text.NABRE: He paid attention to them, expecting to receive something from them.
text.DRB: But he looked earnestly upon them, hoping that he should receive something of them.

text.NRSV: But Peter said, “I have no silver or gold, but what I have I give you; in the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, stand up and walk.”
text.Rotherham: But Peter said—Silver and gold, have none, but, what I have, the same, unto thee do I give:—In the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene, Walk!
text.KJV: Then Peter said, Silver and gold have I none; but such as I have give I thee: In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth rise up and walk.
text.NABRE: Peter said, “I have neither silver nor gold, but what I do have I give you: in the name of Jesus Christ the Nazorean, [rise and] walk.”
text.DRB: But Peter said: Silver and gold I have not, but that which I have, the same I give to thee: In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth arise, and walk.

text.NRSV: And he took him by the right hand and raised him up; and immediately his feet and ankles were made strong.
text.Rotherham: And, laying hold of him by the right hand, he raised him up; and, instantly, were his feet and ankles strengthened;—
text.KJV: And he took him by the right hand, and lifted him up: and immediately his feet and ankle bones received strength.
text.NABRE: Then Peter took him by the right hand and raised him up, and immediately his feet and ankles grew strong.
text.DRB: And taking his right hand, he lifted him up, and forthwith his feet & soles were made strong.

text.NRSV: Jumping up, he stood and began to walk, and he entered the temple with them, walking and leaping and praising God.
text.Rotherham: And, leaping forward, he stood and walked, and entered with them into the temple, walking and leaping and praising God.
text.KJV: And he leaping up stood, and walked, and entered with them into the temple, walking, and leaping, and praising God.
text.NABRE: He leaped up, stood, and walked around, and went into the temple with them, walking and jumping and praising God.
text.DRB: And springing he stood, & walked; & went in with them into the temple walking, and leaping, and praising God.

text.NRSV: All the people saw him walking and praising God,
text.Rotherham: And all the people saw him, walking and praising God;
text.KJV: And all the people saw him walking and praising God:
text.NABRE: When all the people saw him walking and praising God,
text.DRB: And all the people saw him walking and praising God.

text.NRSV: and they recognized him as the one who used to sit and ask for alms at the Beautiful Gate of the temple; and they were filled with wonder and amazement at what had happened to him.
text.Rotherham: And they began to recognise him, that, the same, was he who, for the alms, used to sit at the Beautiful Gate of the temple;—and they were filled with amazement and transport at what had happened unto him.
text.KJV: And they knew that it was he which sat for alms at the Beautiful gate of the temple: and they were filled with wonder and amazement at that which had happened unto him.
text.NABRE: they recognized him as the one who used to sit begging at the Beautiful Gate of the temple, and they were filled with amazement and astonishment at what had happened to him.
text.DRB: And they knew him, that it was he which sat for alms at the Specious gate of the temple: and they were exceedingly astonished and aghast at that that had chanced to him.

text.NRSV: While he clung to Peter and John, all the people ran together to them in the portico called Solomon’s Portico, utterly astonished.
text.Rotherham: And, as he held fast Peter and John, all the people ran together unto them, in the portico which is called Solomon’s,—greatly amazed.
text.KJV: And as the lame man which was healed held Peter and John, all the people ran together unto them in the porch that is called Solomon’s, greatly wondering.
text.NABRE: Peter’s Speech. As he clung to Peter and John, all the people hurried in amazement toward them in the portico called “Solomon’s Portico.”
text.DRB: And as he held Peter and John, all the people ran to them unto the porch which is called Salomon's, wondering.

text.NRSV: When Peter saw it, he addressed the people, “You Israelites, why do you wonder at this, or why do you stare at us, as though by our own power or piety we had made him walk?
text.Rotherham: And Peter, seeing it, made answer unto the people:—Ye men of Israel! Why marvel ye at this man? Or, upon us, why are ye intently looking, as though, by our own power or godliness, we had made him walk?
text.KJV: And when Peter saw it, he answered unto the people, Ye men of Israel, why marvel ye at this? or why look ye so earnestly on us, as though by our own power or holiness we had made this man to walk?
text.NABRE: When Peter saw this, he addressed the people, “You Israelites, why are you amazed at this, and why do you look so intently at us as if we had made him walk by our own power or piety?
text.DRB: But Peter seeing them, made answer to the people: Ye men of Israel, why marvel you at this, or why look you upon us, as though by our power or holiness we have made this man to walk?

text.NRSV: The God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, the God of our ancestors has glorified his servant Jesus, whom you handed over and rejected in the presence of Pilate, though he had decided to release him.
text.Rotherham: The God of Abraham and Isaac and Jacob,—the God of our fathers, hath glorified his servant Jesus:—whom, ye, indeed, delivered up and denied to Pilate’s, face when he had adjudged to release him;
text.KJV: The God of Abraham, and of Isaac, and of Jacob, the God of our fathers, hath glorified his Son Jesus; whom ye delivered up, and denied him in the presence of Pilate, when he was determined to let him go.
text.NABRE: The God of Abraham, [the God] of Isaac, and [the God] of Jacob, the God of our ancestors, has glorified his servant Jesus whom you handed over and denied in Pilate’s presence, when he had decided to release him.
text.DRB: The God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, the God of our fathers hath glorified his Son Jesus, whom you indeed delivered & denied before the face of Pilate, he judging him to be released.

text.NRSV: But you rejected the Holy and Righteous One and asked to have a murderer given to you,
text.Rotherham: But, ye, denied, the Holy and Righteous One, and claimed a man that was a murderer to be granted you;
text.KJV: But ye denied the Holy One and the Just, and desired a murderer to be granted unto you;
text.NABRE: You denied the Holy and Righteous One and asked that a murderer be released to you.
text.DRB: But you denied the Holy and the Just One, and asked a man-killer to be given unto you.

text.NRSV: and you killed the Author of life, whom God raised from the dead. To this we are witnesses.
text.Rotherham: But, the Princely Leader of Life, ye slew:—whom, God, raised from among the dead, whereof, we, are witnesses.
text.KJV: And killed the Prince of life, whom God hath raised from the dead; whereof we are witnesses.
text.NABRE: The author of life you put to death, but God raised him from the dead; of this we are witnesses.
text.DRB: But the Author of life you killed, whom God hath raised from the dead, of which we are witnesses.

text.NRSV: And by faith in his name, his name itself has made this man strong, whom you see and know; and the faith that is through Jesus has given him this perfect health in the presence of all of you.
text.Rotherham: And, by the faith of his name, unto this man whom ye see and know, hath his name given strength,—yea, the faith which is through him, hath given unto him this perfect soundness, over against you all!
text.KJV: And his name through faith in his name hath made this man strong, whom ye see and know: yea, the faith which is by him hath given him this perfect soundness in the presence of you all.
text.NABRE: And by faith in his name, this man, whom you see and know, his name has made strong, and the faith that comes through it has given him this perfect health, in the presence of all of you.
text.DRB: And in the faith of his name, this man whom you see and know, his name hath strengthened; & the faith which is by him, hath given this perfect health in the sight of all you.

text.NRSV: “And now, friends, I know that you acted in ignorance, as did also your rulers.
text.Rotherham: And now brethren, I know that by way of ignorance ye acted,—just as even your rulers.
text.KJV: And now, brethren, I wot that through ignorance ye did it, as did also your rulers.
text.NABRE: Now I know, brothers, that you acted out of ignorance, just as your leaders did;
text.DRB: And now (brethren) I know that you did it through ignorance, as also your princes.

text.NRSV: In this way God fulfilled what he had foretold through all the prophets, that his Messiah would suffer.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, God—what things he had before declared, through the mouth of all the prophets, for his Christ to suffer,—did thus fulfill!
text.KJV: But those things, which God before had shewed by the mouth of all his prophets, that Christ should suffer, he hath so fulfilled.
text.NABRE: but God has thus brought to fulfillment what he had announced beforehand through the mouth of all the prophets, that his Messiah would suffer.
text.DRB: But God who foreshewed by the mouth of all the Prophets that his Christ should suffer, hath so fulfilled it.

text.NRSV: Repent therefore, and turn to God so that your sins may be wiped out,
text.Rotherham: Repent ye, therefore, and turn,—unto the blotting out of your sins; to the end that in that case, there may come seasons of refreshing from the face of the Lord,
text.KJV: Repent ye therefore, and be converted, that your sins may be blotted out, when the times of refreshing shall come from the presence of the Lord;
text.NABRE: Repent, therefore, and be converted, that your sins may be wiped away,
text.DRB: Be Penitent therefore & convert, that your sins may be put out.

text.NRSV: so that times of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord, and that he may send the Messiah appointed for you, that is, Jesus,
text.Rotherham: And he may send forth him who had been fore-appointed for you—Christ Jesus:
text.KJV: And he shall send Jesus Christ, which before was preached unto you:
text.NABRE: and that the Lord may grant you times of refreshment and send you the Messiah already appointed for you, Jesus,
text.DRB: That, when the times shall come of refreshing by the sight of our Lord, and he shall send him that hath been preached unto you Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: who must remain in heaven until the time of universal restoration that God announced long ago through his holy prophets.
text.Rotherham: Unto whom, indeed, heaven must needs give welcome, until the times of the due establishment of all things, of which God hath spoken through the mouth of his holy age-past prophets.
text.KJV: Whom the heaven must receive until the times of restitution of all things, which God hath spoken by the mouth of all his holy prophets since the world began.
text.NABRE: whom heaven must receive until the times of universal restoration of which God spoke through the mouth of his holy prophets from of old.
text.DRB: Whom Heaven truly must receive until the times of the restitution of all things, which God spake by the mouth of his holy Prophets from the beginning of the world.

text.NRSV: Moses said, ‘The Lord your God will raise up for you from your own people a prophet like me. You must listen to whatever he tells you.
text.Rotherham: Moses, indeed, said—A prophet, unto you, shall the Lord God raise up among your brethren, like unto me: unto him, shall ye hearken respecting all things whatsoever he shall speak unto you.
text.KJV: For Moses truly said unto the fathers, A prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear in all things whatsoever he shall say unto you.
text.NABRE: For Moses said: ‘A prophet like me will the Lord, your God, raise up for you from among your own kinsmen; to him you shall listen in all that he may say to you.
text.DRB: Moyses indeed said: That a prophet shall the Lord your God raise up to you of your brethren, as myself: him you shall hear according to all things whatsoever he shall speak to you.

text.NRSV: And it will be that everyone who does not listen to that prophet will be utterly rooted out of the people.’
text.Rotherham: And it shall be—every soul, whatsoever, which shall not hearken unto that prophet, shall be utterly destroyed from among the people.
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass, that every soul, which will not hear that prophet, shall be destroyed from among the people.
text.NABRE: Everyone who does not listen to that prophet will be cut off from the people.’
text.DRB: And it shall be, every soul that shall not hear that Prophet, shall be destroyed out of the people.

text.NRSV: And all the prophets, as many as have spoken, from Samuel and those after him, also predicted these days.
text.Rotherham: But, indeed, all the prophets,—from Samuel and those following after, as many as have spoken, have even announced these days.
text.KJV: Yea, and all the prophets from Samuel and those that follow after, as many as have spoken, have likewise foretold of these days.
text.NABRE: Moreover, all the prophets who spoke, from Samuel and those afterwards, also announced these days.
text.DRB: And all the Prophets from Samuel and afterward that have spoken, told of these days.

text.NRSV: You are the descendants of the prophets and of the covenant that God gave to your ancestors, saying to Abraham, ‘And in your descendants all the families of the earth shall be blessed.’
text.Rotherham: Ye, are the sons of the prophets, and of the covenant which God covenanted unto your fathers, saying unto Abraham—And, in thy seed, shall be blessed, all the families of the ground.
text.KJV: Ye are the children of the prophets, and of the covenant which God made with our fathers, saying unto Abraham, And in thy seed shall all the kindreds of the earth be blessed.
text.NABRE: You are the children of the prophets and of the covenant that God made with your ancestors when he said to Abraham, ‘In your offspring all the families of the earth shall be blessed.’
text.DRB: You are the children of the Prophets and of the Testament which God made to our fathers, saying to Abraham: And in thy seed shall all the families of the earth be blessed.

text.NRSV: When God raised up his servant, he sent him first to you, to bless you by turning each of you from your wicked ways.”
text.Rotherham: Unto you first, God—having raised up his Servant—hath sent him forth, ready to bless you, when ye are turning away, each one, from your wickednesses.
text.KJV: Unto you first God, having raised up his Son Jesus, sent him to bless you, in turning away every one of you from his iniquities.
text.NABRE: For you first, God raised up his servant and sent him to bless you by turning each of you from your evil ways.”
text.DRB: To you first God raising up his Son, hath sent him blessing you: that every one should convert himself from his naughtiness.

text.NRSV: While Peter and John were speaking to the people, the priests, the captain of the temple, and the Sadducees came to them,
text.Rotherham: But, as they were speaking unto the people, the High-priests and the Captain of the temple and the Sadducees came upon them,
text.KJV: And as they spake unto the people, the priests, and the captain of the temple, and the Sadducees, came upon them,
text.NABRE: While they were still speaking to the people, the priests, the captain of the temple guard, and the Sadducees confronted them,
text.DRB: And when they were speaking to the people, the Priests and Magistrates of the temple & the Sadducees came upon them,

text.NRSV: much annoyed because they were teaching the people and proclaiming that in Jesus there is the resurrection of the dead.
text.Rotherham: Being tired out because of their teaching the people, and announcing, in Jesus, the resurrection from among the dead;
text.KJV: Being grieved that they taught the people, and preached through Jesus the resurrection from the dead.
text.NABRE: disturbed that they were teaching the people and proclaiming in Jesus the resurrection of the dead.
text.DRB: being grieved that they taught the people, and shewed in Jesus the resurrection from the dead:

text.NRSV: So they arrested them and put them in custody until the next day, for it was already evening.
text.Rotherham: And they thrust on them their hands, and put them in custody for the morrow, for it was evening, already.
text.KJV: And they laid hands on them, and put them in hold unto the next day: for it was now eventide.
text.NABRE: They laid hands on them and put them in custody until the next day, since it was already evening.
text.DRB: and they laid hands upon them, and put them into ward, until the morrow, for it was now evening.

text.NRSV: But many of those who heard the word believed; and they numbered about five thousand.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, many of them that heard the word, believed; and the number of the men became about five thousand.
text.KJV: Howbeit many of them which heard the word believed; and the number of the men was about five thousand.
text.NABRE: But many of those who heard the word came to believe and [the] number of men grew to [about] five thousand.
text.DRB: And many of them that had heard the word, believed: and the number of the men was made five thousand.

text.NRSV: The next day their rulers, elders, and scribes assembled in Jerusalem,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, upon the morrow, that there were gathered together of them, the rulers and the elders and the scribes, in Jerusalem;
text.KJV: And it came to pass on the morrow, that their rulers, and elders, and scribes,
text.NABRE: Before the Sanhedrin. On the next day, their leaders, elders, and scribes were assembled in Jerusalem,
text.DRB: And it came to pass on the morrow, that their princes, and Ancients, and Scribes were gathered into Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, and Alexander, and all who were of the high-priestly family.
text.Rotherham: Also Annas, the high-priest, and Caiaphas, and John, and Alexander, and as many as were of high-priestly descent;—
text.KJV: And Annas the high priest, and Caiaphas, and John, and Alexander, and as many as were of the kindred of the high priest, were gathered together at Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and all who were of the high-priestly class.
text.DRB: And Annas the high Priest, and Caiphas and John, and Alexander, and as many as were of the priest's stock.

text.NRSV: When they had made the prisoners stand in their midst, they inquired, “By what power or by what name did you do this?”
text.Rotherham: And, setting them in the midst, they began to enquire—In what manner of power, or in what manner of name, did, ye, do this?
text.KJV: And when they had set them in the midst, they asked, By what power, or by what name, have ye done this?
text.NABRE: They brought them into their presence and questioned them, “By what power or by what name have you done this?”
text.DRB: And setting them in the midst, they asked: In what power or in what name have you done this?

text.NRSV: Then Peter, filled with the Holy Spirit, said to them, “Rulers of the people and elders,
text.Rotherham: Then Peter, filled with Holy Spirit, said unto them—Ye rulers of the people, and elders!
text.KJV: Then Peter, filled with the Holy Ghost, said unto them, Ye rulers of the people, and elders of Israel,
text.NABRE: Then Peter, filled with the holy Spirit, answered them, “Leaders of the people and elders:
text.DRB: Then Peter replenished with the Holy Ghost, said to them: Ye princes of the people and Ancients:

text.NRSV: if we are questioned today because of a good deed done to someone who was sick and are asked how this man has been healed,
text.Rotherham: If, we, this day, are to be examined for doing good to a sick man,—in whom, this, man hath been made well,
text.KJV: If we this day be examined of the good deed done to the impotent man, by what means he is made whole;
text.NABRE: If we are being examined today about a good deed done to a cripple, namely, by what means he was saved,
text.DRB: If we this day be examined for a good deed upon an impotent man, in what he hath been made whole,

text.NRSV: let it be known to all of you, and to all the people of Israel, that this man is standing before you in good health by the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, whom you crucified, whom God raised from the dead.
text.Rotherham: Be it known unto you all, and unto all the people of Israel: that, in the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene,—whom, ye, crucified, whom, God, raised from among the dead, in him, doth, this man stand by, in your presence, whole.
text.KJV: Be it known unto you all, and to all the people of Israel, that by the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, whom ye crucified, whom God raised from the dead, even by him doth this man stand here before you whole.
text.NABRE: then all of you and all the people of Israel should know that it was in the name of Jesus Christ the Nazorean whom you crucified, whom God raised from the dead; in his name this man stands before you healed.
text.DRB: be it known to all you and to all the people of Israel, that in the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, whom you did crucify, whom God hath raised from the dead, in this same this man standeth before you whole.

text.NRSV: This Jesus is ‘the stone that was rejected by you, the builders; it has become the cornerstone.’
text.Rotherham: This, is the stone, set at nought by you the builders, which became the head of the corner;
text.KJV: This is the stone which was set at nought of you builders, which is become the head of the corner.
text.NABRE: He is ‘the stone rejected by you, the builders, which has become the cornerstone.’
text.DRB: This is the stone that was rejected of you the builders: which is made into the head of the corner:

text.NRSV: There is salvation in no one else, for there is no other name under heaven given among mortals by which we must be saved.”
text.Rotherham: And there is in no one else, salvation, for, neither is there any other name, under heaven, which hath been set forth among men, in which we must needs be saved.
text.KJV: Neither is there salvation in any other: for there is none other name under heaven given among men, whereby we must be saved.
text.NABRE: There is no salvation through anyone else, nor is there any other name under heaven given to the human race by which we are to be saved.”
text.DRB: and there is not salvation in any other. For neither is there any other name under Heaven given to men, wherein we must be saved.

text.NRSV: Now when they saw the boldness of Peter and John and realized that they were uneducated and ordinary men, they were amazed and recognized them as companions of Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, looking at Peter’s boldness of speech, and John’s, and having discovered that they were unlettered and obscure men, they began to marvel, recognising them also, that they had been with Jesus;
text.KJV: Now when they saw the boldness of Peter and John, and perceived that they were unlearned and ignorant men, they marvelled; and they took knowledge of them, that they had been with Jesus.
text.NABRE: Observing the boldness of Peter and John and perceiving them to be uneducated, ordinary men, they were amazed, and they recognized them as the companions of Jesus.
text.DRB: And seeing Peter's constancy and John's, understanding that they were men unlettered, and of the vulgar sort, they marveled; and they knew them that they had been with Jesus:

text.NRSV: When they saw the man who had been cured standing beside them, they had nothing to say in opposition.
text.Rotherham: Seeing, the man also standing, with them, even the [man] who had been cured, they had nothing wherewith to contradict.
text.KJV: And beholding the man which was healed standing with them, they could say nothing against it.
text.NABRE: Then when they saw the man who had been cured standing there with them, they could say nothing in reply.
text.DRB: seeing the man also that had been cured, standing with them, they could say nothing to the contrary,

text.NRSV: So they ordered them to leave the council while they discussed the matter with one another.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, ordering them to go forth outside the high-council, they began to confer one with another,
text.KJV: But when they had commanded them to go aside out of the council, they conferred among themselves,
text.NABRE: So they ordered them to leave the Sanhedrin, and conferred with one another, saying,
text.DRB: But they commanded them to go aside forth out of the council: and they conferred together,

text.NRSV: They said, “What will we do with them? For it is obvious to all who live in Jerusalem that a notable sign has been done through them; we cannot deny it.
text.Rotherham: Saying—What are we to do with these men? For, indeed, that a notable sign hath come to pass through their means, unto all who are dwelling in Jerusalem, is manifest, and we cannot deny [it]!
text.KJV: Saying, What shall we do to these men? for that indeed a notable miracle hath been done by them is manifest to all them that dwell in Jerusalem; and we cannot deny it.
text.NABRE: “What are we to do with these men? Everyone living in Jerusalem knows that a remarkable sign was done through them, and we cannot deny it.
text.DRB: saying: What shall we do to these men? for a notorious sign indeed hath been done by them, to all the inhabitants of Jerusalem: it is manifest, and we cannot deny it.

text.NRSV: But to keep it from spreading further among the people, let us warn them to speak no more to anyone in this name.”
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, lest it further spread abroad among the people, let us charge them with threats, to be no more speaking upon this name, to any soul of man.
text.KJV: But that it spread no further among the people, let us straitly threaten them, that they speak henceforth to no man in this name.
text.NABRE: But so that it may not be spread any further among the people, let us give them a stern warning never again to speak to anyone in this name.”
text.DRB: But that it be no further spread abroad among the people, let us threaten them, that they speak no more in this name to any man.

text.NRSV: So they called them and ordered them not to speak or teach at all in the name of Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, calling them, they gave them the sweeping charge,—not to be sounding aloud, nor even to be teaching, upon the name of Jesus.
text.KJV: And they called them, and commanded them not to speak at all nor teach in the name of Jesus.
text.NABRE: So they called them back and ordered them not to speak or teach at all in the name of Jesus.
text.DRB: And calling them, they charged them that they should not speak at all, nor teach in the name of Jesus.

text.NRSV: But Peter and John answered them, “Whether it is right in God’s sight to listen to you rather than to God, you must judge;
text.Rotherham: But, Peter and John, answering, said unto them—Whether it be right in presence of God, unto you, to be hearkening, rather than unto God, judge!
