text.KJV: But Peter and John answered and said unto them, Whether it be right in the sight of God to hearken unto you more than unto God, judge ye.
text.NABRE: Peter and John, however, said to them in reply, “Whether it is right in the sight of God for us to obey you rather than God, you be the judges.
text.DRB: But Peter and John answering, said to them: If it be just in the sight of God, to hear you rather than God, judge ye.

text.NRSV: for we cannot keep from speaking about what we have seen and heard.”
text.Rotherham: For, we, cannot but be speaking the things which we have seen and heard.
text.KJV: For we cannot but speak the things which we have seen and heard.
text.NABRE: It is impossible for us not to speak about what we have seen and heard.”
text.DRB: For we cannot but speak the things which we have seen and heard.

text.NRSV: After threatening them again, they let them go, finding no way to punish them because of the people, for all of them praised God for what had happened.
text.Rotherham: They, however, further charging them with threats, let them go, finding nothing as to how they might chastise them, on account of the people; because, all, were glorifying God for that which had come to pass;—
text.KJV: So when they had further threatened them, they let them go, finding nothing how they might punish them, because of the people: for all men glorified God for that which was done.
text.NABRE: After threatening them further, they released them, finding no way to punish them, on account of the people who were all praising God for what had happened.
text.DRB: But they threatening, dismissed them: not finding how they might punish them, for the people, because all glorified that which had been done, in that which was chanced.

text.NRSV: For the man on whom this sign of healing had been performed was more than forty years old.
text.Rotherham: For, more than forty years old, was the man, upon whom had taken place, this healing sign.
text.KJV: For the man was above forty years old, on whom this miracle of healing was shewed.
text.NABRE: For the man on whom this sign of healing had been done was over forty years old.
text.DRB: For the man was more than forty years old in whom that sign of health had been wrought.

text.NRSV: After they were released, they went to their friends and reported what the chief priests and the elders had said to them.
text.Rotherham: But, when they had been let go, they came unto their own [friends], and told as many things as, unto them, the High-priests and Elders, had said.
text.KJV: And being let go, they went to their own company, and reported all that the chief priests and elders had said unto them.
text.NABRE: Prayer of the Community. After their release they went back to their own people and reported what the chief priests and elders had told them.
text.DRB: And being dismissed they came to theirs, and shewed all that the chief priests and Ancients had said to them.

text.NRSV: When they heard it, they raised their voices together to God and said, “Sovereign Lord, who made the heaven and the earth, the sea, and everything in them,
text.Rotherham: And, they, having heard, with one accord, uplifted voice unto God, and said—O Sovereign! Thou, art he that made the heaven, and the earth, and the sea, and all things that are herein:
text.KJV: And when they heard that, they lifted up their voice to God with one accord, and said, Lord, thou art God, which hast made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and all that in them is:
text.NABRE: And when they heard it, they raised their voices to God with one accord and said, “Sovereign Lord, maker of heaven and earth and the sea and all that is in them,
text.DRB: Who having heard it, with one accord lifted up their voice to God, and said: Lord, thou that didst make Heaven & earth, the sea, and all things that are in them,

text.NRSV: it is you who said by the Holy Spirit through our ancestor David, your servant: ‘Why did the Gentiles rage, and the peoples imagine vain things?
text.Rotherham: Who, by our father, through means of the Holy Spirit, even by the mouth of David thy servant, said—Unto what end did nations rage, and, peoples, busy themselves with empty things?
text.KJV: Who by the mouth of thy servant David hast said, Why did the heathen rage, and the people imagine vain things?
text.NABRE: you said by the holy Spirit through the mouth of our father David, your servant: ‘Why did the Gentiles rage and the peoples entertain folly?
text.DRB: who in the Holy Ghost by the mouth of our Father David thy servant hast said: Why did the Gentiles rage, and the people meditate vain things:

text.NRSV: The kings of the earth took their stand, and the rulers have gathered together against the Lord and against his Messiah.’
text.Rotherham: The kings of the earth stationed themselves, and, the rulers, were gathered together, with one intent, against the Lord, and against his Christ.
text.KJV: The kings of the earth stood up, and the rulers were gathered together against the Lord, and against his Christ.
text.NABRE: The kings of the earth took their stand and the princes gathered together against the Lord and against his anointed.’
text.DRB: the kings of the earth stand up, and the Princes assemble together against our Lord, and against his Christ?

text.NRSV: For in this city, in fact, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles and the peoples of Israel, gathered together against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed,
text.Rotherham: For they were gathered together, of a truth, in this city, against thy holy servant Jesus, whom thou hadst anointed,—both Herod, and Pontius Pilate, with them of the nations, and peoples of Israel;—
text.KJV: For of a truth against thy holy child Jesus, whom thou hast anointed, both Herod, and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles, and the people of Israel, were gathered together,
text.NABRE: Indeed they gathered in this city against your holy servant Jesus whom you anointed, Herod and Pontius Pilate, together with the Gentiles and the peoples of Israel,
text.DRB: For there assembled indeed in this city against the holy child Jesus whom thou hast anointed, Herod, and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles and the people of Israel;

text.NRSV: to do whatever your hand and your plan had predestined to take place.
text.Rotherham: To do whatsoever, thy hand and thy counsel, marked out beforehand to come to pass.
text.KJV: For to do whatsoever thy hand and thy counsel determined before to be done.
text.NABRE: to do what your hand and [your] will had long ago planned to take place.
text.DRB: to do what thy hand and thy counsel decreed to be done.

text.NRSV: And now, Lord, look at their threats, and grant to your servants to speak your word with all boldness,
text.Rotherham: As to the present things, then, O Lord,—look upon their threats, and grant unto thy servants, with all freedom of utterance, to be speaking thy word,
text.KJV: And now, Lord, behold their threatenings: and grant unto thy servants, that with all boldness they may speak thy word,
text.NABRE: And now, Lord, take note of their threats, and enable your servants to speak your word with all boldness,
text.DRB: And now Lord look upon their threatenings, and give unto thy servants with all confidence to speak thy word,

text.NRSV: while you stretch out your hand to heal, and signs and wonders are performed through the name of your holy servant Jesus.”
text.Rotherham: By stretching forth thy hand for healing, and by the coming to pass of both signs and wonders, through the name of thy holy servant Jesus.
text.KJV: By stretching forth thine hand to heal; and that signs and wonders may be done by the name of thy holy child Jesus.
text.NABRE: as you stretch forth [your] hand to heal, and signs and wonders are done through the name of your holy servant Jesus.”
text.DRB: in that, that thou extend thy hand to cures & signs and wonders to be done by the name of thy holy son Jesus.

text.NRSV: When they had prayed, the place in which they were gathered together was shaken; and they were all filled with the Holy Spirit and spoke the word of God with boldness.
text.Rotherham: And, when they had made supplication, the place was shaken in which they were gathered together, and they were filled, one and all, with the Holy Spirit, and began speaking the word of God with freedom of utterance.
text.KJV: And when they had prayed, the place was shaken where they were assembled together; and they were all filled with the Holy Ghost, and they spake the word of God with boldness.
text.NABRE: As they prayed, the place where they were gathered shook, and they were all filled with the holy Spirit and continued to speak the word of God with boldness. Life in the Christian Community.
text.DRB: And when they had prayed, the place was moved wherein they were gathered: and they were all replenished with the Holy Ghost, and they spake the word of God with confidence.

text.NRSV: Now the whole group of those who believed were of one heart and soul, and no one claimed private ownership of any possessions, but everything they owned was held in common.
text.Rotherham: And, the throng of them that believed, had one heart and soul, and not so much as one was saying that, aught of his goods, was, his own, but they had all things common.
text.KJV: And the multitude of them that believed were of one heart and of one soul: neither said any of them that ought of the things which he possessed was his own; but they had all things common.
text.NABRE: The community of believers was of one heart and mind, and no one claimed that any of his possessions was his own, but they had everything in common.
text.DRB: And the multitude of believers had one heart and one soul: neither did any one say that ought was his own of those things which he possessed, but all things were common unto them.

text.NRSV: With great power the apostles gave their testimony to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and great grace was upon them all.
text.Rotherham: And, with great power, were the apostles giving forth their witness of the resurrection of the Lord Jesus; great favour also was upon them all.
text.KJV: And with great power gave the apostles witness of the resurrection of the Lord Jesus: and great grace was upon them all.
text.NABRE: With great power the apostles bore witness to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and great favor was accorded them all.
text.DRB: And with great power did the Apostles give testimony of the resurrection of Jesus Christ our Lord: and great grace was in all them.

text.NRSV: There was not a needy person among them, for as many as owned lands or houses sold them and brought the proceeds of what was sold.
text.Rotherham: For there was not so much as anyone, lacking, among them; for, as many as were possessors of lands or houses, selling them, were bringing the prices of the things that were being sold,
text.KJV: Neither was there any among them that lacked: for as many as were possessors of lands or houses sold them, and brought the prices of the things that were sold,
text.NABRE: There was no needy person among them, for those who owned property or houses would sell them, bring the proceeds of the sale,
text.DRB: For neither was there any one needy among them. For as many as were owners of lands or houses, sold and brought the prices of those things which they sold,

text.NRSV: They laid it at the apostles’ feet, and it was distributed to each as any had need.
text.Rotherham: And laying them at the feet of the Apostles, while on the other hand they were distributing unto each one, in so far as any one had, need.
text.KJV: And laid them down at the apostles’ feet: and distribution was made unto every man according as he had need.
text.NABRE: and put them at the feet of the apostles, and they were distributed to each according to need.
text.DRB: and laid it before the feet of the Apostles. And to every one was divided according as every one had need.

text.NRSV: There was a Levite, a native of Cyprus, Joseph, to whom the apostles gave the name Barnabas (which means “son of encouragement”).
text.Rotherham: And, Joseph, who had been surnamed Barnabas by the Apostles, which is to be translated Son of Exhortation,—a Levite, a Cyprian by nation,
text.KJV: And Joses, who by the apostles was surnamed Barnabas, (which is, being interpreted, The son of consolation,) a Levite, and of the country of Cyprus,
text.NABRE: Thus Joseph, also named by the apostles Barnabas (which is translated “son of encouragement”), a Levite, a Cypriot by birth,
text.DRB: And Joseph who was surnamed of the Apostles Barnabas (which is by interpretation, the son of consolation) a Levite, a Cyprian born,

text.NRSV: He sold a field that belonged to him, then brought the money, and laid it at the apostles’ feet.
text.Rotherham: Having a field, sold it, and brought the money and laid it at the feet of the Apostles.
text.KJV: Having land, sold it, and brought the money, and laid it at the apostles’ feet.
text.NABRE: sold a piece of property that he owned, then brought the money and put it at the feet of the apostles.
text.DRB: whereas he had a piece of land, sold it, and brought the price, and laid it before the feet of the Apostles.

text.NRSV: But a man named Ananias, with the consent of his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property;
text.Rotherham: But, a certain man, Ananias by name, with Sapphira his wife, sold a possession,
text.KJV: But a certain man named Ananias, with Sapphira his wife, sold a possession,
text.NABRE: A man named Ananias, however, with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property.
text.DRB: But a certain man named Ananias, with Saphira his wife sold a piece of land,

text.NRSV: with his wife’s knowledge, he kept back some of the proceeds, and brought only a part and laid it at the apostles’ feet.
text.Rotherham: And kept back part of the price, his wife also being privy to it; and, bringing a certain part, at the feet of the Apostles, he laid it.
text.KJV: And kept back part of the price, his wife also being privy to it, and brought a certain part, and laid it at the apostles’ feet.
text.NABRE: He retained for himself, with his wife’s knowledge, some of the purchase price, took the remainder, and put it at the feet of the apostles.
text.DRB: and defrauded of the price of the land, his wife being privy thereto: and bringing a certain portion, laid it at the feet of the Apostles.

text.NRSV: “Ananias,” Peter asked, “why has Satan filled your heart to lie to the Holy Spirit and to keep back part of the proceeds of the land?
text.Rotherham: But Peter said—Ananias! wherefore did Satan fill thy heart, that thou shouldst deal falsely with the Holy Spirit, and keep back part of the price of the field?
text.KJV: But Peter said, Ananias, why hath Satan filled thine heart to lie to the Holy Ghost, and to keep back part of the price of the land?
text.NABRE: But Peter said, “Ananias, why has Satan filled your heart so that you lied to the holy Spirit and retained part of the price of the land?
text.DRB: And Peter said: Ananias, why hath Satan tempted thy heart, that thou shouldest lie to the Holy Ghost, and defraud of the price of the land?

text.NRSV: While it remained unsold, did it not remain your own? And after it was sold, were not the proceeds at your disposal? How is it that you have contrived this deed in your heart? You did not lie to us but to God!”
text.Rotherham: While it remained, was it not, as thine own, it remained? and, when sold, was it not, in thine own authority, that it still continued? Why was it that thou didst contrive in thy heart this deed? Thou hast not dealt falsely with men but, with God.
text.KJV: Whiles it remained, was it not thine own? and after it was sold, was it not in thine own power? why hast thou conceived this thing in thine heart? thou hast not lied unto men, but unto God.
text.NABRE: While it remained unsold, did it not remain yours? And when it was sold, was it not still under your control? Why did you contrive this deed? You have lied not to human beings, but to God.”
text.DRB: Remaining, did it not remain to thee: and being sold, was it not in thy power? Why hast thou put this thing in thy heart? Thou hast not lied to men, but to God.

text.NRSV: Now when Ananias heard these words, he fell down and died. And great fear seized all who heard of it.
text.Rotherham: And, as Ananias heard these words, he fell, and expired. And there came great fear upon all that heard;
text.KJV: And Ananias hearing these words fell down, and gave up the ghost: and great fear came on all them that heard these things.
text.NABRE: When Ananias heard these words, he fell down and breathed his last, and great fear came upon all who heard of it.
text.DRB: And Ananias hearing these words, fell down, and gave up the ghost. And there came great fear upon all that heard it.

text.NRSV: The young men came and wrapped up his body, then carried him out and buried him.
text.Rotherham: But the young men, rising up, wrapped him about, and, bearing him forth, buried him.
text.KJV: And the young men arose, wound him up, and carried him out, and buried him.
text.NABRE: The young men came and wrapped him up, then carried him out and buried him.
text.DRB: And young men rising up, removed him, & bearing him forth buried him.

text.NRSV: After an interval of about three hours his wife came in, not knowing what had happened.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after about three hours interval, that his wife, not knowing what had happened, came in.
text.KJV: And it was about the space of three hours after, when his wife, not knowing what was done, came in.
text.NABRE: After an interval of about three hours, his wife came in, unaware of what had happened.
text.DRB: And it was the space as it were of three hours, and his wife, not knowing what was chanced, came in.

text.NRSV: Peter said to her, “Tell me whether you and your husband sold the land for such and such a price.” And she said, “Yes, that was the price.”
text.Rotherham: And Peter began to say unto her—Tell me! was it, for so much, ye gave up the field? And she said—Yea! for so much.
text.KJV: And Peter answered unto her, Tell me whether ye sold the land for so much? And she said, Yea, for so much.
text.NABRE: Peter said to her, “Tell me, did you sell the land for this amount?” She answered, “Yes, for that amount.”
text.DRB: And Peter answered her: Tell me woman, whether did you sell the land for so much? But she said: Yea, for so much.

text.NRSV: Then Peter said to her, “How is it that you have agreed together to put the Spirit of the Lord to the test? Look, the feet of those who have buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out.”
text.Rotherham: And, Peter, [said] unto her—Why was it agreed by you to put to the proof the Spirit of the Lord? Lo! the feet of them that have buried thy husband, are at the door, and they shall bear thee forth.
text.KJV: Then Peter said unto her, How is it that ye have agreed together to tempt the Spirit of the Lord? behold, the feet of them which have buried thy husband are at the door, and shall carry thee out.
text.NABRE: Then Peter said to her, “Why did you agree to test the Spirit of the Lord? Listen, the footsteps of those who have buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out.”
text.DRB: And Peter unto her: Why have you agreed together to tempt the Spirit of our Lord? Behold, their feet that have buried thy husband, at the door, and they shall bear thee forth.

text.NRSV: Immediately she fell down at his feet and died. When the young men came in they found her dead, so they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
text.Rotherham: And she fell instantly at his feet, and expired. And the young men, coming in, found her dead; and, bearing her forth, they buried her with her husband.
text.KJV: Then fell she down straightway at his feet, and yielded up the ghost: and the young men came in, and found her dead, and, carrying her forth, buried her by her husband.
text.NABRE: At once, she fell down at his feet and breathed her last. When the young men entered they found her dead, so they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
text.DRB: Forthwith she fell before his feet, and gave up the ghost. And the young men going in, found her dead: and carried her forth, & buried her by her husband.

text.NRSV: And great fear seized the whole church and all who heard of these things.
text.Rotherham: And there came great fear upon the whole assembly, and upon all them that heard these things.
text.KJV: And great fear came upon all the church, and upon as many as heard these things.
text.NABRE: And great fear came upon the whole church and upon all who heard of these things. Signs and Wonders of the Apostles.
text.DRB: And there fell great fear in the whole Church, and upon all that heard these things.

text.NRSV: Now many signs and wonders were done among the people through the apostles. And they were all together in Solomon’s Portico.
text.Rotherham: And, through the hands of the Apostles, were coming to pass many signs and wonders among the people; and they were all with one accord in the portico of Solomon;
text.KJV: And by the hands of the apostles were many signs and wonders wrought among the people; (and they were all with one accord in Solomon’s porch.
text.NABRE: Many signs and wonders were done among the people at the hands of the apostles. They were all together in Solomon’s portico.
text.DRB: And by the hands of the Apostles were many signs and wonders done among the people. And they were all with one accord in Salomon's porch.

text.NRSV: None of the rest dared to join them, but the people held them in high esteem.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, of the rest, no one, durst join himself unto them,—nevertheless the people continued to magnify them;
text.KJV: And of the rest durst no man join himself to them: but the people magnified them.
text.NABRE: None of the others dared to join them, but the people esteemed them.
text.DRB: But of the rest none durst join themselves unto them: but the people magnified them.

text.NRSV: Yet more than ever believers were added to the Lord, great numbers of both men and women,
text.Rotherham: And, the more, were being added, when they believed in the Lord, throngs both of men and women;—
text.KJV: And believers were the more added to the Lord, multitudes both of men and women.)
text.NABRE: Yet more than ever, believers in the Lord, great numbers of men and women, were added to them.
text.DRB: And the multitude of men and women that believed in our Lord, was more increased:

text.NRSV: so that they even carried out the sick into the streets, and laid them on cots and mats, in order that Peter’s shadow might fall on some of them as he came by.
text.Rotherham: So that, even into the broad-ways, were they bringing forth the sick, and laying them on small couches and beds, in order that, if Peter were coming along, even perchance his shadow, might overshadow some one of them.
text.KJV: Insomuch that they brought forth the sick into the streets, and laid them on beds and couches, that at the least the shadow of Peter passing by might overshadow some of them.
text.NABRE: Thus they even carried the sick out into the streets and laid them on cots and mats so that when Peter came by, at least his shadow might fall on one or another of them.
text.DRB: so that they did bring forth the sick into the streets, & laid them in beds and couches, that when Peter came, his shadow at the least might overshadow any of them, and they all might be delivered from their infirmities.

text.NRSV: A great number of people would also gather from the towns around Jerusalem, bringing the sick and those tormented by unclean spirits, and they were all cured.
text.Rotherham: Moreover even the throng of the cities all round Jerusalem was coming together, bearing sick folk, and such as were harassed by impure spirits,—who, indeed, were being cured, one and all.
text.KJV: There came also a multitude out of the cities round about unto Jerusalem, bringing sick folks, and them which were vexed with unclean spirits: and they were healed every one.
text.NABRE: A large number of people from the towns in the vicinity of Jerusalem also gathered, bringing the sick and those disturbed by unclean spirits, and they were all cured. Trial Before the Sanhedrin.
text.DRB: And there ran together unto Jerusalem the multitude also of the cities adjoining, bringing sick persons and such as were vexed of unclean spirits; who were all cured.

text.NRSV: Then the high priest took action; he and all who were with him (that is, the sect of the Sadducees), being filled with jealousy,
text.Rotherham: But the High-priest, arising, and all who were with him,—being the sect of the Sadducees,—were filled with jealousy,
text.KJV: Then the high priest rose up, and all they that were with him, (which is the sect of the Sadducees,) and were filled with indignation,
text.NABRE: Then the high priest rose up and all his companions, that is, the party of the Sadducees, and, filled with jealousy,
text.DRB: And the high Priest rising up, and all that were with him, which is the heresy of the Sadducees, were replenished with zeal:

text.NRSV: arrested the apostles and put them in the public prison.
text.Rotherham: And thrust their hands upon the Apostles, and put them in a public ward.
text.KJV: And laid their hands on the apostles, and put them in the common prison.
text.NABRE: laid hands upon the apostles and put them in the public jail.
text.DRB: laid hands upon the Apostles, and put them in the common prison.

text.NRSV: But during the night an angel of the Lord opened the prison doors, brought them out, and said,
text.Rotherham: But, a messenger of the Lord, by night, opened the doors of the prison; and, leading them out, said—
text.KJV: But the angel of the Lord by night opened the prison doors, and brought them forth, and said,
text.NABRE: But during the night, the angel of the Lord opened the doors of the prison, led them out, and said,
text.DRB: But an Angel of our Lord by night opening the gates of the prison, & leading them forth, said:

text.NRSV: “Go, stand in the temple and tell the people the whole message about this life.”
text.Rotherham: Be going your way, and, taking your stand, be speaking, in the temple, unto the people, all the declarations of this Life.
text.KJV: Go, stand and speak in the temple to the people all the words of this life.
text.NABRE: “Go and take your place in the temple area, and tell the people everything about this life.”
text.DRB: Go; and standing speak in the temple to the people all the words of this life.

text.NRSV: When they heard this, they entered the temple at daybreak and went on with their teaching. When the high priest and those with him arrived, they called together the council and the whole body of the elders of Israel, and sent to the prison to have them brought.
text.Rotherham: Now, when they heard this, they entered, under the dawn, into the temple, and began teaching. And the High-priest and they who were with him, arriving, called together the high-council, and all the senate of the sons of Israel; and sent off unto the prison, to have them brought.
text.KJV: And when they heard that, they entered into the temple early in the morning, and taught. But the high priest came, and they that were with him, and called the council together, and all the senate of the children of Israel, and sent to the prison to have them brought.
text.NABRE: When they heard this, they went to the temple early in the morning and taught. When the high priest and his companions arrived, they convened the Sanhedrin, the full senate of the Israelites, and sent to the jail to have them brought in.
text.DRB: Who having heard this, early in the morning entered into the temple, and taught. And the high priest coming, and they that were with him, called together the Council & all the ancients of the children of Israel: and they sent to the prison that they might be brought.

text.NRSV: But when the temple police went there, they did not find them in the prison; so they returned and reported,
text.Rotherham: And, the officers who came to the place, found them not in the prison; and, returning, brought tidings,
text.KJV: But when the officers came, and found them not in the prison, they returned, and told,
text.NABRE: But the court officers who went did not find them in the prison, so they came back and reported,
text.DRB: But when the ministers were come, and opening the prison, found them not; returning they told, saying:

text.NRSV: “We found the prison securely locked and the guards standing at the doors, but when we opened them, we found no one inside.”
text.Rotherham: Saying—The prison, found we locked with all safety, and the keepers standing at the doors; but, when we had opened the prison, inside, found we, no one.
text.KJV: Saying, The prison truly found we shut with all safety, and the keepers standing without before the doors: but when we had opened, we found no man within.
text.NABRE: “We found the jail securely locked and the guards stationed outside the doors, but when we opened them, we found no one inside.”
text.DRB: The prison truly we found shut with all diligence, and the keepers standing before the gates: but opening it, we found no man within.

text.NRSV: Now when the captain of the temple and the chief priests heard these words, they were perplexed about them, wondering what might be going on.
text.Rotherham: Now, when both the captain of the temple and the High-priests heard these words, they were utterly at a loss concerning them—what perhaps this might come to.
text.KJV: Now when the high priest and the captain of the temple and the chief priests heard these things, they doubted of them whereunto this would grow.
text.NABRE: When they heard this report, the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests were at a loss about them, as to what this would come to.
text.DRB: And as soon as the Magistrates of the temple and the chief Priests heard these words, they were in doubt of them, what would befall.

text.NRSV: Then someone arrived and announced, “Look, the men whom you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people!”
text.Rotherham: But some one, entering, brought tidings unto them—Lo! the men whom ye put in the prison, are in the temple, standing, and teaching the people!
text.KJV: Then came one and told them, saying, Behold, the men whom ye put in prison are standing in the temple, and teaching the people.
text.NABRE: Then someone came in and reported to them, “The men whom you put in prison are in the temple area and are teaching the people.”
text.DRB: And there came a certain man and told them: That the men, lo, which you did put in prison, are in the temple standing, and teaching the people.

text.NRSV: Then the captain went with the temple police and brought them, but without violence, for they were afraid of being stoned by the people.
text.Rotherham: Then, the captain with the officers, departing, brought them—not with violence,—for they were afraid of the people, lest they should be stoned;
text.KJV: Then went the captain with the officers, and brought them without violence: for they feared the people, lest they should have been stoned.
text.NABRE: Then the captain and the court officers went and brought them in, but without force, because they were afraid of being stoned by the people.
text.DRB: Then went the Magistrate with the ministers, & brought them without force, for they feared the people, lest they should be stoned.

text.NRSV: When they had brought them, they had them stand before the council. The high priest questioned them,
text.Rotherham: But, leading them in, set them in the high- council. And the High-priest questioned them,
text.KJV: And when they had brought them, they set them before the council: and the high priest asked them,
text.NABRE: When they had brought them in and made them stand before the Sanhedrin, the high priest questioned them,
text.DRB: And when they had brought them, they set them in the Council. And the high Priest asked them,

text.NRSV: saying, “We gave you strict orders not to teach in this name, yet here you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching and you are determined to bring this man’s blood on us.”
text.Rotherham: Saying—Strictly, did we charge you not to be teaching upon this name; and lo! ye have filled Jerusalem with your teaching, and are minded to bring down upon us, the blood of this man.
text.KJV: Saying, Did not we straitly command you that ye should not teach in this name? and, behold, ye have filled Jerusalem with your doctrine, and intend to bring this man’s blood upon us.
text.NABRE: “We gave you strict orders [did we not?] to stop teaching in that name. Yet you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching and want to bring this man’s blood upon us.”
text.DRB: saying: Commanding we commanded you that you should not teach in this name: and behold you have filled Jerusalem with your doctrine, and you will bring upon us the blood of this man.

text.NRSV: But Peter and the apostles answered, “We must obey God rather than any human authority.
text.Rotherham: But Peter and the Apostles, answering, said—It is needful to be yielding obedience unto God rather than unto men:—
text.KJV: Then Peter and the other apostles answered and said, We ought to obey God rather than men.
text.NABRE: But Peter and the apostles said in reply, “We must obey God rather than men.
text.DRB: But Peter answering and the Apostles, said: God must be obeyed, rather than men.

text.NRSV: The God of our ancestors raised up Jesus, whom you had killed by hanging him on a tree.
text.Rotherham: The God of our fathers, hath raised up Jesus,—whom, ye, got into your hands, suspending him upon a tree:—
text.KJV: The God of our fathers raised up Jesus, whom ye slew and hanged on a tree.
text.NABRE: The God of our ancestors raised Jesus, though you had him killed by hanging him on a tree.
text.DRB: The God of our Fathers hath raised up Jesus, whom you did kill, hanging him upon a tree.

text.NRSV: God exalted him at his right hand as Leader and Savior that he might give repentance to Israel and forgiveness of sins.
text.Rotherham: Him, as Princely-leader and Saviour, hath God exalted unto his right hand,—to give repentance unto Israel, and remission of sins.
text.KJV: Him hath God exalted with his right hand to be a Prince and a Saviour, for to give repentance to Israel, and forgiveness of sins.
text.NABRE: God exalted him at his right hand as leader and savior to grant Israel repentance and forgiveness of sins.
text.DRB: This Prince and Saviour God hath exalted with his right hand, to give repentance to Israel, and remission of sins.

text.NRSV: And we are witnesses to these things, and so is the Holy Spirit whom God has given to those who obey him.”
text.Rotherham: And, we, are witnesses of these things,—also the Holy Spirit, which God hath given unto them who are yielding obedience unto him.
text.KJV: And we are his witnesses of these things; and so is also the Holy Ghost, whom God hath given to them that obey him.
text.NABRE: We are witnesses of these things, as is the holy Spirit that God has given to those who obey him.”
text.DRB: And we are witnesses of these words, and the Holy Ghost, whom God hath given to all that obey him.

text.NRSV: When they heard this, they were enraged and wanted to kill them.
text.Rotherham: And, they, when they heard this, were cut to the heart, and were making up their minds to slay them.
text.KJV: When they heard that, they were cut to the heart, and took counsel to slay them.
text.NABRE: When they heard this, they became infuriated and wanted to put them to death.
text.DRB: When they had heard these things, it cut them to the heart, and they consulted to kill them.

text.NRSV: But a Pharisee in the council named Gamaliel, a teacher of the law, respected by all the people, stood up and ordered the men to be put outside for a short time.
text.Rotherham: But a certain man, rising up in the high-council, by name Gamaliel, a law-teacher honoured by all the people, gave orders to put the men, outside, for a little,—
text.KJV: Then stood there up one in the council, a Pharisee, named Gamaliel, a doctor of the law, had in reputation among all the people, and commanded to put the apostles forth a little space;
text.NABRE: But a Pharisee in the Sanhedrin named Gamaliel, a teacher of the law, respected by all the people, stood up, ordered the men to be put outside for a short time,
text.DRB: But one in the Council rising up, a Pharisee named Gamaliel, a Doctor of law honourable to all the people, commanded the men to be put forth a while.

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Fellow Israelites, consider carefully what you propose to do to these men.
text.Rotherham: And said unto them—Men of Israel! be taking heed unto yourselves, what ye are about to inflict upon these men.
text.KJV: And said unto them, Ye men of Israel, take heed to yourselves what ye intend to do as touching these men.
text.NABRE: and said to them, “Fellow Israelites, be careful what you are about to do to these men.
text.DRB: and he said to them: Ye men of Israel, take heed to yourselves touching these men what you mean to do.

text.NRSV: For some time ago Theudas rose up, claiming to be somebody, and a number of men, about four hundred, joined him; but he was killed, and all who followed him were dispersed and disappeared.
text.Rotherham: For, before these days, there rose up one Theudas, affirming, himself, to be somebody,—unto whom was inclined a number of men, about four hundred; who was slain, and, all as many as had been trusting in him, were disbanded, and came to nothing.
text.KJV: For before these days rose up Theudas, boasting himself to be somebody; to whom a number of men, about four hundred, joined themselves: who was slain; and all, as many as obeyed him, were scattered, and brought to nought.
text.NABRE: Some time ago, Theudas appeared, claiming to be someone important, and about four hundred men joined him, but he was killed, and all those who were loyal to him were disbanded and came to nothing.
text.DRB: For before these days there rose Theodas, saying he was somebody, to whom consented a number of men about four hundred, who was slain: and all that believed him, were dispersed, and brought to nothing.

text.NRSV: After him Judas the Galilean rose up at the time of the census and got people to follow him; he also perished, and all who followed him were scattered.
text.Rotherham: After him, rose up Judas the Galilaean, in the days of the enrolling, and drew a people into revolt after him; and, he, perished, and, all, as many as had trusted in him, were scattered.
text.KJV: After this man rose up Judas of Galilee in the days of the taxing, and drew away much people after him: he also perished; and all, even as many as obeyed him, were dispersed.
text.NABRE: After him came Judas the Galilean at the time of the census. He also drew people after him, but he too perished and all who were loyal to him were scattered.
text.DRB: After this fellow there rose Judas of Galilee in the days of the Enrolling, and drew away the people after him, and he perished: and as many as ever consented to him, were dispersed.

text.NRSV: So in the present case, I tell you, keep away from these men and let them alone; because if this plan or this undertaking is of human origin, it will fail;
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, I say unto you—stand aloof from these men, and let them alone; because if, of men, be this project or this work, it will be overthrown,—
text.KJV: And now I say unto you, Refrain from these men, and let them alone: for if this counsel or this work be of men, it will come to nought:
text.NABRE: So now I tell you, have nothing to do with these men, and let them go. For if this endeavor or this activity is of human origin, it will destroy itself.
text.DRB: And now therefore I say to you, depart from these men and let them alone: for if this counsel or work be of men, it will be dissolved:

text.NRSV: but if it is of God, you will not be able to overthrow them—in that case you may even be found fighting against God!” They were convinced by him,
text.Rotherham: But, if it is, of God, ye will not be able to overthrow them: lest once, even fighters against God, ye be found.
text.KJV: But if it be of God, ye cannot overthrow it; lest haply ye be found even to fight against God.
text.NABRE: But if it comes from God, you will not be able to destroy them; you may even find yourselves fighting against God.” They were persuaded by him.
text.DRB: but if it be of God, you are not able to dissolve them, lest perhaps you be found to resist God also. And they consented to him.

text.NRSV: and when they had called in the apostles, they had them flogged. Then they ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, and let them go.
text.Rotherham: And they were persuaded by him; and, calling unto them the Apostles, they, with beating, charged them not be speaking upon the name of Jesus; and let them go.
text.KJV: And to him they agreed: and when they had called the apostles, and beaten them, they commanded that they should not speak in the name of Jesus, and let them go.
text.NABRE: After recalling the apostles, they had them flogged, ordered them to stop speaking in the name of Jesus, and dismissed them.
text.DRB: And calling in the Apostles, after they had scourged them, they charged them that they should not speak in the name of Jesus, and dismissed them.

text.NRSV: As they left the council, they rejoiced that they were considered worthy to suffer dishonor for the sake of the name.
text.Rotherham: They, therefore, went rejoicing from the presence of the high-council; in that they had been accounted worthy, in behalf of The Name, to suffer dishonour.
text.KJV: And they departed from the presence of the council, rejoicing that they were counted worthy to suffer shame for his name.
text.NABRE: So they left the presence of the Sanhedrin, rejoicing that they had been found worthy to suffer dishonor for the sake of the name.
text.DRB: And they went from the sight of the Council rejoicing, because they were accounted worthy to suffer reproach for the name of Jesus.

text.NRSV: And every day in the temple and at home they did not cease to teach and proclaim Jesus as the Messiah.
text.Rotherham: And, every day in the temple, and at home, they ceased not to be teaching, and telling the good news as to the Anointed Jesus.
text.KJV: And daily in the temple, and in every house, they ceased not to teach and preach Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: And all day long, both at the temple and in their homes, they did not stop teaching and proclaiming the Messiah, Jesus.
text.DRB: And every day they ceased not in the temple, and from house to house to teach and evangelize Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: Now during those days, when the disciples were increasing in number, the Hellenists complained against the Hebrews because their widows were being neglected in the daily distribution of food.
text.Rotherham: But, in these days, the disciples, multiplying, there arose a murmuring of the Grecian Jews against the Hebrews, in that, their widows, were being overlooked in the daily ministry.
text.KJV: And in those days, when the number of the disciples was multiplied, there arose a murmuring of the Grecians against the Hebrews, because their widows were neglected in the daily ministration.
text.NABRE: At that time, as the number of disciples continued to grow, the Hellenists complained against the Hebrews because their widows were being neglected in the daily distribution.
text.DRB: And in those days the number of Disciples increasing, there arose a murmuring of the Greeks against the Hebrews, for that their widows were despised in the daily ministry.

text.NRSV: And the twelve called together the whole community of the disciples and said, “It is not right that we should neglect the word of God in order to wait on tables.
text.Rotherham: And the Twelve, calling near the throng of the disciples, said—It doth not seem, right, that, we, forsaking the word of God, should be ministering unto tables.
text.KJV: Then the twelve called the multitude of the disciples unto them, and said, It is not reason that we should leave the word of God, and serve tables.
text.NABRE: So the Twelve called together the community of the disciples and said, “It is not right for us to neglect the word of God to serve at table.
text.DRB: And the Twelve calling together the multitude of the Disciples, said: it is not reason, that we leave the word of God, and serve tables.

text.NRSV: Therefore, friends, select from among yourselves seven men of good standing, full of the Spirit and of wisdom, whom we may appoint to this task,
text.Rotherham: But look out for yourselves, brethren, seven men from among you, who can be well-attested, full of Spirit and wisdom,—whom we will appoint over this need;
text.KJV: Wherefore, brethren, look ye out among you seven men of honest report, full of the Holy Ghost and wisdom, whom we may appoint over this business.
text.NABRE: Brothers, select from among you seven reputable men, filled with the Spirit and wisdom, whom we shall appoint to this task,
text.DRB: Consider therefore brethren, seven men of you of good testimony, full of the Holy Ghost and wisdom, whom we may appoint over this business.

text.NRSV: while we, for our part, will devote ourselves to prayer and to serving the word.”
text.Rotherham: But, we, unto prayer and unto the ministry of the word, will give constant attention.
text.KJV: But we will give ourselves continually to prayer, and to the ministry of the word.
text.NABRE: whereas we shall devote ourselves to prayer and to the ministry of the word.”
text.DRB: But we will be instant in prayer and the ministry of the word.

text.NRSV: What they said pleased the whole community, and they chose Stephen, a man full of faith and the Holy Spirit, together with Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus, a proselyte of Antioch.
text.Rotherham: And the word was pleasing in the sight of all the throng; and they selected Stephen, a man full of faith and Holy Spirit, and Philip and Prochorus and Nicanor and Timon and Parmenas and Nicholaus, a proselyte of Antioch;
text.KJV: And the saying pleased the whole multitude: and they chose Stephen, a man full of faith and of the Holy Ghost, and Philip, and Prochorus, and Nicanor, and Timon, and Parmenas, and Nicolas a proselyte of Antioch:
text.NABRE: The proposal was acceptable to the whole community, so they chose Stephen, a man filled with faith and the holy Spirit, also Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicholas of Antioch, a convert to Judaism.
text.DRB: And the saying was liked before all the multitude. And they chose Steven a man full of faith and of the Holy Ghost, and Philippe, and Prochorus, and Nicanor, and Timon, and Parmenas, and Nicolas a stranger of Antioch:

text.NRSV: They had these men stand before the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
text.Rotherham: Whom they set before the Apostles, and, praying, they laid upon them their hands.
text.KJV: Whom they set before the apostles: and when they had prayed, they laid their hands on them.
text.NABRE: They presented these men to the apostles who prayed and laid hands on them.
text.DRB: These they did set in the presence of the Apostles: and praying they imposed hands upon them.

text.NRSV: The word of God continued to spread; the number of the disciples increased greatly in Jerusalem, and a great many of the priests became obedient to the faith.
text.Rotherham: And, the word of God, went on growing, and the number of the disciples in Jerusalem continued to multiply exceedingly; a great multitude of the priests also, were becoming obedient unto the faith.
text.KJV: And the word of God increased; and the number of the disciples multiplied in Jerusalem greatly; and a great company of the priests were obedient to the faith.
text.NABRE: The word of God continued to spread, and the number of the disciples in Jerusalem increased greatly; even a large group of priests were becoming obedient to the faith.
text.DRB: And the word of God increased, and the number of the Disciples was multiplied in Jerusalem exceedingly: a great multitude also of the Priests obeyed the faith.

text.NRSV: Stephen, full of grace and power, did great wonders and signs among the people.
text.Rotherham: Now, Stephen, full of favour and power, began to do great wonders and signs among the people.
text.KJV: And Stephen, full of faith and power, did great wonders and miracles among the people.
text.NABRE: Accusation Against Stephen. Now Stephen, filled with grace and power, was working great wonders and signs among the people.
text.DRB: And Steven full of grace and fortitude did great wonders & signs among the people.

text.NRSV: Then some of those who belonged to the synagogue of the Freedmen (as it was called), Cyrenians, Alexandrians, and others of those from Cilicia and Asia, stood up and argued with Stephen.
text.Rotherham: But there rose up certain of those out of the synagogue which is called [the synagogue] of them of Libertium and Cyrene and Alexandria, and certain of them from Cilicia and Asia,—disputing with Stephen;
text.KJV: Then there arose certain of the synagogue, which is called the synagogue of the Libertines, and Cyrenians, and Alexandrians, and of them of Cilicia and of Asia, disputing with Stephen.
text.NABRE: Certain members of the so-called Synagogue of Freedmen, Cyrenians, and Alexandrians, and people from Cilicia and Asia, came forward and debated with Stephen,
text.DRB: And there arose certain of that which is called the Synagogue of the Libertines, and of the Cyrenians, and of the Alexandrians, and of them that were of Cilicia and Asia, disputing with Steven:

text.NRSV: But they could not withstand the wisdom and the Spirit with which he spoke.
text.Rotherham: And they could not withstand the wisdom and the Spirit with which he was speaking,
text.KJV: And they were not able to resist the wisdom and the spirit by which he spake.
text.NABRE: but they could not withstand the wisdom and the spirit with which he spoke.
text.DRB: and they could not resist the wisdom and the Spirit that spake.

text.NRSV: Then they secretly instigated some men to say, “We have heard him speak blasphemous words against Moses and God.”
text.Rotherham: Then, they set on men who were saying—We have heard him speaking profane things against Moses and God.
text.KJV: Then they suborned men, which said, We have heard him speak blasphemous words against Moses, and against God.
text.NABRE: Then they instigated some men to say, “We have heard him speaking blasphemous words against Moses and God.”
text.DRB: Then they suborned men, to say they had heard him speak words of blasphemy against Moyses and God.

text.NRSV: They stirred up the people as well as the elders and the scribes; then they suddenly confronted him, seized him, and brought him before the council.
text.Rotherham: And they stirred up the people and the elders and the scribes; and, coming upon him, they caught him away, and led him into the high-council.
text.KJV: And they stirred up the people, and the elders, and the scribes, and came upon him, and caught him, and brought him to the council,
text.NABRE: They stirred up the people, the elders, and the scribes, accosted him, seized him, and brought him before the Sanhedrin.
text.DRB: They therefore stirred up the people, and the Ancients, and the Scribes: and running together they took him, and brought him into the Council,

text.NRSV: They set up false witnesses who said, “This man never stops saying things against this holy place and the law;
text.Rotherham: And they set up false witnesses, who said—This man, ceaseth not speaking things against this holy place and the law;
text.KJV: And set up false witnesses, which said, This man ceaseth not to speak blasphemous words against this holy place, and the law:
text.NABRE: They presented false witnesses who testified, “This man never stops saying things against [this] holy place and the law.
text.DRB: and they set false witnesses that said: This man ceaseth not to speak words against the holy place & the Law.

text.NRSV: for we have heard him say that this Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and will change the customs that Moses handed on to us.”
text.Rotherham: For we have heard him saying—This Jesus the Nazarene, will overthrow this place, and will change the customs which, Moses, delivered unto us.
text.KJV: For we have heard him say, that this Jesus of Nazareth shall destroy this place, and shall change the customs which Moses delivered us.
text.NABRE: For we have heard him claim that this Jesus the Nazorean will destroy this place and change the customs that Moses handed down to us.”
text.DRB: For we have heard him say, that this same Jesus of Nazareth shall destroy this place, and shall change the traditions, which Moyses delivered unto us.

text.NRSV: And all who sat in the council looked intently at him, and they saw that his face was like the face of an angel.
text.Rotherham: And, looking steadfastly at him, all they who were sitting in the high-council, saw his face, as if the face of a messenger;
text.KJV: And all that sat in the council, looking stedfastly on him, saw his face as it had been the face of an angel.
text.NABRE: All those who sat in the Sanhedrin looked intently at him and saw that his face was like the face of an angel.
text.DRB: And all that sat in the Council beholding him, saw his face as it were the face of an Angel.

text.NRSV: Then the high priest asked him, “Are these things so?”
text.Rotherham: And the High-priest said—Are these things, so?
text.KJV: Then said the high priest, Are these things so?
text.NABRE: Then the high priest asked, “Is this so?”
text.DRB: And the chief priest said: Are these things so?

text.NRSV: And Stephen replied: “Brothers and fathers, listen to me. The God of glory appeared to our ancestor Abraham when he was in Mesopotamia, before he lived in Haran,
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—Brethren and fathers, hearken! The God of Glory, appeared unto our father Abraham, while he was in Mesopotamia, before he dwelt in Haran,—
text.KJV: And he said, Men, brethren, and fathers, hearken; The God of glory appeared unto our father Abraham, when he was in Mesopotamia, before he dwelt in Charran,
text.NABRE: And he replied, “My brothers and fathers, listen. The God of glory appeared to our father Abraham while he was in Mesopotamia, before he had settled in Haran,
text.DRB: Who said: Ye men, brethren and fathers, hear. The God of glory appeared to our father Abraham when he was in Mesopotamia, before that he abode in Charan,

text.NRSV: and said to him, ‘Leave your country and your relatives and go to the land that I will show you.’
text.Rotherham: And said unto him—Get thee forth, out of thy land, and from among thy kindred, and come unto the land which, unto thee, I will point out.
text.KJV: And said unto him, Get thee out of thy country, and from thy kindred, and come into the land which I shall shew thee.
text.NABRE: and said to him, ‘Go forth from your land and [from] your kinsfolk to the land that I will show you.’
text.DRB: & said to him: Go forth out of thy country, and out of thy kindred, & come into a land that I shall shew thee.

text.NRSV: Then he left the country of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, God had him move from there to this country in which you are now living.
text.Rotherham: Then, coming forth out of the land of the Chaldeans, he dwelt in Haran; and, from thence, when his father was dead, he removed him into this land, in which, ye, now dwell;
text.KJV: Then came he out of the land of the Chaldeans, and dwelt in Charran: and from thence, when his father was dead, he removed him into this land, wherein ye now dwell.
text.NABRE: So he went forth from the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. And from there, after his father died, he made him migrate to this land where you now dwell.
text.DRB: Then went he forth out of the land of the Chaldees, and dwelt in Charan. And from thence, after his father was dead, he translated him into this land wherein you do now dwell.

text.NRSV: He did not give him any of it as a heritage, not even a foot’s length, but promised to give it to him as his possession and to his descendants after him, even though he had no child.
text.Rotherham: And gave him no inheritance therein, not even a place to set his foot on; and yet promised to give it unto him in possession, and unto his seed after him, when as yet he had not a child.
text.KJV: And he gave him none inheritance in it, no, not so much as to set his foot on: yet he promised that he would give it to him for a possession, and to his seed after him, when as yet he had no child.
text.NABRE: Yet he gave him no inheritance in it, not even a foot’s length, but he did promise to give it to him and his descendants as a possession, even though he was childless.
text.DRB: And he gave him no inheritance in it, no not the pace of a foot: and he promised to give it him in possession, and to his seed after him, when as he had no child.

text.NRSV: And God spoke in these terms, that his descendants would be resident aliens in a country belonging to others, who would enslave them and mistreat them during four hundred years.
text.Rotherham: But God, spake thus—His seed shall be a sojourner in a foreign land, and they will bring it into bondage, and ill-treat it four-hundred years;
text.KJV: And God spake on this wise, That his seed should sojourn in a strange land; and that they should bring them into bondage, and entreat them evil four hundred years.
text.NABRE: And God spoke thus, ‘His descendants shall be aliens in a land not their own, where they shall be enslaved and oppressed for four hundred years;
text.DRB: And God spake to him: That his seed shall be a sojourner in a strange country, and they shall subdue them to servitude, and shall evil entreat them four hundred years.

text.NRSV: ‘But I will judge the nation that they serve,’ said God, ‘and after that they shall come out and worship me in this place.’
text.Rotherham: And, the nation unto which they shall be in bondage, will, I, judge—said God; and, after these things, shall they come forth, and render divine service unto me, in this place.
text.KJV: And the nation to whom they shall be in bondage will I judge, said God: and after that shall they come forth, and serve me in this place.
text.NABRE: but I will bring judgment on the nation they serve,’ God said, ‘and after that they will come out and worship me in this place.’
text.DRB: and the nation which they shall serve, will I judge, said God. And after these things they shall go forth and shall serve me in this place.

text.NRSV: Then he gave him the covenant of circumcision. And so Abraham became the father of Isaac and circumcised him on the eighth day; and Isaac became the father of Jacob, and Jacob of the twelve patriarchs.
text.Rotherham: And he gave unto him a covenant of circumcision; and, thus, he begat Isaac, and circumcised him on the eighth day, and Isaac, Jacob, and Jacob, the twelve patriarchs.
text.KJV: And he gave him the covenant of circumcision: and so Abraham begat Isaac, and circumcised him the eighth day; and Isaac begat Jacob; and Jacob begat the twelve patriarchs.
text.NABRE: Then he gave him the covenant of circumcision, and so he became the father of Isaac, and circumcised him on the eighth day, as Isaac did Jacob, and Jacob the twelve patriarchs.
text.DRB: And he gave him the testament of circumcision, and so he begat Isaac, and circumcised him the eighth day: and Isaac, Jacob: and Jacob, the twelve Patriarchs.

text.NRSV: “The patriarchs, jealous of Joseph, sold him into Egypt; but God was with him,
text.Rotherham: And, the patriarchs, being jealous of Joseph, gave him up into Egypt; and, God, was, with him,
text.KJV: And the patriarchs, moved with envy, sold Joseph into Egypt: but God was with him,
text.NABRE: “And the patriarchs, jealous of Joseph, sold him into slavery in Egypt; but God was with him
text.DRB: And the Patriarchs through emulation, sold Joseph into Ægypt. And God was with him:

text.NRSV: and rescued him from all his afflictions, and enabled him to win favor and to show wisdom when he stood before Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and over all his household.
text.Rotherham: And rescued him out of all his tribulations, and gave him favour and wisdom before Pharaoh king of Egypt; and he appointed him governor over Egypt and all his house.
text.KJV: And delivered him out of all his afflictions, and gave him favour and wisdom in the sight of Pharaoh king of Egypt; and he made him governor over Egypt and all his house.
text.NABRE: and rescued him from all his afflictions. He granted him favor and wisdom before Pharaoh, the king of Egypt, who put him in charge of Egypt and [of] his entire household.
text.DRB: and delivered him out of all his tribulations. And he gave him grace and wisdom in the sight of Pharao the king of Ægypt, and he appointed him Governor over Ægypt and over all his house.

text.NRSV: Now there came a famine throughout Egypt and Canaan, and great suffering, and our ancestors could find no food.
text.Rotherham: And there came a famine upon all Egypt and Canaan, and great tribulation, and our fathers could not find pasture.
text.KJV: Now there came a dearth over all the land of Egypt and Chanaan, and great affliction: and our fathers found no sustenance.
text.NABRE: Then a famine and great affliction struck all Egypt and Canaan, and our ancestors could find no food;
text.DRB: And there came famine upon all Ægypt and Chanaan, and great tribulation: and our fathers found no victuals.

text.NRSV: But when Jacob heard that there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there on their first visit.
text.Rotherham: But Jacob, hearing there was corn in Egypt, sent off our fathers, first;
text.KJV: But when Jacob heard that there was corn in Egypt, he sent out our fathers first.
text.NABRE: but when Jacob heard that there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there a first time.
text.DRB: But when Jacob had heard that there was corn in Ægypt; he sent our fathers first:

text.NRSV: On the second visit Joseph made himself known to his brothers, and Joseph’s family became known to Pharaoh.
text.Rotherham: And, the second time, Joseph was made known unto his brethren, and, the race of Joseph was made, manifest, unto Pharaoh.
text.KJV: And at the second time Joseph was made known to his brethren; and Joseph’s kindred was made known unto Pharaoh.
text.NABRE: The second time, Joseph made himself known to his brothers, and Joseph’s family became known to Pharaoh.
text.DRB: and at the second time Joseph was known of his brethren, and his kindred was made known unto Pharao.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph sent and invited his father Jacob and all his relatives to come to him, seventy-five in all;
text.Rotherham: And Joseph, sending forth, called for Jacob his father, and all the kindred, consisting of seventy-five souls;
text.KJV: Then sent Joseph, and called his father Jacob to him, and all his kindred, threescore and fifteen souls.
text.NABRE: Then Joseph sent for his father Jacob, inviting him and his whole clan, seventy-five persons;
text.DRB: And Joseph sending, called thither Jacob his father and all his kindred in seventy five souls.

text.NRSV: so Jacob went down to Egypt. He himself died there as well as our ancestors,
text.Rotherham: And Jacob went down [into Egypt]. And he died, he, and our fathers;
text.KJV: So Jacob went down into Egypt, and died, he, and our fathers,
text.NABRE: and Jacob went down to Egypt. And he and our ancestors died
text.DRB: And Jacob descended into Ægypt: and he died, and our fathers.

text.NRSV: and their bodies were brought back to Shechem and laid in the tomb that Abraham had bought for a sum of silver from the sons of Hamor in Shechem.
text.Rotherham: And were brought over into Shechem, and laid in the tomb which Abraham had purchased, for a price of silver, of the sons of Hamor in Shechem.
text.KJV: And were carried over into Sychem, and laid in the sepulchre that Abraham bought for a sum of money of the sons of Emmor the father of Sychem.
text.NABRE: and were brought back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had purchased for a sum of money from the sons of Hamor at Shechem.
text.DRB: And they were translated into Sichem, and were laid in the sepulchre that Abraham bought for a price of silver of the sons of Hemor the son of Sichem.

text.NRSV: “But as the time drew near for the fulfillment of the promise that God had made to Abraham, our people in Egypt increased and multiplied
text.Rotherham: Now, just as the time of the promise was drawing near wherewith God had agreed with Abraham, the people grew, and were multiplied in Egypt,—
text.KJV: But when the time of the promise drew nigh, which God had sworn to Abraham, the people grew and multiplied in Egypt,
text.NABRE: “When the time drew near for the fulfillment of the promise that God pledged to Abraham, the people had increased and become very numerous in Egypt,
text.DRB: And when the time drew near of the promise which God had promised to Abraham, the people increased and was multiplied in Ægypt,

text.NRSV: until another king who had not known Joseph ruled over Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Until there arose another sort of king over Egypt, who had not known Joseph.
text.KJV: Till another king arose, which knew not Joseph.
text.NABRE: until another king who knew nothing of Joseph came to power [in Egypt].
text.DRB: until another king arose in Ægypt, that knew not Joseph.

text.NRSV: He dealt craftily with our race and forced our ancestors to abandon their infants so that they would die.
text.Rotherham: The same, dealing craftily with our race, ill-treated our fathers, so as to cause their babes to be exposed, to the end they might not be suffered to live.
text.KJV: The same dealt subtilly with our kindred, and evil entreated our fathers, so that they cast out their young children, to the end they might not live.
text.NABRE: He dealt shrewdly with our people and oppressed [our] ancestors by forcing them to expose their infants, that they might not survive.
text.DRB: This same circumventing our stock, afflicted our fathers, that they should expose their children, to the end they might not be kept alive.

text.NRSV: At this time Moses was born, and he was beautiful before God. For three months he was brought up in his father’s house;
text.Rotherham: In which season, Moses, was born, and was exceeding goodly,—who was nourished up three months in the house of his father;
text.KJV: In which time Moses was born, and was exceeding fair, and nourished up in his father’s house three months:
text.NABRE: At this time Moses was born, and he was extremely beautiful. For three months he was nursed in his father’s house;
text.DRB: The same time was Moyses born, and he was acceptable to God; who was nourished three months in his father's house.

text.NRSV: and when he was abandoned, Pharaoh’s daughter adopted him and brought him up as her own son.
text.Rotherham: But, when he was exposed, the daughter of Pharaoh rescued him, and nourished him for herself, as a son.
text.KJV: And when he was cast out, Pharaoh’s daughter took him up, and nourished him for her own son.
text.NABRE: but when he was exposed, Pharaoh’s daughter adopted him and brought him up as her own son.
text.DRB: And when he was exposed, Pharao's daughter took him up, and nourished him for her own son.

text.NRSV: So Moses was instructed in all the wisdom of the Egyptians and was powerful in his words and deeds.
text.Rotherham: And, so Moses was trained in all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and was mighty in his words and works.
text.KJV: And Moses was learned in all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and was mighty in words and in deeds.
text.NABRE: Moses was educated [in] all the wisdom of the Egyptians and was powerful in his words and deeds.
text.DRB: And Moyses was instructed in all the wisdom of the Ægyptians: and he was mighty in his words and works.

text.NRSV: “When he was forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his relatives, the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: And, when there was being fulfilled unto him a period of forty years, it came up on his heart to look after his brethren the sons of Israel;
text.KJV: And when he was full forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his brethren the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: “When he was forty years old, he decided to visit his kinsfolk, the Israelites.
text.DRB: And when he was fully of the age of forty years, it came to his mind to visit his brethren the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: When he saw one of them being wronged, he defended the oppressed man and avenged him by striking down the Egyptian.
text.Rotherham: And, seeing one being wronged, he defended him, and avenged him that was getting worn out, smiting the Egyptian.
text.KJV: And seeing one of them suffer wrong, he defended him, and avenged him that was oppressed, and smote the Egyptian:
text.NABRE: When he saw one of them treated unjustly, he defended and avenged the oppressed man by striking down the Egyptian.
text.DRB: And when he had seen one suffer wrong, he defended him: and striking the Ægyptian, he revenged his quarrel that sustained the wrong.

text.NRSV: He supposed that his kinsfolk would understand that God through him was rescuing them, but they did not understand.
text.Rotherham: But he supposed his brethren would understand that, God, through his hand, would give them deliverance; whereas they understood not.
text.KJV: For he supposed his brethren would have understood how that God by his hand would deliver them: but they understood not.
text.NABRE: He assumed [his] kinsfolk would understand that God was offering them deliverance through him, but they did not understand.
text.DRB: And he thought that his brethren did understand that God by his hand would save them: but they understood it not.

text.NRSV: The next day he came to some of them as they were quarreling and tried to reconcile them, saying, ‘Men, you are brothers; why do you wrong each other?’
text.Rotherham: On the following day also, he appeared unto them, as they were contending, and would have reconciled them in peace, saying—Men! ye are, brothers! Wherefore wrong ye one another?
text.KJV: And the next day he shewed himself unto them as they strove, and would have set them at one again, saying, Sirs, ye are brethren; why do ye wrong one to another?
text.NABRE: The next day he appeared to them as they were fighting and tried to reconcile them peacefully, saying, ‘Men, you are brothers. Why are you harming one another?’
text.DRB: And the day following he appeared to them being at strife: and he reconciled them unto peace, saying: Men, ye are brethren, wherefore hurt you one another?

text.NRSV: But the man who was wronging his neighbor pushed Moses aside, saying, ‘Who made you a ruler and a judge over us?
text.Rotherham: But, he that was wronging his neighbour, thrust him away, saying—Who hath appointed, thee, to be ruler and judge over us?
text.KJV: But he that did his neighbour wrong thrust him away, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a judge over us?
text.NABRE: Then the one who was harming his neighbor pushed him aside, saying, ‘Who appointed you ruler and judge over us?
text.DRB: But he that did the injury to his neighbour, repelled him, saying: Who hath appointed thee prince and judge over us:

text.NRSV: Do you want to kill me as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?’
text.Rotherham: Art, thou, wishing, to kill me, in the same way thou didst kill, yesterday, the Egyptian?
text.KJV: Wilt thou kill me, as thou diddest the Egyptian yesterday?
text.NABRE: Are you thinking of killing me as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?’
text.DRB: What, wilt thou kill me, as thou didst yesterday kill the Ægyptian?

text.NRSV: When he heard this, Moses fled and became a resident alien in the land of Midian. There he became the father of two sons.
text.Rotherham: And Moses fled at this saying, and became a sojourner in the land of Midian, where he begat two sons.
text.KJV: Then fled Moses at this saying, and was a stranger in the land of Madian, where he begat two sons.
text.NABRE: Moses fled when he heard this and settled as an alien in the land of Midian, where he became the father of two sons.
text.DRB: And Moyses fled upon this word: and he became a sojourner in the land of Madian, where he begat two sons.

text.NRSV: “Now when forty years had passed, an angel appeared to him in the wilderness of Mount Sinai, in the flame of a burning bush.
text.Rotherham: And, when forty years were fulfilled, there appeared unto him, in the desert of Mount Sinai, a messenger, in a flame of fire, in a bush.
text.KJV: And when forty years were expired, there appeared to him in the wilderness of mount Sina an angel of the Lord in a flame of fire in a bush.
text.NABRE: “Forty years later, an angel appeared to him in the desert near Mount Sinai in the flame of a burning bush.
text.DRB: And after forty years were expired, there appeared to him in the desert of mount Sina an Angel in the fire of the flame of a bush.

text.NRSV: When Moses saw it, he was amazed at the sight; and as he approached to look, there came the voice of the Lord:
text.Rotherham: And, Moses, seeing it, marvelled at the sight; and, as he was going near to observe, there came a voice of the Lord—
text.KJV: When Moses saw it, he wondered at the sight: and as he drew near to behold it, the voice of the Lord came unto him,
text.NABRE: When Moses saw it, he was amazed at the sight, and as he drew near to look at it, the voice of the Lord came,
text.DRB: And Moyses seeing it, marveled at the vision. And as he went near to view it, the voice of our Lord was made to him:

text.NRSV: ‘I am the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.’ Moses began to tremble and did not dare to look.
text.Rotherham: I, am the God of thy fathers, The God of Abraham and Isaac and Jacob. And Moses becoming, terrified, durst not observe.
text.KJV: Saying, I am the God of thy fathers, the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. Then Moses trembled, and durst not behold.
text.NABRE: ‘I am the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob.’ Then Moses, trembling, did not dare to look at it.
text.DRB: I am the God of thy fathers; the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. And Moyses being made to tremble, durst not view it.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to him, ‘Take off the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground.
text.Rotherham: And the Lord said unto him—Loose the sandals of thy feet; for, the place whereon thou art standing, is, hallowed ground.
text.KJV: Then said the Lord to him, Put off thy shoes from thy feet: for the place where thou standest is holy ground.
text.NABRE: But the Lord said to him, ‘Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place where you stand is holy ground.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to him: Loose off the shoe of thy feet, for the place wherein thou standest, is holy ground.

text.NRSV: I have surely seen the mistreatment of my people who are in Egypt and have heard their groaning, and I have come down to rescue them. Come now, I will send you to Egypt.’
text.Rotherham: I have, indeed seen, the ill-treatment of my people that is in Egypt, and, unto their groaning, have I hearkened, and have come down to rescue them. Now, therefore, come! I will send thee into Egypt.
text.KJV: I have seen, I have seen the affliction of my people which is in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning, and am come down to deliver them. And now come, I will send thee into Egypt.
text.NABRE: I have witnessed the affliction of my people in Egypt and have heard their groaning, and I have come down to rescue them. Come now, I will send you to Egypt.’
text.DRB: Seeing I have seen the affliction of my people which is in Ægypt, and I have heard their groaning and am descended to deliver them. And now come, and I will send thee into Ægypt.

text.NRSV: “It was this Moses whom they rejected when they said, ‘Who made you a ruler and a judge?’ and whom God now sent as both ruler and liberator through the angel who appeared to him in the bush.
text.Rotherham: The same Moses whom they had refused, saying—Who hath appointed thee to be ruler and judge? the same, both as ruler and redeemer, did God send, by the hand of the messenger who had appeared unto him in the bush:
text.KJV: This Moses whom they refused, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a judge? the same did God send to be a ruler and a deliverer by the hand of the angel which appeared to him in the bush.
text.NABRE: This Moses, whom they had rejected with the words, ‘Who appointed you ruler and judge?’ God sent as [both] ruler and deliverer, through the angel who appeared to him in the bush.
text.DRB: This Moyses, whom they denied, saying: Who hath appointed thee prince and Captain? him God sent prince & redeemer with the hand of the Angel that appeared to him in the bush.

text.NRSV: He led them out, having performed wonders and signs in Egypt, at the Red Sea, and in the wilderness for forty years.
text.Rotherham: The same, led them forth, doing wonders and signs in Egypt, and in the Red Sea, and in the desert, forty years:
text.KJV: He brought them out, after that he had shewed wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, and in the Red sea, and in the wilderness forty years.
text.NABRE: This man led them out, performing wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, at the Red Sea, and in the desert for forty years.
text.DRB: He brought them forth doing wonders and signs in the land of Ægypt, and in the red sea, and in the desert forty years.

text.NRSV: This is the Moses who said to the Israelites, ‘God will raise up a prophet for you from your own people as he raised me up.’
text.Rotherham: The same, is the Moses that said unto the sons of Israel—A prophet, unto you, will God raise up, from among your brethren, like unto me:
text.KJV: This is that Moses, which said unto the children of Israel, A prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear.
text.NABRE: It was this Moses who said to the Israelites, ‘God will raise up for you, from among your own kinsfolk, a prophet like me.’
text.DRB: This is that Moyses which said to the children of Israel: A Prophet will God raise up to you of your own brethren as myself: him you shall hear.

text.NRSV: He is the one who was in the congregation in the wilderness with the angel who spoke to him at Mount Sinai, and with our ancestors; and he received living oracles to give to us.
text.Rotherham: The same, is he that came to be in the assembly in the desert, with the messenger who was speaking with him in Mount Sinai, and with our fathers, he who welcomed living utterances, to give unto us:
text.KJV: This is he, that was in the church in the wilderness with the angel which spake to him in the mount Sina, and with our fathers: who received the lively oracles to give unto us:
text.NABRE: It was he who, in the assembly in the desert, was with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai and with our ancestors, and he received living utterances to hand on to us.
text.DRB: This is he that was in the assembly in the wilderness, with the Angel that spake to him in Mount Sina, and with our fathers: who received the words of life to give unto us.
