text.Rotherham: And, as within himself Peter was doubting what the vision which he had seen might mean, lo! the men who had been sent by Cornelius, having sought out the house of Simon, stood at the gate,
text.KJV: Now while Peter doubted in himself what this vision which he had seen should mean, behold, the men which were sent from Cornelius had made enquiry for Simon’s house, and stood before the gate,
text.NABRE: While Peter was in doubt about the meaning of the vision he had seen, the men sent by Cornelius asked for Simon’s house and arrived at the entrance.
text.DRB: And whiles Peter doubted within himself, what the vision should be that he had seen, behold the men that were sent from Cornelius, inquiring for Simon's house, stood at the gate.

text.NRSV: They called out to ask whether Simon, who was called Peter, was staying there.
text.Rotherham: And, calling, enquired whether, Simon who was surnamed Peter, was there being entertained.
text.KJV: And called, and asked whether Simon, which was surnamed Peter, were lodged there.
text.NABRE: They called out inquiring whether Simon, who is called Peter, was staying there.
text.DRB: And when they had called, they asked, if Simon that is surnamed Peter were lodged there.

text.NRSV: While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Spirit said to him, “Look, three men are searching for you.
text.Rotherham: And, as Peter was pondering over the vision, the Spirit said—Lo! two men, seeking thee.
text.KJV: While Peter thought on the vision, the Spirit said unto him, Behold, three men seek thee.
text.NABRE: As Peter was pondering the vision, the Spirit said [to him], “There are three men here looking for you.
text.DRB: And as Peter was thinking of the vision, the Spirit said to him: Behold, three men do seek thee.

text.NRSV: Now get up, go down, and go with them without hesitation; for I have sent them.”
text.Rotherham: But rise, go down, and be journeying with them, nothing, doubting; because, I, have sent them.
text.KJV: Arise therefore, and get thee down, and go with them, doubting nothing: for I have sent them.
text.NABRE: So get up, go downstairs, and accompany them without hesitation, because I have sent them.”
text.DRB: Arise therefore, and get thee down, and go with them, doubting nothing; for I have sent them.

text.NRSV: So Peter went down to the men and said, “I am the one you are looking for; what is the reason for your coming?”
text.Rotherham: And Peter, going down unto the men, said—Lo! I, am he whom ye are seeking: What is the cause, for which ye are come?
text.KJV: Then Peter went down to the men which were sent unto him from Cornelius; and said, Behold, I am he whom ye seek: what is the cause wherefore ye are come?
text.NABRE: Then Peter went down to the men and said, “I am the one you are looking for. What is the reason for your being here?”
text.DRB: And Peter going down to the men, said: Behold I am he whom you seek; what is the cause, for the which you are come?

text.NRSV: They answered, “Cornelius, a centurion, an upright and God-fearing man, who is well spoken of by the whole Jewish nation, was directed by a holy angel to send for you to come to his house and to hear what you have to say.”
text.Rotherham: And they said—Cornelius, a centurion, a man righteous and fearing God, well-attested by the whole nation of the Jews, hath been divinely instructed by a holy messenger to send for thee unto his house, and to hear words from thee.
text.KJV: And they said, Cornelius the centurion, a just man, and one that feareth God, and of good report among all the nation of the Jews, was warned from God by an holy angel to send for thee into his house, and to hear words of thee.
text.NABRE: They answered, “Cornelius, a centurion, an upright and God-fearing man, respected by the whole Jewish nation, was directed by a holy angel to summon you to his house and to hear what you have to say.”
text.DRB: Who said: Cornelius the Centurion, a just man and that feareth God, and having testimony of all the nation of the Jews, received an answer of an holy Angel to send for thee into his house, and to hear words of thee.

text.NRSV: So Peter invited them in and gave them lodging. The next day he got up and went with them, and some of the believers from Joppa accompanied him.
text.Rotherham: Inviting them in, therefore, he entertained them; but, on the morrow, he rose up and went forth with them, and certain of the brethren who were from Joppa went with him;
text.KJV: Then called he them in, and lodged them. And on the morrow Peter went away with them, and certain brethren from Joppa accompanied him.
text.NABRE: So he invited them in and showed them hospitality. The next day he got up and went with them, and some of the brothers from Joppa went with him.
text.DRB: Therefore bringing them in, he lodged them. And the day following he arose and went with them: and certain of the brethren of Joppe accompanied him.

text.NRSV: The following day they came to Caesarea. Cornelius was expecting them and had called together his relatives and close friends.
text.Rotherham: And, on the morrow, he entered into Caesarea. And, Cornelius, was expecting them, having called together his kinsfolk and intimate friends.
text.KJV: And the morrow after they entered into Cesarea. And Cornelius waited for them, and had called together his kinsmen and near friends.
text.NABRE: On the following day he entered Caesarea. Cornelius was expecting them and had called together his relatives and close friends.
text.DRB: And on the morrow he entered into Cæsarea. And Cornelius expected them, having called together his kin, and special friends.

text.NRSV: On Peter’s arrival Cornelius met him, and falling at his feet, worshiped him.
text.Rotherham: And, when it came about that Peter entered, Cornelius met him, and, falling at his feet, did homage.
text.KJV: And as Peter was coming in, Cornelius met him, and fell down at his feet, and worshipped him.
text.NABRE: When Peter entered, Cornelius met him and, falling at his feet, paid him homage.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when Peter was come in, Cornelius came to meet him, & falling at his feet adored.

text.NRSV: But Peter made him get up, saying, “Stand up; I am only a mortal.”
text.Rotherham: But, Peter, raised him up, saying—Arise! I also myself, am, a man.
text.KJV: But Peter took him up, saying, Stand up; I myself also am a man.
text.NABRE: Peter, however, raised him up, saying, “Get up. I myself am also a human being.”
text.DRB: But Peter lifted him up saying: Arise, myself also am a man.

text.NRSV: And as he talked with him, he went in and found that many had assembled;
text.Rotherham: And, conversing with him, he went in, and findeth many come together;
text.KJV: And as he talked with him, he went in, and found many that were come together.
text.NABRE: While he conversed with him, he went in and found many people gathered together
text.DRB: And talking with him, he went in, and findeth many that were assembled.

text.NRSV: and he said to them, “You yourselves know that it is unlawful for a Jew to associate with or to visit a Gentile; but God has shown me that I should not call anyone profane or unclean.
text.Rotherham: And said unto the—Ye, well know, how unlawful it is, for, a Jew, to be joining himself or coming in unto one of another race. And yet, unto me, hath God pointed out that I should be calling no man, common or unclean.
text.KJV: And he said unto them, Ye know how that it is an unlawful thing for a man that is a Jew to keep company, or come unto one of another nation; but God hath shewed me that I should not call any man common or unclean.
text.NABRE: and said to them, “You know that it is unlawful for a Jewish man to associate with, or visit, a Gentile, but God has shown me that I should not call any person profane or unclean.
text.DRB: And he said to them: You know how abominable it is for a man that is a Jew, to join, or to approach unto a stranger: but God hath shewed to me, to call no man common or unclean.

text.NRSV: So when I was sent for, I came without objection. Now may I ask why you sent for me?”
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, even without gainsaying, came I when sent for. I ask, therefore, for what reason ye sent for me.
text.KJV: Therefore came I unto you without gainsaying, as soon as I was sent for: I ask therefore for what intent ye have sent for me?
text.NABRE: And that is why I came without objection when sent for. May I ask, then, why you summoned me?”
text.DRB: For the which cause, making no doubt, I came when I was sent for. I demand therefore, for what cause you have sent for me?

text.NRSV: Cornelius replied, “Four days ago at this very hour, at three o’clock, I was praying in my house when suddenly a man in dazzling clothes stood before me.
text.Rotherham: And Cornelius said—Four days ago, counting unto this very hour, I was keeping, the ninth hour, as one of prayer, in my house. And lo! a man stood before me, in bright clothing,
text.KJV: And Cornelius said, Four days ago I was fasting until this hour; and at the ninth hour I prayed in my house, and, behold, a man stood before me in bright clothing,
text.NABRE: Cornelius replied, “Four days ago at this hour, three o’clock in the afternoon, I was at prayer in my house when suddenly a man in dazzling robes stood before me and said,
text.DRB: And Cornelius said: Four days since, until this hour, I was praying the ninth hour in my house, and behold a man stood before me in white apparel,

text.NRSV: He said, ‘Cornelius, your prayer has been heard and your alms have been remembered before God.
text.Rotherham: And saith—Cornelius! thy prayer hath been heard, and, thine alms, have been remembered before God.
text.KJV: And said, Cornelius, thy prayer is heard, and thine alms are had in remembrance in the sight of God.
text.NABRE: ‘Cornelius, your prayer has been heard and your almsgiving remembered before God.
text.DRB: and said: Cornelius, thy prayer is heard, and thy alms-deeds are in memory in the sight of God.

text.NRSV: Send therefore to Joppa and ask for Simon, who is called Peter; he is staying in the home of Simon, a tanner, by the sea.’
text.Rotherham: Send, therefore, unto Joppa, and fetch Simon, who is surnamed Peter. The same is being entertained in the house of one Simon a tanner, by the sea.
text.KJV: Send therefore to Joppa, and call hither Simon, whose surname is Peter; he is lodged in the house of one Simon a tanner by the sea side: who, when he cometh, shall speak unto thee.
text.NABRE: Send therefore to Joppa and summon Simon, who is called Peter. He is a guest in the house of Simon, a tanner, by the sea.’
text.DRB: Send therefore to Joppe, and call hither Simon that is surnamed Peter: he lodgeth in the house of Simon a tanner by the sea side.

text.NRSV: Therefore I sent for you immediately, and you have been kind enough to come. So now all of us are here in the presence of God to listen to all that the Lord has commanded you to say.”
text.Rotherham: Immediately, therefore, I sent unto thee: Thou, also hast, well, done in coming. Now, therefore, all we, before God are present, to hear all things that have been enjoined upon thee by the Lord.
text.KJV: Immediately therefore I sent to thee; and thou hast well done that thou art come. Now therefore are we all here present before God, to hear all things that are commanded thee of God.
text.NABRE: So I sent for you immediately, and you were kind enough to come. Now therefore we are all here in the presence of God to listen to all that you have been commanded by the Lord.” Peter’s Speech.
text.DRB: Immediately therefore I sent to thee: and thou hast done well in coming. Now therefore all we are present in thy sight, to hear all things whatsoever are commanded thee of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Peter began to speak to them: “I truly understand that God shows no partiality,
text.Rotherham: And Peter, opening his mouth, said—Of a truth, I find that God is no respecter of persons;
text.KJV: Then Peter opened his mouth, and said, Of a truth I perceive that God is no respecter of persons:
text.NABRE: Then Peter proceeded to speak and said, “In truth, I see that God shows no partiality.
text.DRB: And Peter opening his mouth, said: In very deed I perceive that God is not an accepter of persons.

text.NRSV: but in every nation anyone who fears him and does what is right is acceptable to him.
text.Rotherham: But, in every nation, he that feareth him and worketh righteousness, is acceptable unto him.
text.KJV: But in every nation he that feareth him, and worketh righteousness, is accepted with him.
text.NABRE: Rather, in every nation whoever fears him and acts uprightly is acceptable to him.
text.DRB: But in every nation, he that feareth him, and worketh justice, is acceptable to him.

text.NRSV: You know the message he sent to the people of Israel, preaching peace by Jesus Christ—he is Lord of all.
text.Rotherham: As touching the word he hath sent unto the sons of Israel, announcing the glad tidings of peace through Jesus Christ—the same, is Lord, of all,
text.KJV: The word which God sent unto the children of Israel, preaching peace by Jesus Christ: (he is Lord of all:)
text.NABRE: You know the word [that] he sent to the Israelites as he proclaimed peace through Jesus Christ, who is Lord of all,
text.DRB: The word did God send to the children of Israel, preaching peace by Jesus Christ (this is Lord of all.)

text.NRSV: That message spread throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee after the baptism that John announced:
text.Rotherham: Ye yourselves, know what hath come to pass throughout the whole of Judaea, beginning from Galilee, after the immersion which John proclaimed, respecting Jesus who was of Nazareth:—
text.KJV: That word, I say, ye know, which was published throughout all Judea, and began from Galilee, after the baptism which John preached;
text.NABRE: what has happened all over Judea, beginning in Galilee after the baptism that John preached,
text.DRB: You know the word that hath been made through all Jewry, for beginning from Galilee, after the baptism which John preached:

text.NRSV: how God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power; how he went about doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, for God was with him.
text.Rotherham: How God anointed him with Holy Spirit and with power, who went about doing good and healing all that were oppressed by the adversary, because, God, was with him.
text.KJV: How God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Ghost and with power: who went about doing good, and healing all that were oppressed of the devil; for God was with him.
text.NABRE: how God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the holy Spirit and power. He went about doing good and healing all those oppressed by the devil, for God was with him.
text.DRB: Jesus of Nazareth how God anointed him with the Holy Ghost and with power, who went throughout doing good and healing all that were oppressed of the devil, because God was with him.

text.NRSV: We are witnesses to all that he did both in Judea and in Jerusalem. They put him to death by hanging him on a tree;
text.Rotherham: We also, are witnesses of all things which he did, both in the country of the Jews and Jerusalem; whom they even slew by suspending upon a tree;—
text.KJV: And we are witnesses of all things which he did both in the land of the Jews, and in Jerusalem; whom they slew and hanged on a tree:
text.NABRE: We are witnesses of all that he did both in the country of the Jews and [in] Jerusalem. They put him to death by hanging him on a tree.
text.DRB: And we are witnesses of all things that he did in the country of the Jews and in Jerusalem, whom they killed hanging him upon a tree.

text.NRSV: but God raised him on the third day and allowed him to appear,
text.Rotherham: The same, God raised up on the third day, and gave him to become, manifest,
text.KJV: Him God raised up the third day, and shewed him openly;
text.NABRE: This man God raised [on] the third day and granted that he be visible,
text.DRB: Him God raised up the third day and gave him to be made manifest,

text.NRSV: not to all the people but to us who were chosen by God as witnesses, and who ate and drank with him after he rose from the dead.
text.Rotherham: Not unto all the people, but unto witnesses who had been fore-appointed by God, unto us, who, indeed, did eat and drink with him after his rising from among the dead.
text.KJV: Not to all the people, but unto witnesses chosen before of God, even to us, who did eat and drink with him after he rose from the dead.
text.NABRE: not to all the people, but to us, the witnesses chosen by God in advance, who ate and drank with him after he rose from the dead.
text.DRB: not to all the people, but to us, who did eat and drink with him after he rose again from the dead.

text.NRSV: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one ordained by God as judge of the living and the dead.
text.Rotherham: And he charged us to proclaim unto the people, and bear full witness, that—This, is he that hath been marked out by God to be judge of living and dead.
text.KJV: And he commanded us to preach unto the people, and to testify that it is he which was ordained of God to be the Judge of quick and dead.
text.NABRE: He commissioned us to preach to the people and testify that he is the one appointed by God as judge of the living and the dead.
text.DRB: And he commanded us to preach to the people, & to testify that it is he that of God was appointed judge of the living and of the dead.

text.NRSV: All the prophets testify about him that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.”
text.Rotherham: Unto the same, do all the prophets bear witness, That, remission of sins, is to be received through his name, by every one that believeth on him.
text.KJV: To him give all the prophets witness, that through his name whosoever believeth in him shall receive remission of sins.
text.NABRE: To him all the prophets bear witness, that everyone who believes in him will receive forgiveness of sins through his name.”
text.DRB: To him all the prophets give testimony, that all receive remission of sins by his name, which believe in him.

text.NRSV: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit fell upon all who heard the word.
text.Rotherham: While Peter was yet speaking these words, the Holy Spirit fell upon all who were hearing the word.
text.KJV: While Peter yet spake these words, the Holy Ghost fell on all them which heard the word.
text.NABRE: The Baptism of Cornelius. While Peter was still speaking these things, the holy Spirit fell upon all who were listening to the word.
text.DRB: As Peter was yet speaking these words, the Holy Ghost fell upon all that heard the word.

text.NRSV: The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were astounded that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles,
text.Rotherham: And the faithful, of the circumcision, who had come with Peter, were amazed,—in that, upon the nations also, the free-gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out;
text.KJV: And they of the circumcision which believed were astonished, as many as came with Peter, because that on the Gentiles also was poured out the gift of the Holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: The circumcised believers who had accompanied Peter were astounded that the gift of the holy Spirit should have been poured out on the Gentiles also,
text.DRB: And the faithful of the Circumcision that came with Peter, were astonished, for that the grace of the Holy Ghost was poured out upon the Gentiles also.

text.NRSV: for they heard them speaking in tongues and extolling God. Then Peter said,
text.Rotherham: For they heard them speaking with tongues, and magnifying God. Then answered Peter—
text.KJV: For they heard them speak with tongues, and magnify God. Then answered Peter,
text.NABRE: for they could hear them speaking in tongues and glorifying God. Then Peter responded,
text.DRB: For they heard them speaking with tongues, and magnifying God.

text.NRSV: “Can anyone withhold the water for baptizing these people who have received the Holy Spirit just as we have?”
text.Rotherham: Surely then, the water, can no man forbid, that these should not be immersed,—seeing that, the Holy Spirit, they have received, as well as we.
text.KJV: Can any man forbid water, that these should not be baptized, which have received the Holy Ghost as well as we?
text.NABRE: “Can anyone withhold the water for baptizing these people, who have received the holy Spirit even as we have?”
text.DRB: Then Peter answered: Can any man forbid water, that these should not be baptized which have received the Holy Ghost as well as we?

text.NRSV: So he ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they invited him to stay for several days.
text.Rotherham: And he commanded them in the name of Jesus Christ to be immersed. Then, requested they him, to abide still some days.
text.KJV: And he commanded them to be baptized in the name of the Lord. Then prayed they him to tarry certain days.
text.NABRE: He ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: And he commanded them to be baptized in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ. Then they desired him that he would tarry with them certain days.

text.NABRE: Then they invited him to stay for a few days.

text.NRSV: Now the apostles and the believers who were in Judea heard that the Gentiles had also accepted the word of God.
text.Rotherham: Now the Apostles and the brethren who were throughout Judaea heard that, the nations also, had welcomed the word of God.
text.KJV: And the apostles and brethren that were in Judea heard that the Gentiles had also received the word of God.
text.NABRE: Now the apostles and the brothers who were in Judea heard that the Gentiles too had accepted the word of God.
text.DRB: And the Apostles and brethren that were in Jewry, heard that the Gentiles also received the word of God.

text.NRSV: So when Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcised believers criticized him,
text.Rotherham: And, when Peter came up unto Jerusalem, they of the circumcision began to find fault with him,
text.KJV: And when Peter was come up to Jerusalem, they that were of the circumcision contended with him,
text.NABRE: So when Peter went up to Jerusalem the circumcised believers confronted him,
text.DRB: And when Peter was come up to Jerusalem, they that were of the Circumcision reasoned against him, saying:

text.NRSV: saying, “Why did you go to uncircumcised men and eat with them?”
text.Rotherham: Saying—he went in unto men uncircumcised, and did eat with them.
text.KJV: Saying, Thou wentest in to men uncircumcised, and didst eat with them.
text.NABRE: saying, “You entered the house of uncircumcised people and ate with them.”
text.DRB: Why didst thou enter in to men uncircumcised, and didst eat with them?

text.NRSV: Then Peter began to explain it to them, step by step, saying,
text.Rotherham: But Peter, making a beginning, went on to set forth the matter unto them in order, saying—
text.KJV: But Peter rehearsed the matter from the beginning, and expounded it by order unto them, saying,
text.NABRE: Peter began and explained it to them step by step, saying,
text.DRB: But Peter began and declared to them the order, saying:

text.NRSV: “I was in the city of Joppa praying, and in a trance I saw a vision. There was something like a large sheet coming down from heaven, being lowered by its four corners; and it came close to me.
text.Rotherham: I was in the city of Joppa praying, and saw in a trance, a vision:—coming down a sort of vessel, like a large sheet, by four corners, being let down out of heaven, and it came even unto me:
text.KJV: I was in the city of Joppa praying: and in a trance I saw a vision, A certain vessel descend, as it had been a great sheet, let down from heaven by four corners; and it came even to me:
text.NABRE: “I was at prayer in the city of Joppa when in a trance I had a vision, something resembling a large sheet coming down, lowered from the sky by its four corners, and it came to me.
text.DRB: I was in the city of Joppe praying, & I saw in an excess of mind a vision, a certain vessel descending as it were a great sheet with four corners let down from heaven, & it came even unto me.

text.NRSV: As I looked at it closely I saw four-footed animals, beasts of prey, reptiles, and birds of the air.
text.Rotherham: Into which steadfastly looking, began to observe, and saw the quadrupeds of the earth, and the wild beasts, and the creeping things, and the birds of heaven.
text.KJV: Upon the which when I had fastened mine eyes, I considered, and saw fourfooted beasts of the earth, and wild beasts, and creeping things, and fowls of the air.
text.NABRE: Looking intently into it, I observed and saw the four-legged animals of the earth, the wild beasts, the reptiles, and the birds of the sky.
text.DRB: Into which I looking considered, & saw four footed beasts of the earth, & cattle, & such as creep, & fowls of the air.

text.NRSV: I also heard a voice saying to me, ‘Get up, Peter; kill and eat.’
text.Rotherham: Moreover heard a voice also, saying unto me—Rise, Peter! slay and eat.
text.KJV: And I heard a voice saying unto me, Arise, Peter; slay and eat.
text.NABRE: I also heard a voice say to me, ‘Get up, Peter. Slaughter and eat.’
text.DRB: And I heard also a voice saying to me: Arise Peter, kill and eat.

text.NRSV: But I replied, ‘By no means, Lord; for nothing profane or unclean has ever entered my mouth.’
text.Rotherham: But I said—By no means, Lord, because, a common or unclean thing, hath at no time entered into my mouth.
text.KJV: But I said, Not so, Lord: for nothing common or unclean hath at any time entered into my mouth.
text.NABRE: But I said, ‘Certainly not, sir, because nothing profane or unclean has ever entered my mouth.’
text.DRB: And I said: Not so Lord; for common or unclean thing never entered into my mouth.

text.NRSV: But a second time the voice answered from heaven, ‘What God has made clean, you must not call profane.’
text.Rotherham: And a voice answered, a second time, out of heaven—What things, God, hath cleansed, be not, thou, making common.
text.KJV: But the voice answered me again from heaven, What God hath cleansed, that call not thou common.
text.NABRE: But a second time a voice from heaven answered, ‘What God has made clean, you are not to call profane.’
text.DRB: And a voice answered the second time from heaven: That which God hath made clean, do not thou call common.

text.NRSV: This happened three times; then everything was pulled up again to heaven.
text.Rotherham: And, this, took place thrice,—and the whole was drawn up again into heaven;
text.KJV: And this was done three times: and all were drawn up again into heaven.
text.NABRE: This happened three times, and then everything was drawn up again into the sky.
text.DRB: And this was done thrice: and all were taken up again into heaven.

text.NRSV: At that very moment three men, sent to me from Caesarea, arrived at the house where we were.
text.Rotherham: And lo! immediately, three men halted at the house wherein we were, sent from Caesarea unto me.
text.KJV: And, behold, immediately there were three men already come unto the house where I was, sent from Cesarea unto me.
text.NABRE: Just then three men appeared at the house where we were, who had been sent to me from Caesarea.
text.DRB: And behold, three men immediately were come to the house wherein I was, sent to me from Cæsarea.

text.NRSV: The Spirit told me to go with them and not to make a distinction between them and us. These six brothers also accompanied me, and we entered the man’s house.
text.Rotherham: And the Spirit bade me go with them, nothing doubting. And there went with me these six brethren also; and we entered into the house of the man,
text.KJV: And the spirit bade me go with them, nothing doubting. Moreover these six brethren accompanied me, and we entered into the man’s house:
text.NABRE: The Spirit told me to accompany them without discriminating. These six brothers also went with me, and we entered the man’s house.
text.DRB: And the spirit said to me, that I should go with them, doubting nothing. And there came with me these six brethren also: and we went into the man's house.

text.NRSV: He told us how he had seen the angel standing in his house and saying, ‘Send to Joppa and bring Simon, who is called Peter;
text.Rotherham: And he related to us how he had seen the messenger in his house, standing, and saying—Send away unto Joppa, and fetch Simon, who is surnamed Peter;
text.KJV: And he shewed us how he had seen an angel in his house, which stood and said unto him, Send men to Joppa, and call for Simon, whose surname is Peter;
text.NABRE: He related to us how he had seen [the] angel standing in his house, saying, ‘Send someone to Joppa and summon Simon, who is called Peter,
text.DRB: And he told us, how he had seen an Angel in his house, standing and saying to him: Send to Joppe, and call hither Simon, that is surnamed Peter,

text.NRSV: he will give you a message by which you and your entire household will be saved.’
text.Rotherham: Who shall speak words unto thee, whereby thou shall be saved, thou, and all thy house.
text.KJV: Who shall tell thee words, whereby thou and all thy house shall be saved.
text.NABRE: who will speak words to you by which you and all your household will be saved.’
text.DRB: who shall speak to thee words wherein thou shalt be saved and all thy house.

text.NRSV: And as I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell upon them just as it had upon us at the beginning.
text.Rotherham: And, as I began to be speaking, the Holy Spirit fell upon them, just as upon us also at the beginning.
text.KJV: And as I began to speak, the Holy Ghost fell on them, as on us at the beginning.
text.NABRE: As I began to speak, the holy Spirit fell upon them as it had upon us at the beginning,
text.DRB: And when I had begun to speak, the Holy Ghost fell upon them, as upon us also in the beginning.

text.NRSV: And I remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said, ‘John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.’
text.Rotherham: And I was put in mind of the word of the Lord, how he used to say—John, indeed, immersed with water; but, ye, shall be immersed in Holy Spirit.
text.KJV: Then remembered I the word of the Lord, how that he said, John indeed baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: and I remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said, ‘John baptized with water but you will be baptized with the holy Spirit.’
text.DRB: And I remembered the word of our Lord, according as he said: John indeed baptized with water, but you shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost.

text.NRSV: If then God gave them the same gift that he gave us when we believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, who was I that I could hinder God?”
text.Rotherham: If therefore the like free-gift God gave unto them, as even unto us, when we had believed upon the Lord Jesus Christ, who was, I, that could withstand God?
text.KJV: Forasmuch then as God gave them the like gift as he did unto us, who believed on the Lord Jesus Christ; what was I, that I could withstand God?
text.NABRE: If then God gave them the same gift he gave to us when we came to believe in the Lord Jesus Christ, who was I to be able to hinder God?”
text.DRB: If therefore God hath given them the same grace, as to us also that believed in our Lord Jesus Christ: who was I that might prohibit God?

text.NRSV: When they heard this, they were silenced. And they praised God, saying, “Then God has given even to the Gentiles the repentance that leads to life.”
text.Rotherham: And, having heard these things, they held their peace, and glorified God, saying—Hence, even unto the nations, God hath granted repentance unto life.
text.KJV: When they heard these things, they held their peace, and glorified God, saying, Then hath God also to the Gentiles granted repentance unto life.
text.NABRE: When they heard this, they stopped objecting and glorified God, saying, “God has then granted life-giving repentance to the Gentiles too.” The Church at Antioch.
text.DRB: Having heard these things, they held their peace: & glorified God, saying: God then to the Gentiles also hath given repentance unto life.

text.NRSV: Now those who were scattered because of the persecution that took place over Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, and they spoke the word to no one except Jews.
text.Rotherham: They, therefore, who had been scattered abroad by reason of the tribulation that took place on account of Stephen, passed through as far as Phoenice and Cyprus and Antioch, unto no one speaking the word, save alone unto Jews.
text.KJV: Now they which were scattered abroad upon the persecution that arose about Stephen travelled as far as Phenice, and Cyprus, and Antioch, preaching the word to none but unto the Jews only.
text.NABRE: Now those who had been scattered by the persecution that arose because of Stephen went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, preaching the word to no one but Jews.
text.DRB: And they truly that had been dispersed by the tribulation that was made under Steven, walked throughout unto Phoenice & Cypres & Antioch, speaking the word to none, but to the Jews only.

text.NRSV: But among them were some men of Cyprus and Cyrene who, on coming to Antioch, spoke to the Hellenists also, proclaiming the Lord Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And there were some from among them, Cyprians and Cyrenians, who, indeed, coming unto Antioch, began speaking, even unto the Grecian Jews, announcing the glad tidings as to the Lord Jesus;
text.KJV: And some of them were men of Cyprus and Cyrene, which, when they were come to Antioch, spake unto the Grecians, preaching the Lord Jesus.
text.NABRE: There were some Cypriots and Cyrenians among them, however, who came to Antioch and began to speak to the Greeks as well, proclaiming the Lord Jesus.
text.DRB: But certain of them were men of Cypres and Cyrene, who when they were entered into Antioch, spake to the Greeks, preaching our Lord Jesus.

text.NRSV: The hand of the Lord was with them, and a great number became believers and turned to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the hand of the Lord was with them, and a great number—they who believed—turned unto the Lord.
text.KJV: And the hand of the Lord was with them: and a great number believed, and turned unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: The hand of the Lord was with them and a great number who believed turned to the Lord.
text.DRB: And the hand of our Lord was with them: and a great number of believers was converted to our Lord.

text.NRSV: News of this came to the ears of the church in Jerusalem, and they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
text.Rotherham: And the matter was reported in the hearing of the assembly that was in Jerusalem, concerning them; and they sent forth Barnabas, as far as Antioch;
text.KJV: Then tidings of these things came unto the ears of the church which was in Jerusalem: and they sent forth Barnabas, that he should go as far as Antioch.
text.NABRE: The news about them reached the ears of the church in Jerusalem, and they sent Barnabas [to go] to Antioch.
text.DRB: And the report came to the ears of the Church that was at Jerusalem, touching these things: and they sent Barnabas as far as Antioch.

text.NRSV: When he came and saw the grace of God, he rejoiced, and he exhorted them all to remain faithful to the Lord with steadfast devotion;
text.Rotherham: Who, arriving, and seeing the favour which was of God, rejoiced, and went on to beseech all, with the purpose of their heart, to abide [in] the Lord;
text.KJV: Who, when he came, and had seen the grace of God, was glad, and exhorted them all, that with purpose of heart they would cleave unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: When he arrived and saw the grace of God, he rejoiced and encouraged them all to remain faithful to the Lord in firmness of heart,
text.DRB: Who when he was come, and saw the grace of God, rejoiced: and he exhorted all with purpose of heart to continue in our Lord:

text.NRSV: for he was a good man, full of the Holy Spirit and of faith. And a great many people were brought to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Because he was a good man, and full of Holy Spirit and faith; and a considerable multitude were added unto the Lord.
text.KJV: For he was a good man, and full of the Holy Ghost and of faith: and much people was added unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: for he was a good man, filled with the holy Spirit and faith. And a large number of people was added to the Lord.
text.DRB: because he was a good man, and full of the Holy Ghost and faith. And a great multitude was added to our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul,
text.Rotherham: He went away, however, unto Tarsus, to seek up Saul;
text.KJV: Then departed Barnabas to Tarsus, for to seek Saul:
text.NABRE: Then he went to Tarsus to look for Saul,
text.DRB: And he went forth to Tarsus, to seek Saul:

text.NRSV: and when he had found him, he brought him to Antioch. So it was that for an entire year they met with the church and taught a great many people, and it was in Antioch that the disciples were first called “Christians.”
text.Rotherham: And, finding him, he brought him unto Antioch. And so it was with them, that, for a whole year, they were brought together in the assembly, and taught a considerable multitude; also that the disciples, first in Antioch, were called, Christians.
text.KJV: And when he had found him, he brought him unto Antioch. And it came to pass, that a whole year they assembled themselves with the church, and taught much people. And the disciples were called Christians first in Antioch.
text.NABRE: and when he had found him he brought him to Antioch. For a whole year they met with the church and taught a large number of people, and it was in Antioch that the disciples were first called Christians. The Prediction of Agabus.
text.DRB: whom when he had found, he brought him to Antioch. And they conversed there in the church a whole year: and they taught a great multitude, so that the Disciples were at Antioch first named Christians.

text.NRSV: At that time prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
text.Rotherham: Now, in these days, there came down from Jerusalem, prophets, unto Antioch.
text.KJV: And in these days came prophets from Jerusalem unto Antioch.
text.NABRE: At that time some prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch,
text.DRB: And in these days there came Prophets from Jerusalem to Antioch,

text.NRSV: One of them named Agabus stood up and predicted by the Spirit that there would be a severe famine over all the world; and this took place during the reign of Claudius.
text.Rotherham: And one from among them, by name Agabus, rising up, gave a sign, through means of the Spirit, that, a great famine, was coming over all the inhabited earth; which, indeed, came to pass under Claudius.
text.KJV: And there stood up one of them named Agabus, and signified by the spirit that there should be great dearth throughout all the world: which came to pass in the days of Claudius Cesar.
text.NABRE: and one of them named Agabus stood up and predicted by the Spirit that there would be a severe famine all over the world, and it happened under Claudius.
text.DRB: and one of them rising, named Agabus, did by the Spirit signify a great famine that should be in the whole world, which fell under Claudius.

text.NRSV: The disciples determined that according to their ability, each would send relief to the believers living in Judea;
text.Rotherham: And, according as any one of the disciples was being prospered, they each one of them set apart [something] for ministering, to send unto the brethren, who dwelt in Jerusalem;—
text.KJV: Then the disciples, every man according to his ability, determined to send relief unto the brethren which dwelt in Judea:
text.NABRE: So the disciples determined that, according to ability, each should send relief to the brothers who lived in Judea.
text.DRB: And the Disciples according as each man had, purposed every one to send, for to serve the brethren that dwelt in Jewry:

text.NRSV: this they did, sending it to the elders by Barnabas and Saul.
text.Rotherham: Which thing they also did, sending it unto the Elders, through the hand of Barnabas and Saul.
text.KJV: Which also they did, and sent it to the elders by the hands of Barnabas and Saul.
text.NABRE: This they did, sending it to the presbyters in care of Barnabas and Saul.
text.DRB: which also they did, sending to the ancients by the hands of Barnabas and Saul.

text.NRSV: About that time King Herod laid violent hands upon some who belonged to the church.
text.Rotherham: Now, in the course of that season, Herod the king thrust forth his hands to harm some of them of the assembly,—
text.KJV: Now about that time Herod the king stretched forth his hands to vex certain of the church.
text.NABRE: About that time King Herod laid hands upon some members of the church to harm them.
text.DRB: And at the same time Herod the king set his hands, to afflict certain of the Church.

text.NRSV: He had James, the brother of John, killed with the sword.
text.Rotherham: And slew James the brother of John with a sword;
text.KJV: And he killed James the brother of John with the sword.
text.NABRE: He had James, the brother of John, killed by the sword,
text.DRB: And he killed James the brother of John with the sword.

text.NRSV: After he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to arrest Peter also. (This was during the festival of Unleavened Bread.)
text.Rotherham: And, seeing that it was acceptable unto the Jews, he went on to apprehend Peter also (now they were the days of unleavened bread),—
text.KJV: And because he saw it pleased the Jews, he proceeded further to take Peter also. (Then were the days of unleavened bread.)
text.NABRE: and when he saw that this was pleasing to the Jews he proceeded to arrest Peter also. (It was [the] feast of Unleavened Bread.)
text.DRB: And seeing that it pleased the Jews, he added to apprehend Peter also. And it was the days of the Azymes.

text.NRSV: When he had seized him, he put him in prison and handed him over to four squads of soldiers to guard him, intending to bring him out to the people after the Passover.
text.Rotherham: Whom also having seized, he put into prison, delivering him up unto four quaternions of soldiers, to be guarding him,—intending, after the passover, to bring him up unto the people.
text.KJV: And when he had apprehended him, he put him in prison, and delivered him to four quaternions of soldiers to keep him; intending after Easter to bring him forth to the people.
text.NABRE: He had him taken into custody and put in prison under the guard of four squads of four soldiers each. He intended to bring him before the people after Passover.
text.DRB: Whom when he had apprehended, he cast into prison, delivering him to four quaternions of soldiers to be kept, meaning after the Pasch to bring him forth to the people.

text.NRSV: While Peter was kept in prison, the church prayed fervently to God for him.
text.Rotherham: Peter, therefore, was kept in the prison; but, prayer, was, earnestly, being made by the assembly, unto God, concerning him.
text.KJV: Peter therefore was kept in prison: but prayer was made without ceasing of the church unto God for him.
text.NABRE: Peter thus was being kept in prison, but prayer by the church was fervently being made to God on his behalf.
text.DRB: And Peter indeed was kept in prison. But prayer was made of the Church without intermission unto God for him.

text.NRSV: The very night before Herod was going to bring him out, Peter, bound with two chains, was sleeping between two soldiers, while guards in front of the door were keeping watch over the prison.
text.Rotherham: And, when Herod was about to bring him forth, on that night, was Peter sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, guards, also, before the door, were keeping the prison.
text.KJV: And when Herod would have brought him forth, the same night Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains: and the keepers before the door kept the prison.
text.NABRE: On the very night before Herod was to bring him to trial, Peter, secured by double chains, was sleeping between two soldiers, while outside the door guards kept watch on the prison.
text.DRB: And when Herod would have brought him forth, the same night Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains: and the keepers before the door kept the prison.

text.NRSV: Suddenly an angel of the Lord appeared and a light shone in the cell. He tapped Peter on the side and woke him, saying, “Get up quickly.” And the chains fell off his wrists.
text.Rotherham: And lo! a messenger of the Lord stood over him, and a light shone in the cell; and, smiting the side of Peter, he roused him up, saying—Rise up quickly! And his chains fell off out of his hands.
text.KJV: And, behold, the angel of the Lord came upon him, and a light shined in the prison: and he smote Peter on the side, and raised him up, saying, Arise up quickly. And his chains fell off from his hands.
text.NABRE: Suddenly the angel of the Lord stood by him and a light shone in the cell. He tapped Peter on the side and awakened him, saying, “Get up quickly.” The chains fell from his wrists.
text.DRB: And behold an Angel of our Lord stood in presence: and light shined in the house: and striking Peter's side, he raised him, saying: Arise quickly. And the chains fell from his hands.

text.NRSV: The angel said to him, “Fasten your belt and put on your sandals.” He did so. Then he said to him, “Wrap your cloak around you and follow me.”
text.Rotherham: And the messenger said unto him—Gird thyself, and bind on thy sandals. And he did so. And he saith unto him—Throw around thee thy mantle, and be following me.
text.KJV: And the angel said unto him, Gird thyself, and bind on thy sandals. And so he did. And he saith unto him, Cast thy garment about thee, and follow me.
text.NABRE: The angel said to him, “Put on your belt and your sandals.” He did so. Then he said to him, “Put on your cloak and follow me.”
text.DRB: And the Angel said to him: Gird thee, and put on thy shoes. And he did so. And he said to him: Put thy garment about thee, & follow me.

text.NRSV: Peter went out and followed him; he did not realize that what was happening with the angel’s help was real; he thought he was seeing a vision.
text.Rotherham: And, coming out, he began following, and knew not that it was true which was coming about through means of the messenger; but supposed that, a vision, he was beholding.
text.KJV: And he went out, and followed him; and wist not that it was true which was done by the angel; but thought he saw a vision.
text.NABRE: So he followed him out, not realizing that what was happening through the angel was real; he thought he was seeing a vision.
text.DRB: And going forth he followed him, & he knew not that it was true which was done by the Angel: but he thought that he saw a vision.

text.NRSV: After they had passed the first and the second guard, they came before the iron gate leading into the city. It opened for them of its own accord, and they went outside and walked along a lane, when suddenly the angel left him.
text.Rotherham: And, passing through the first ward and the second, they came unto the iron gate that leadeth into the city, the which, of its own accord, opened unto them; and, coming out, they went on through one street, and, straightway, the messenger was parted from him.
text.KJV: When they were past the first and the second ward, they came unto the iron gate that leadeth unto the city; which opened to them of his own accord: and they went out, and passed on through one street; and forthwith the angel departed from him.
text.NABRE: They passed the first guard, then the second, and came to the iron gate leading out to the city, which opened for them by itself. They emerged and made their way down an alley, and suddenly the angel left him.
text.DRB: And passing through the first & the second watch, they came to the iron gate that leadeth to the city, which of itself opened to them. And going out, they went forward one street: and incontinent the Angel departed from him.

text.NRSV: Then Peter came to himself and said, “Now I am sure that the Lord has sent his angel and rescued me from the hands of Herod and from all that the Jewish people were expecting.”
text.Rotherham: And, Peter, coming to himself said—Now, know I, of a truth, that the Lord hath sent forth his messenger, and taken me out of the hand of Herod, and all the expectation of the people of the Jews.
text.KJV: And when Peter was come to himself, he said, Now I know of a surety, that the Lord hath sent his angel, and hath delivered me out of the hand of Herod, and from all the expectation of the people of the Jews.
text.NABRE: Then Peter recovered his senses and said, “Now I know for certain that [the] Lord sent his angel and rescued me from the hand of Herod and from all that the Jewish people had been expecting.”
text.DRB: And Peter returning to himself, said: Now I know in very deed that our Lord hath sent his Angel, and delivered me out of Herod's hand, & from all the expectation of the people of the Jews.

text.NRSV: As soon as he realized this, he went to the house of Mary, the mother of John whose other name was Mark, where many had gathered and were praying.
text.Rotherham: And, considering the matter, he came unto the house of Mary, the mother of John who was surnamed Mark, where a considerable number were gathered together and praying.
text.KJV: And when he had considered the thing, he came to the house of Mary the mother of John, whose surname was Mark; where many were gathered together praying.
text.NABRE: When he realized this, he went to the house of Mary, the mother of John who is called Mark, where there were many people gathered in prayer.
text.DRB: And considering, he came to the house of Mary the mother of John, who was surnamed Mark, where many were gathered and praying.

text.NRSV: When he knocked at the outer gate, a maid named Rhoda came to answer.
text.Rotherham: And, when he had knocked at the door of the porch, there came unto it a maiden to hearken, by name Rhoda;
text.KJV: And as Peter knocked at the door of the gate, a damsel came to hearken, named Rhoda.
text.NABRE: When he knocked on the gateway door, a maid named Rhoda came to answer it.
text.DRB: And when he knocked at the door of the gate, there came forth a wench to see, named Rhode.

text.NRSV: On recognizing Peter’s voice, she was so overjoyed that, instead of opening the gate, she ran in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
text.Rotherham: And, recognizing the voice of Peter, by reason of her joy, she opened not the porch,—but, running in, bare tidings that Peter was standing before the porch. But, they, unto her, said—Thou art raving
text.KJV: And when she knew Peter’s voice, she opened not the gate for gladness, but ran in, and told how Peter stood before the gate.
text.NABRE: She was so overjoyed when she recognized Peter’s voice that, instead of opening the gate, she ran in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
text.DRB: And as she knew Peter's voice, for joy she opened not the gate, but running in she told that Peter stood before the gate.

text.NRSV: They said to her, “You are out of your mind!” But she insisted that it was so. They said, “It is his angel.”
text.Rotherham: She, however, kept on strongly declaring that, so, it was. But, they, were saying—It is his, messenger.
text.KJV: And they said unto her, Thou art mad. But she constantly affirmed that it was even so. Then said they, It is his angel.
text.NABRE: They told her, “You are out of your mind,” but she insisted that it was so. But they kept saying, “It is his angel.”
text.DRB: But they said to her: Thou art mad. But she affirmed that it was so. But they said: It is his Angel.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile Peter continued knocking; and when they opened the gate, they saw him and were amazed.
text.Rotherham: And, Peter, continued knocking; and, opening, they saw him, and were amazed.
text.KJV: But Peter continued knocking: and when they had opened the door, and saw him, they were astonished.
text.NABRE: But Peter continued to knock, and when they opened it, they saw him and were astounded.
text.DRB: And Peter continued knocking. And when they had opened, they saw him, & were astonished.

text.NRSV: He motioned to them with his hand to be silent, and described for them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. And he added, “Tell this to James and to the believers.” Then he left and went to another place.
text.Rotherham: But, making a sign to them with his hand to hold their peace, he related to them how, the Lord, had brought, him, forth out of the prison; and he said—Carry tidings unto James and the brethren, as to these things. And, going out, he went his way unto some other place.
text.KJV: But he, beckoning unto them with the hand to hold their peace, declared unto them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. And he said, Go shew these things unto James, and to the brethren. And he departed, and went into another place.
text.NABRE: He motioned to them with his hand to be quiet and explained [to them] how the Lord had led him out of the prison, and said, “Report this to James and the brothers.” Then he left and went to another place.
text.DRB: And beckoning with his hand to them, that they should hold their peace, he told how our Lord had brought him out of prison, & he said: Tell these things to James & to the brethren. And going forth he went into another place.

text.NRSV: When morning came, there was no small commotion among the soldiers over what had become of Peter.
text.Rotherham: And, when it became day, there was no small commotion among the soldiers, as to what, then, Peter had become!
text.KJV: Now as soon as it was day, there was no small stir among the soldiers, what was become of Peter.
text.NABRE: At daybreak there was no small commotion among the soldiers over what had become of Peter.
text.DRB: And when day was come, there was no little ado between the soldiers, what was become of Peter.

text.NRSV: When Herod had searched for him and could not find him, he examined the guards and ordered them to be put to death. Then he went down from Judea to Caesarea and stayed there.
text.Rotherham: And, Herod, seeking after and not finding, him, having examined the guards, ordered them to be led away [to death]; and, going down from Judaea unto Caesarea, stayed there.
text.KJV: And when Herod had sought for him, and found him not, he examined the keepers, and commanded that they should be put to death. And he went down from Judea to Cesarea, and there abode.
text.NABRE: Herod, after instituting a search but not finding him, ordered the guards tried and executed. Then he left Judea to spend some time in Caesarea.
text.DRB: And Herod, when he had sought him, and had not found, making inquisition of the keepers, commanded them to be led away: & going down from Jewry into Cæsarea, there he abode.

text.NRSV: Now Herod was angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon. So they came to him in a body; and after winning over Blastus, the king’s chamberlain, they asked for a reconciliation, because their country depended on the king’s country for food.
text.Rotherham: Now he was bitterly hostile to them of Tyre and Zidon; but, with one accord, they came unto him, and, persuading Blastus, who was over the bed-chamber of the king, they were suing for peace; because their country was fed by the king’s,
text.KJV: And Herod was highly displeased with them of Tyre and Sidon: but they came with one accord to him, and, having made Blastus the king’s chamberlain their friend, desired peace; because their country was nourished by the king’s country.
text.NABRE: Herod’s Death. He had long been very angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon, who now came to him in a body. After winning over Blastus, the king’s chamberlain, they sued for peace because their country was supplied with food from the king’s territory.
text.DRB: And he was angry with the Tyrians and the Sidonians. But they with one accord came to him, and persuading Blastus that was chief of the king's chamber, they desired peace, for that their countries were nourished by him.

text.NRSV: On an appointed day Herod put on his royal robes, took his seat on the platform, and delivered a public address to them.
text.Rotherham: And, on an appointed day, Herod, putting on royal apparel, and seating himself upon the tribunal, proceeded to deliver an oration unto them.
text.KJV: And upon a set day Herod, arrayed in royal apparel, sat upon his throne, and made an oration unto them.
text.NABRE: On an appointed day, Herod, attired in royal robes, [and] seated on the rostrum, addressed them publicly.
text.DRB: And upon a day appointed, Herod being arrayed with kingly attire, sat in the judgement seat, and made an oration to them.

text.NRSV: The people kept shouting, “The voice of a god, and not of a mortal!”
text.Rotherham: And, the populace, began to shout—A god’s, voice, and not, a man’s!
text.KJV: And the people gave a shout, saying, It is the voice of a god, and not of a man.
text.NABRE: The assembled crowd cried out, “This is the voice of a god, not of a man.”
text.DRB: And the people made acclamation, The voices of a God, & not of a man.

text.NRSV: And immediately, because he had not given the glory to God, an angel of the Lord struck him down, and he was eaten by worms and died.
text.Rotherham: And, instantly, there smote him, a messenger of the Lord, because he gave not the glory unto God; and, becoming worm-eaten, he expired.
text.KJV: And immediately the angel of the Lord smote him, because he gave not God the glory: and he was eaten of worms, and gave up the ghost.
text.NABRE: At once the angel of the Lord struck him down because he did not ascribe the honor to God, and he was eaten by worms and breathed his last.
text.DRB: And forthwith an Angel of our Lord strook him, because he had not given the honour to God: and being consumed of worms, he gave up the ghost.

text.NRSV: But the word of God continued to advance and gain adherents.
text.Rotherham: And, the word of the Lord, went on growing and multiplying.
text.KJV: But the word of God grew and multiplied.
text.NABRE: But the word of God continued to spread and grow.
text.DRB: But the word of our Lord increased and multiplied.

text.NRSV: Then after completing their mission Barnabas and Saul returned to Jerusalem and brought with them John, whose other name was Mark.
text.Rotherham: And, Barnabas and Saul, returned unto Jerusalem, fulfilling the ministering, taking with them John who was surnamed Mark.
text.KJV: And Barnabas and Saul returned from Jerusalem, when they had fulfilled their ministry, and took with them John, whose surname was Mark.
text.NABRE: Mission of Barnabas and Saul. After Barnabas and Saul completed their relief mission, they returned to Jerusalem, taking with them John, who is called Mark.
text.DRB: And Barnabas and Saul returned from Jerusalem, having accomplished their ministry, taking with them John that was surnamed Mark.

text.NRSV: Now in the church at Antioch there were prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon who was called Niger, Lucius of Cyrene, Manaen a member of the court of Herod the ruler, and Saul.
text.Rotherham: Now there were in Antioch, distributed through the existing assembly,—prophets and teachers: both Barnabas and Symeon who was called Niger, and Lucius the Cyrenian, Manaen also, Herod the tetrarch’s foster-brother, and Saul.
text.KJV: Now there were in the church that was at Antioch certain prophets and teachers; as Barnabas, and Simeon that was called Niger, and Lucius of Cyrene, and Manaen, which had been brought up with Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.
text.NABRE: Now there were in the church at Antioch prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Symeon who was called Niger, Lucius of Cyrene, Manaen who was a close friend of Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.
text.DRB: And there were in the Church which was at Antioch, Prophets and Doctors, among whom was Barnabas, and Simon that was called Niger, and Lucius of Cyrene, and Manahen who was the foster-brother of Herod the Tetrarch, and Saul.

text.NRSV: While they were worshiping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, “Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.”
text.Rotherham: And, as they were publicly ministering unto the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said—Separate forthwith unto me, Barnabas and Saul, unto the work whereunto I have called them.
text.KJV: As they ministered to the Lord, and fasted, the Holy Ghost said, Separate me Barnabas and Saul for the work whereunto I have called them.
text.NABRE: While they were worshiping the Lord and fasting, the holy Spirit said, “Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.”
text.DRB: And as they were ministering to our Lord, & fasting, the Holy Ghost said: Separate me Saul and Barnabas unto the work, whereto I have taken them.

text.NRSV: Then after fasting and praying they laid their hands on them and sent them off.
text.Rotherham: Then, fasting and praying, and laying their hands upon them, they sent them away.
text.KJV: And when they had fasted and prayed, and laid their hands on them, they sent them away.
text.NABRE: Then, completing their fasting and prayer, they laid hands on them and sent them off.
text.DRB: Then they fasting and praying, and imposing hands upon them, dismissed them.

text.NRSV: So, being sent out by the Holy Spirit, they went down to Seleucia; and from there they sailed to Cyprus.
text.Rotherham: They, therefore, being sent forth by the Holy Spirit, went down unto Seleucia, and, from thence, sailed away unto Cyprus;
text.KJV: So they, being sent forth by the Holy Ghost, departed unto Seleucia; and from thence they sailed to Cyprus.
text.NABRE: First Mission Begins in Cyprus. So they, sent forth by the holy Spirit, went down to Seleucia and from there sailed to Cyprus.
text.DRB: And they being sent of the Holy Ghost, went to Seleucia, and thence sailed to Cypres.

text.NRSV: When they arrived at Salamis, they proclaimed the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews. And they had John also to assist them.
text.Rotherham: And, coming to be in Salamis, they declared the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews;—and they had, John also, as an attendant.
text.KJV: And when they were at Salamis, they preached the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews: and they had also John to their minister.
text.NABRE: When they arrived in Salamis, they proclaimed the word of God in the Jewish synagogues. They had John also as their assistant.
text.DRB: And when they were come to Salamina, they preached the word of God in the Synagogues of the Jews. And they had John also in their ministry.

text.NRSV: When they had gone through the whole island as far as Paphos, they met a certain magician, a Jewish false prophet, named Bar-Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, passing through the whole island, as far as Paphos, they found a certain man, a magician, a false-prophet, a Jew, whose name was Bar-jesus;
text.KJV: And when they had gone through the isle unto Paphos, they found a certain sorcerer, a false prophet, a Jew, whose name was Bar–jesus:
text.NABRE: When they had traveled through the whole island as far as Paphos, they met a magician named Bar-Jesus who was a Jewish false prophet.
text.DRB: And when they had walked throughout the whole island as far as Paphos, they found a certain man that was a magician, a false-prophet, a Jew, whose name was Bar-iesu,

text.NRSV: He was with the proconsul, Sergius Paulus, an intelligent man, who summoned Barnabas and Saul and wanted to hear the word of God.
text.Rotherham: Who was with the proconsul, Sergius Paulus, an intelligent man. The same, calling for Barnabas and Saul, sought to hear the word of God.
text.KJV: Which was with the deputy of the country, Sergius Paulus, a prudent man; who called for Barnabas and Saul, and desired to hear the word of God.
text.NABRE: He was with the proconsul Sergius Paulus, a man of intelligence, who had summoned Barnabas and Saul and wanted to hear the word of God.
text.DRB: who was with the Proconsul Sergius Paulus a wise man. He sending for Barnabas & Saul, desired to hear the word of God.

text.NRSV: But the magician Elymas (for that is the translation of his name) opposed them and tried to turn the proconsul away from the faith.
text.Rotherham: But Elymas the magician,—for so, when translated, is his name,—withstood them; seeking to turn aside the proconsul from the faith.
text.KJV: But Elymas the sorcerer (for so is his name by interpretation) withstood them, seeking to turn away the deputy from the faith.
text.NABRE: But Elymas the magician (for that is what his name means) opposed them in an attempt to turn the proconsul away from the faith.
text.DRB: But Elymas the magician (for so is his name interpreted) resisted them, seeking to avert the Proconsul from the faith.

text.NRSV: But Saul, also known as Paul, filled with the Holy Spirit, looked intently at him
text.Rotherham: But Saul, who is also Paul, filled with Holy Spirit, looking steadfastly at him,
text.KJV: Then Saul, (who also is called Paul,) filled with the Holy Ghost, set his eyes on him,
text.NABRE: But Saul, also known as Paul, filled with the holy Spirit, looked intently at him
text.DRB: But Saul, otherwise Paul, replenished with the Holy Ghost, looking upon him,

text.NRSV: and said, “You son of the devil, you enemy of all righteousness, full of all deceit and villainy, will you not stop making crooked the straight paths of the Lord?
text.Rotherham: Said—O full of all guile, and all recklessness! Son of an adversary! Enemy of all righteousness!—Wilt thou not cease to pervert the straight ways of the Lord?
text.KJV: And said, O full of all subtilty and all mischief, thou child of the devil, thou enemy of all righteousness, wilt thou not cease to pervert the right ways of the Lord?
text.NABRE: and said, “You son of the devil, you enemy of all that is right, full of every sort of deceit and fraud. Will you not stop twisting the straight paths of [the] Lord?
text.DRB: said: O full of all guile, and all deceit, son of the devil, enemy of all justice, thou ceasest not to subvert the right ways of our Lord.

text.NRSV: And now listen—the hand of the Lord is against you, and you will be blind for a while, unable to see the sun.” Immediately mist and darkness came over him, and he went about groping for someone to lead him by the hand.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, lo! the hand of the Lord is upon thee, and thou shalt be blind, not seeing the sun, until a fitting time. And, instantly, there fell upon him a mist and darkness; and, going about, he was seeking such as might lead him by the hand.
text.KJV: And now, behold, the hand of the Lord is upon thee, and thou shalt be blind, not seeing the sun for a season. And immediately there fell on him a mist and a darkness; and he went about seeking some to lead him by the hand.
text.NABRE: Even now the hand of the Lord is upon you. You will be blind, and unable to see the sun for a time.” Immediately a dark mist fell upon him, and he went about seeking people to lead him by the hand.
text.DRB: And now behold the hand of our Lord upon thee, & thou shalt be blind, not seeing the sun until a time. And forthwith there fell dimness and darkness upon him, and going about he sought somebody that would give him his hand.

text.NRSV: When the proconsul saw what had happened, he believed, for he was astonished at the teaching about the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then, the proconsul, seeing what had happened, believed, being amazed at the teaching of the Lord.
text.KJV: Then the deputy, when he saw what was done, believed, being astonished at the doctrine of the Lord.
text.NABRE: When the proconsul saw what had happened, he came to believe, for he was astonished by the teaching about the Lord.
text.DRB: Then the Proconsul, when he had seen that which was done, believed, marveling at the doctrine of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Paul and his companions set sail from Paphos and came to Perga in Pamphylia. John, however, left them and returned to Jerusalem;
text.Rotherham: And, setting sail from Paphos, Paul’s company came into Perga of Pamphylia; but, John, withdrawing from them, returned unto Jerusalem.
text.KJV: Now when Paul and his company loosed from Paphos, they came to Perga in Pamphylia: and John departing from them returned to Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: Paul’s Arrival at Antioch in Pisidia. From Paphos, Paul and his companions set sail and arrived at Perga in Pamphylia. But John left them and returned to Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And when Paul and they that were with him had sailed from Paphos, they came to Perge in Pamphylia. And John departing from them, returned to Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: but they went on from Perga and came to Antioch in Pisidia. And on the sabbath day they went into the synagogue and sat down.
text.Rotherham: They, however, passing through from Perga, arrived at Antioch of Pisidia; and, going into the synagogue on the sabbath-day, sat down.
text.KJV: But when they departed from Perga, they came to Antioch in Pisidia, and went into the synagogue on the sabbath day, and sat down.
text.NABRE: They continued on from Perga and reached Antioch in Pisidia. On the sabbath they entered [into] the synagogue and took their seats.
text.DRB: But they passing through Perge, came to Antioch in Pisidia: and entering into the Synagogue on the day of the Sabboth, they sat down.

text.NRSV: After the reading of the law and the prophets, the officials of the synagogue sent them a message, saying, “Brothers, if you have any word of exhortation for the people, give it.”
text.Rotherham: And, after the reading of the law and the prophets, the synagogue-rulers sent unto them, saying—Brethren! if there is in you a word of exhortation unto the people, say on.
text.KJV: And after the reading of the law and the prophets the rulers of the synagogue sent unto them, saying, Ye men and brethren, if ye have any word of exhortation for the people, say on.
text.NABRE: After the reading of the law and the prophets, the synagogue officials sent word to them, “My brothers, if one of you has a word of exhortation for the people, please speak.”
text.DRB: And after the lesson of the Law and the Prophets, the princes of the Synagogue sent to them, saying: Men brethren, if there be among you any sermon of exhortation to the people, speak.

text.NRSV: So Paul stood up and with a gesture began to speak: “You Israelites, and others who fear God, listen.
text.Rotherham: And Paul, standing up, and making a sign with his hand, said—Ye men of Israel! and such as revere God! hearken:—
text.KJV: Then Paul stood up, and beckoning with his hand said, Men of Israel, and ye that fear God, give audience.
text.NABRE: Paul’s Address in the Synagogue. So Paul got up, motioned with his hand, and said, “Fellow Israelites and you others who are God-fearing, listen.
text.DRB: And Paul rising up, and with his hand beckoning for silence, said: Ye men of Israel, and you that fear God, harken.

text.NRSV: The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors and made the people great during their stay in the land of Egypt, and with uplifted arm he led them out of it.
text.Rotherham: The God of this people Israel, chose our fathers, and, the people, he exalted, by their sojourn in the land of Egypt,—and, with a high arm, brought he them out of it;
text.KJV: The God of this people of Israel chose our fathers, and exalted the people when they dwelt as strangers in the land of Egypt, and with an high arm brought he them out of it.
text.NABRE: The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors and exalted the people during their sojourn in the land of Egypt. With uplifted arm he led them out of it
text.DRB: The God of the people of Israel chose our fathers, and exalted the people when they were sojourners in the land of Ægypt, and in a mighty arm brought them out thereof,

text.NRSV: For about forty years he put up with them in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: And, for the time of about forty years, bare with their manners in the desert;
text.KJV: And about the time of forty years suffered he their manners in the wilderness.
text.NABRE: and for about forty years he put up with them in the desert.
text.DRB: and for the space of forty years tolerated their manners in the desert.

text.NRSV: After he had destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan, he gave them their land as an inheritance
text.Rotherham: And, overthrowing seven nations in the land of Canaan, gave them their land as an inheritance—about four hundred and fifty years.
text.KJV: And when he had destroyed seven nations in the land of Chanaan, he divided their land to them by lot.
text.NABRE: When he had destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan, he gave them their land as an inheritance
text.DRB: And destroying seven nations in the land of Chanaan, by lot he divided their land among them,

text.NRSV: for about four hundred fifty years. After that he gave them judges until the time of the prophet Samuel.
text.Rotherham: And, after these things, he gave them judges, until Samuel the prophet.
text.KJV: And after that he gave unto them judges about the space of four hundred and fifty years, until Samuel the prophet.
text.NABRE: at the end of about four hundred and fifty years. After these things he provided judges up to Samuel [the] prophet.
text.DRB: as it were after four hundred and fifty years, and after these things he gave Judges, until Samuel the Prophet.

text.NRSV: Then they asked for a king; and God gave them Saul son of Kish, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, who reigned for forty years.
text.Rotherham: And, from that time, they asked for themselves a king, and God gave them Saul, son of Kish, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, during forty years;
text.KJV: And afterward they desired a king: and God gave unto them Saul the son of Cis, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, by the space of forty years.
text.NABRE: Then they asked for a king. God gave them Saul, son of Kish, a man from the tribe of Benjamin, for forty years.
text.DRB: And thenceforth they desired a king: & he gave them Saul the son of Cis, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, forty years.

text.NRSV: When he had removed him, he made David their king. In his testimony about him he said, ‘I have found David, son of Jesse, to be a man after my heart, who will carry out all my wishes.’
text.Rotherham: And, setting him aside, raised up, David, unto them for king,—of whom he also said, bearing witness—I have found David, the son of Jesse,— [a man] according to my heart, who will do all my will.
text.KJV: And when he had removed him, he raised up unto them David to be their king; to whom also he gave testimony, and said, I have found David the son of Jesse, a man after mine own heart, which shall fulfil all my will.
text.NABRE: Then he removed him and raised up David as their king; of him he testified, ‘I have found David, son of Jesse, a man after my own heart; he will carry out my every wish.’
text.DRB: and removing him, he raised them up David to be king: to whom giving testimony, he said: I have found David the son of Jesse, a man according to my heart, who shall do all my wills.

text.NRSV: Of this man’s posterity God has brought to Israel a Savior, Jesus, as he promised;
text.Rotherham: From this man’s seed, hath God, according to promise, brought unto Israel, a saviour—Jesus:
text.KJV: Of this man’s seed hath God according to his promise raised unto Israel a Saviour, Jesus:
text.NABRE: From this man’s descendants God, according to his promise, has brought to Israel a savior, Jesus.
text.DRB: Of his seed God according to his promise hath brought forth to Israel a Saviour Jesus,

text.NRSV: before his coming John had already proclaimed a baptism of repentance to all the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: John, beforehand proclaiming, before the face of his coming in, an immersion of repentance, unto all the people of Israel.
text.KJV: When John had first preached before his coming the baptism of repentance to all the people of Israel.
text.NABRE: John heralded his coming by proclaiming a baptism of repentance to all the people of Israel;
text.DRB: John preaching before the face of his coming, baptism of penance to all the people of Israel.

text.NRSV: And as John was finishing his work, he said, ‘What do you suppose that I am? I am not he. No, but one is coming after me; I am not worthy to untie the thong of the sandals on his feet.’
text.Rotherham: And, as John was fulfilling his course, he was saying—Whom are ye supposing that I am? I, am not he! But lo! there cometh, after me, one of whom I am not worthy, the sandals of his feet, to loosen.
text.KJV: And as John fulfilled his course, he said, Whom think ye that I am? I am not he. But, behold, there cometh one after me, whose shoes of his feet I am not worthy to loose.
text.NABRE: and as John was completing his course, he would say, ‘What do you suppose that I am? I am not he. Behold, one is coming after me; I am not worthy to unfasten the sandals of his feet.’
text.DRB: And when John fulfilled his course, he said: Whom do you think me to be? I am not he: but behold there cometh after me, whose shoes of his feet I am not worthy to loose.

text.NRSV: “My brothers, you descendants of Abraham’s family, and others who fear God, to us the message of this salvation has been sent.
text.Rotherham: Brethren! sons of the race of Abraham, and those who among you revere God,—unto you, hath this word of salvation been sent forth.
text.KJV: Men and brethren, children of the stock of Abraham, and whosoever among you feareth God, to you is the word of this salvation sent.
text.NABRE: “My brothers, children of the family of Abraham, and those others among you who are God-fearing, to us this word of salvation has been sent.
text.DRB: Men brethren, children of the stock of Abraham, & they among you that fear God; to you the word of this salvation was sent.

text.NRSV: Because the residents of Jerusalem and their leaders did not recognize him or understand the words of the prophets that are read every sabbath, they fulfilled those words by condemning him.
text.Rotherham: For, they who were dwelling in Jerusalem, and their rulers, not recognising him, have, by judging him, fulfilled, the very voices of the prophets which every sabbath are being read;
text.KJV: For they that dwell at Jerusalem, and their rulers, because they knew him not, nor yet the voices of the prophets which are read every sabbath day, they have fulfilled them in condemning him.
text.NABRE: The inhabitants of Jerusalem and their leaders failed to recognize him, and by condemning him they fulfilled the oracles of the prophets that are read sabbath after sabbath.
text.DRB: For they that inhabited Jerusalem, and the princes thereof, not knowing him, nor the voices of the prophets that are read every Sabboth, judging have fulfilled them,

text.NRSV: Even though they found no cause for a sentence of death, they asked Pilate to have him killed.
text.Rotherham: And, though no single cause of death they found, yet claimed they of Pilate that he should be slain.
text.KJV: And though they found no cause of death in him, yet desired they Pilate that he should be slain.
