text.NABRE: For even though they found no grounds for a death sentence, they asked Pilate to have him put to death,
text.DRB: and finding no cause of death in him, desired of Pilate, that they might kill him.

text.NRSV: When they had carried out everything that was written about him, they took him down from the tree and laid him in a tomb.
text.Rotherham: And, when they had finished all those things which, concerning him, had been written, taking him down from the tree, they put him in a tomb.
text.KJV: And when they had fulfilled all that was written of him, they took him down from the tree, and laid him in a sepulchre.
text.NABRE: and when they had accomplished all that was written about him, they took him down from the tree and placed him in a tomb.
text.DRB: And when they had consummated all things that were written of him, taking him down from the tree, they put him in a monument.

text.NRSV: But God raised him from the dead;
text.Rotherham: But, God, raised him from among the dead:
text.KJV: But God raised him from the dead:
text.NABRE: But God raised him from the dead,
text.DRB: But God raised him up from the dead the third day:

text.NRSV: and for many days he appeared to those who came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, and they are now his witnesses to the people.
text.Rotherham: Who appeared, during many days, unto them who had come up with him from Galilee unto Jerusalem; who, indeed, [now] are his witnesses unto the people.
text.KJV: And he was seen many days of them which came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who are his witnesses unto the people.
text.NABRE: and for many days he appeared to those who had come up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. These are [now] his witnesses before the people.
text.DRB: who was seen for many days of them that came up together with him from Galilee into Jerusalem, who until this present are his witnesses to the people.

text.NRSV: And we bring you the good news that what God promised to our ancestors
text.Rotherham: We, therefore, unto you, bring the good news, as to the promise which, unto our fathers, was made,—
text.KJV: And we declare unto you glad tidings, how that the promise which was made unto the fathers,
text.NABRE: We ourselves are proclaiming this good news to you that what God promised our ancestors
text.DRB: And we preach unto you that promise which was made to our fathers:

text.NRSV: he has fulfilled for us, their children, by raising Jesus; as also it is written in the second psalm, ‘You are my Son; today I have begotten you.’
text.Rotherham: That God hath fulfilled, the same, for our children, by raising up Jesus: as also, in the second psalm, it is written—My son, art, thou: I, this day, have begotten thee.
text.KJV: God hath fulfilled the same unto us their children, in that he hath raised up Jesus again; as it is also written in the second psalm, Thou art my Son, this day have I begotten thee.
text.NABRE: he has brought to fulfillment for us, [their] children, by raising up Jesus, as it is written in the second psalm, ‘You are my son; this day I have begotten you.’
text.DRB: that God hath fulfilled this same to our children, raising up Jesus, as in the second Psalm also it is written: My Son art thou, this day have I begotten thee.

text.NRSV: As to his raising him from the dead, no more to return to corruption, he has spoken in this way, ‘I will give you the holy promises made to David.’
text.Rotherham: And, in that he raised him from among the dead, no more destined to return unto corruption, on this wise hath he spoken—I will give unto you the faithful lovingkindnesses of David.
text.KJV: And as concerning that he raised him up from the dead, now no more to return to corruption, he said on this wise, I will give you the sure mercies of David.
text.NABRE: And that he raised him from the dead never to return to corruption he declared in this way, ‘I shall give you the benefits assured to David.’
text.DRB: And that he raised him up from the dead, not to return now any more into corruption, thus he said: That I will give you the holy things of David faithful.

text.NRSV: Therefore he has also said in another psalm, ‘You will not let your Holy One experience corruption.’
text.Rotherham: Wherefore also, in a different [place], he saith—Thou wilt not give thy man of lovingkindness to see corruption.
text.KJV: Wherefore he saith also in another psalm, Thou shalt not suffer thine Holy One to see corruption.
text.NABRE: That is why he also says in another psalm, ‘You will not suffer your holy one to see corruption.’
text.DRB: And therefore in another place also he saith: Thou shalt not give thy holy one to see corruption.

text.NRSV: For David, after he had served the purpose of God in his own generation, died, was laid beside his ancestors, and experienced corruption;
text.Rotherham: For, David, indeed, unto his own generation having done service, by the counsel of God, fell asleep, and was added unto fathers, and saw corruption;
text.KJV: For David, after he had served his own generation by the will of God, fell on sleep, and was laid unto his fathers, and saw corruption:
text.NABRE: Now David, after he had served the will of God in his lifetime, fell asleep, was gathered to his ancestors, and did see corruption.
text.DRB: For David in his generation when he had served, according to the will of God slept: and he was laid to his fathers & saw corruption.

text.NRSV: but he whom God raised up experienced no corruption.
text.Rotherham: But, he whom God hath raised, did not see corruption.
text.KJV: But he, whom God raised again, saw no corruption.
text.NABRE: But the one whom God raised up did not see corruption.
text.DRB: But he whom God hath raised up, saw no corruption.

text.NRSV: Let it be known to you therefore, my brothers, that through this man forgiveness of sins is proclaimed to you;
text.Rotherham: Be it known unto you, therefore, brethren,—that, through this man, unto you, remission of sins is declared;
text.KJV: Be it known unto you therefore, men and brethren, that through this man is preached unto you the forgiveness of sins:
text.NABRE: You must know, my brothers, that through him forgiveness of sins is being proclaimed to you, [and] in regard to everything from which you could not be justified under the law of Moses,
text.DRB: Be it known therefore to you, men brethren, that through him, forgiveness of sins is preached to you, from all the things from the which you could not be justified by the law of Moyses.

text.NRSV: by this Jesus everyone who believes is set free from all those sins from which you could not be freed by the law of Moses.
text.Rotherham: And, from all things from which ye could not, by the law of Moses, be justified, by this man, everyone that believeth, is justified.
text.KJV: And by him all that believe are justified from all things, from which ye could not be justified by the law of Moses.
text.NABRE: in him every believer is justified.
text.DRB: In him every one that believeth, is justified.

text.NRSV: Beware, therefore, that what the prophets said does not happen to you:
text.Rotherham: Be taking heed, therefore, lest that come upon you which hath been spoken in the prophets—
text.KJV: Beware therefore, lest that come upon you, which is spoken of in the prophets;
text.NABRE: Be careful, then, that what was said in the prophets not come about:
text.DRB: Take heed therefore lest that come upon you which is spoken in the prophets,

text.NRSV: ‘Look, you scoffers! Be amazed and perish, for in your days I am doing a work, a work that you will never believe, even if someone tells you.’”
text.Rotherham: See, ye despisers, and marvel, and disappear: in that, a work, am, I, working in your days,—a work, which in nowise will ye believe, though one relate it in full unto you.
text.KJV: Behold, ye despisers, and wonder, and perish: for I work a work in your days, a work which ye shall in no wise believe, though a man declare it unto you.
text.NABRE: ‘Look on, you scoffers, be amazed and disappear. For I am doing a work in your days, a work that you will never believe even if someone tells you.’”
text.DRB: See ye contemners, and wonder, and perish: because I work a work in your days, a work which you will not believe, if any man shall tell it you.

text.NRSV: As Paul and Barnabas were going out, the people urged them to speak about these things again the next sabbath.
text.Rotherham: And, as they were going out, they kept on beseeching that, on the ensuing sabbath, might be spoken unto them these things.
text.KJV: And when the Jews were gone out of the synagogue, the Gentiles besought that these words might be preached to them the next sabbath.
text.NABRE: As they were leaving, they invited them to speak on these subjects the following sabbath.
text.DRB: And they going forth, they desired them that the Sabboth following they would speak unto them these words.

text.NRSV: When the meeting of the synagogue broke up, many Jews and devout converts to Judaism followed Paul and Barnabas, who spoke to them and urged them to continue in the grace of God.
text.Rotherham: And, when the congregation was broken up, there followed many of the Jews, and of the devout proselytes, with Paul and Barnabas; who, indeed, in speaking unto them, went on persuading them to abide in the favour of God.
text.KJV: Now when the congregation was broken up, many of the Jews and religious proselytes followed Paul and Barnabas: who, speaking to them, persuaded them to continue in the grace of God.
text.NABRE: After the congregation had dispersed, many Jews and worshipers who were converts to Judaism followed Paul and Barnabas, who spoke to them and urged them to remain faithful to the grace of God.
text.DRB: And when the Synagogue was dismissed, many of the Jews, and of the strangers serving God, followed Paul & Barnabas: who speaking exhorted them to continue in the grace of God.

text.NRSV: The next sabbath almost the whole city gathered to hear the word of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, on the coming sabbath, almost all the city, was gathered together, to hear the word of God.
text.KJV: And the next sabbath day came almost the whole city together to hear the word of God.
text.NABRE: Address to the Gentiles. On the following sabbath almost the whole city gathered to hear the word of the Lord.
text.DRB: But the next Sabboth the whole city almost assembled to hear the word of God.

text.NRSV: But when the Jews saw the crowds, they were filled with jealousy; and blaspheming, they contradicted what was spoken by Paul.
text.Rotherham: But, the Jews, seeing, the multitudes, were filled with jealousy,—and began speaking against the things which, by Paul, were being spoken, defaming them.
text.KJV: But when the Jews saw the multitudes, they were filled with envy, and spake against those things which were spoken by Paul, contradicting and blaspheming.
text.NABRE: When the Jews saw the crowds, they were filled with jealousy and with violent abuse contradicted what Paul said.
text.DRB: And the Jews seeing the multitudes, were replenished with envy, & contradicted those things which were said of Paul, blaspheming.

text.NRSV: Then both Paul and Barnabas spoke out boldly, saying, “It was necessary that the word of God should be spoken first to you. Since you reject it and judge yourselves to be unworthy of eternal life, we are now turning to the Gentiles.
text.Rotherham: And Paul and Barnabas, speaking boldly, said—Unto you, was it necessary, that the word of God should first be spoken: seeing ye are thrusting it from you, and, unworthy, are judging yourselves of the age-abiding life, lo! we turn unto the nations;
text.KJV: Then Paul and Barnabas waxed bold, and said, It was necessary that the word of God should first have been spoken to you: but seeing ye put it from you, and judge yourselves unworthy of everlasting life, lo, we turn to the Gentiles.
text.NABRE: Both Paul and Barnabas spoke out boldly and said, “It was necessary that the word of God be spoken to you first, but since you reject it and condemn yourselves as unworthy of eternal life, we now turn to the Gentiles.
text.DRB: Then Paul and Barnabas constantly said: To you it behoved us first to speak the word of God: but because you repel it, and judge yourselves unworthy of eternal life; behold we turn to the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: For so the Lord has commanded us, saying, ‘I have set you to be a light for the Gentiles, so that you may bring salvation to the ends of the earth.’”
text.Rotherham: For so hath the Lord commanded us—I have set thee for a light of nations, that thou mayest be for salvation unto the end of the earth.
text.KJV: For so hath the Lord commanded us, saying, I have set thee to be a light of the Gentiles, that thou shouldest be for salvation unto the ends of the earth.
text.NABRE: For so the Lord has commanded us, ‘I have made you a light to the Gentiles, that you may be an instrument of salvation to the ends of the earth.’”
text.DRB: For so our Lord commanded us: I have put thee to be the light of the Gentiles; that thou mayest be salvation unto the utmost of the earth.

text.NRSV: When the Gentiles heard this, they were glad and praised the word of the Lord; and as many as had been destined for eternal life became believers.
text.Rotherham: And they of the nations, hearing [this], began to rejoice, and to be glorifying God, and they believed—as many as had become disposed for life age-abiding.
text.KJV: And when the Gentiles heard this, they were glad, and glorified the word of the Lord: and as many as were ordained to eternal life believed.
text.NABRE: The Gentiles were delighted when they heard this and glorified the word of the Lord. All who were destined for eternal life came to believe,
text.DRB: And the Gentiles hearing it, were glad, and glorified the word of our Lord: and there believed as many as were preordinate to life everlasting.

text.NRSV: Thus the word of the Lord spread throughout the region.
text.Rotherham: And the word of the Lord went on to be carried through the whole country.
text.KJV: And the word of the Lord was published throughout all the region.
text.NABRE: and the word of the Lord continued to spread through the whole region.
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was spread throughout the whole country.

text.NRSV: But the Jews incited the devout women of high standing and the leading men of the city, and stirred up persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and drove them out of their region.
text.Rotherham: But, the Jews, urged on the devout women of the higher class, and the chief men of the city, and roused up a persecution against Paul and Barnabas,—and thrust them out from their bounds.
text.KJV: But the Jews stirred up the devout and honourable women, and the chief men of the city, and raised persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and expelled them out of their coasts.
text.NABRE: The Jews, however, incited the women of prominence who were worshipers and the leading men of the city, stirred up a persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and expelled them from their territory.
text.DRB: But the Jews stirred up religious and honest women, and the chief of the city, and raised persecution against Paul and Barnabas: and they did cast them forth out of their coasts.

text.NRSV: So they shook the dust off their feet in protest against them, and went to Iconium.
text.Rotherham: But they, shaking off the dust of their feet against them, came into Iconium.
text.KJV: But they shook off the dust of their feet against them, and came unto Iconium.
text.NABRE: So they shook the dust from their feet in protest against them and went to Iconium.
text.DRB: But they shaking off the dust of their feet against them, came to Iconium.

text.NRSV: And the disciples were filled with joy and with the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: And, the disciples, were filled with joy, and with Holy Spirit.
text.KJV: And the disciples were filled with joy, and with the Holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: The disciples were filled with joy and the holy Spirit.
text.DRB: The Disciples also were replenished with joy and with the Holy Ghost.

text.NRSV: The same thing occurred in Iconium, where Paul and Barnabas went into the Jewish synagogue and spoke in such a way that a great number of both Jews and Greeks became believers.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in Iconium that they together entered into the synagogue of the Jews, and so spake that there believed, both of Jews and Greeks, a great throng.
text.KJV: And it came to pass in Iconium, that they went both together into the synagogue of the Jews, and so spake, that a great multitude both of the Jews and also of the Greeks believed.
text.NABRE: In Iconium they entered the Jewish synagogue together and spoke in such a way that a great number of both Jews and Greeks came to believe,
text.DRB: And it came to pass at Iconium that they entered together into the Synagogue of the Jews, and so spake, that a very great multitude of Jews and of the Greeks did believe.

text.NRSV: But the unbelieving Jews stirred up the Gentiles and poisoned their minds against the brothers.
text.Rotherham: But, the unpersuaded Jews, roused up and provoked the souls of them of the nations against the brethren.
text.KJV: But the unbelieving Jews stirred up the Gentiles, and made their minds evil affected against the brethren.
text.NABRE: although the disbelieving Jews stirred up and poisoned the minds of the Gentiles against the brothers.
text.DRB: But the Jews that were incredulous, stirred up and incensed the hearts of the Gentiles to anger against the brethren.

text.NRSV: So they remained for a long time, speaking boldly for the Lord, who testified to the word of his grace by granting signs and wonders to be done through them.
text.Rotherham: A good while, therefore, tarried they, using boldness of speech [in dependence] upon the Lord, who was bearing witness unto his word of favour, granting signs and wonders to be coming to pass through their hands.
text.KJV: Long time therefore abode they speaking boldly in the Lord, which gave testimony unto the word of his grace, and granted signs and wonders to be done by their hands.
text.NABRE: So they stayed for a considerable period, speaking out boldly for the Lord, who confirmed the word about his grace by granting signs and wonders to occur through their hands.
text.DRB: Along time therefore they abode, dealing confidently in our Lord, who gave testimony to the word of his grace, granting signs and wonders to be done by their hands.

text.NRSV: But the residents of the city were divided; some sided with the Jews, and some with the apostles.
text.Rotherham: And the throng of the city was divided; and some indeed were with the Jews, while some were with the apostles.
text.KJV: But the multitude of the city was divided: and part held with the Jews, and part with the apostles.
text.NABRE: The people of the city were divided: some were with the Jews; others, with the apostles.
text.DRB: And the multitude of the city was divided: and certain of them indeed were with the Jews, but certain with the Apostles.

text.NRSV: And when an attempt was made by both Gentiles and Jews, with their rulers, to mistreat them and to stone them,
text.Rotherham: But, when there took place an assault both of them of the nations and of the Jews, with their rulers, to maltreat and to stone them,
text.KJV: And when there was an assault made both of the Gentiles, and also of the Jews with their rulers, to use them despitefully, and to stone them,
text.NABRE: When there was an attempt by both the Gentiles and the Jews, together with their leaders, to attack and stone them,
text.DRB: And when the Gentiles and the Jews with their princes had made an assault, to use them contumeliously, and to stone them,

text.NRSV: the apostles learned of it and fled to Lystra and Derbe, cities of Lycaonia, and to the surrounding country;
text.Rotherham: They became aware of it, and fled unto the cities of Lycaonia, Lystra and Derbe, and the surrounding country;
text.KJV: They were ware of it, and fled unto Lystra and Derbe, cities of Lycaonia, and unto the region that lieth round about:
text.NABRE: they realized it and fled to the Lycaonian cities of Lystra and Derbe and to the surrounding countryside,
text.DRB: understanding it, they fled to the cities of Lycaonia, Lystra and Derbe, and the whole country about, and there they were evangelizing.

text.NRSV: and there they continued proclaiming the good news.
text.Rotherham: And, there, were they announcing the glad tidings.
text.KJV: And there they preached the gospel.
text.NABRE: where they continued to proclaim the good news.

text.NRSV: In Lystra there was a man sitting who could not use his feet and had never walked, for he had been crippled from birth.
text.Rotherham: And, a certain man in Lystra, impotent in his feet, was sitting,—lame from his mother’s womb, who never had walked.
text.KJV: And there sat a certain man at Lystra, impotent in his feet, being a cripple from his mother’s womb, who never had walked:
text.NABRE: Paul and Barnabas at Lystra. At Lystra there was a crippled man, lame from birth, who had never walked.
text.DRB: And a certain man at Lystra impotent of his feet sat there, lame from his mother's womb, that never had walked.

text.NRSV: He listened to Paul as he was speaking. And Paul, looking at him intently and seeing that he had faith to be healed,
text.Rotherham: This man, hearkened unto Paul, as he was speaking,—who, looking steadfastly at him, and seeing he had faith to be made well,
text.KJV: The same heard Paul speak: who stedfastly beholding him, and perceiving that he had faith to be healed,
text.NABRE: He listened to Paul speaking, who looked intently at him, saw that he had the faith to be healed,
text.DRB: This same heard Paul speaking. Who looking upon him, and seeing that he had faith for to be saved,

text.NRSV: said in a loud voice, “Stand upright on your feet.” And the man sprang up and began to walk.
text.Rotherham: Said, with a loud voice—Stand up on thy feet, erect! And he sprang up, and began to walk about.
text.KJV: Said with a loud voice, Stand upright on thy feet. And he leaped and walked.
text.NABRE: and called out in a loud voice, “Stand up straight on your feet.” He jumped up and began to walk about.
text.DRB: he said with a loud voice: Stand up right on thy feet. And he leaped & walked.

text.NRSV: When the crowds saw what Paul had done, they shouted in the Lycaonian language, “The gods have come down to us in human form!”
text.Rotherham: And, the multitudes, seeing what Paul had done, lifted up their voice, in the speech of Lycaonia—The gods, made like unto men, have come down unto us!
text.KJV: And when the people saw what Paul had done, they lifted up their voices, saying in the speech of Lycaonia, The gods are come down to us in the likeness of men.
text.NABRE: When the crowds saw what Paul had done, they cried out in Lycaonian, “The gods have come down to us in human form.”
text.DRB: And the multitudes when they had seen what Paul had done, lifted up their voice in the Lycaonian tongue, saying: Gods made like to men, are descended to us.

text.NRSV: Barnabas they called Zeus, and Paul they called Hermes, because he was the chief speaker.
text.Rotherham: And they went on to call Barnabas, Jupiter, and Paul, Mercury, seeing that, he, was the leader of discourse.
text.KJV: And they called Barnabas, Jupiter; and Paul, Mercurius, because he was the chief speaker.
text.NABRE: They called Barnabas “Zeus” and Paul “Hermes,” because he was the chief speaker.
text.DRB: And they called Barnabas, Jupiter: but Paul Mercury, because he was the chief speaker.

text.NRSV: The priest of Zeus, whose temple was just outside the city, brought oxen and garlands to the gates; he and the crowds wanted to offer sacrifice.
text.Rotherham: Also, the priest of the Jupiter that was before the city, bringing bulls and garlands unto the gates, with the multitudes, would have offered sacrifice.
text.KJV: Then the priest of Jupiter, which was before their city, brought oxen and garlands unto the gates, and would have done sacrifice with the people.
text.NABRE: And the priest of Zeus, whose temple was at the entrance to the city, brought oxen and garlands to the gates, for he together with the people intended to offer sacrifice.
text.DRB: The Priest also of Jupiter that was before the city, bringing oxen and garlands before the gates, would with the people sacrifice.

text.NRSV: When the apostles Barnabas and Paul heard of it, they tore their clothes and rushed out into the crowd, shouting,
text.Rotherham: But the apostles Barnabas and Paul, hearing of it, rending asunder their own mantles, sprang forward amidst the multitude, crying aloud,
text.KJV: Which when the apostles, Barnabas and Paul, heard of, they rent their clothes, and ran in among the people, crying out,
text.NABRE: The apostles Barnabas and Paul tore their garments when they heard this and rushed out into the crowd, shouting,
text.DRB: Which thing when the Apostles Barnabas and Paul heard, renting their coats, they leaped forth into the multitudes, crying

text.NRSV: “Friends, why are you doing this? We are mortals just like you, and we bring you good news, that you should turn from these worthless things to the living God, who made the heaven and the earth and the sea and all that is in them.
text.Rotherham: and saying—Men! Why, these things, are ye doing? We also, of like nature with you, are men, bringing you the good news, that from these vain things, ye should be turning unto a living God:—Who made heaven and the earth and the sea and all things therein;
text.KJV: And saying, Sirs, why do ye these things? We also are men of like passions with you, and preach unto you that ye should turn from these vanities unto the living God, which made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and all things that are therein:
text.NABRE: “Men, why are you doing this? We are of the same nature as you, human beings. We proclaim to you good news that you should turn from these idols to the living God, ‘who made heaven and earth and sea and all that is in them.’
text.DRB: and saying: Ye men, why do you these things? We also are mortal men like unto you, preaching to you for to convert from these vain things, to the living God that made the Heaven, and the earth, and the sea, and all things that are in them:

text.NRSV: In past generations he allowed all the nations to follow their own ways;
text.Rotherham: Who in the bygone generations suffered all the nations to be going on in their own ways,—
text.KJV: Who in times past suffered all nations to walk in their own ways.
text.NABRE: In past generations he allowed all Gentiles to go their own ways;
text.DRB: who in the Generations past suffered all the Gentiles to go their own ways.

text.NRSV: yet he has not left himself without a witness in doing good—giving you rains from heaven and fruitful seasons, and filling you with food and your hearts with joy.”
text.Rotherham: Although, not without witness, he left himself, doing good, from heaven, upon you, giving rain and fruitful seasons, filling, with food and gladness, your hearts.
text.KJV: Nevertheless he left not himself without witness, in that he did good, and gave us rain from heaven, and fruitful seasons, filling our hearts with food and gladness.
text.NABRE: yet, in bestowing his goodness, he did not leave himself without witness, for he gave you rains from heaven and fruitful seasons, and filled you with nourishment and gladness for your hearts.”
text.DRB: Howbeit he left not himself without testimony, being beneficial from Heaven, giving rains, and fruitful seasons, filling our hearts with food and gladness.

text.NRSV: Even with these words, they scarcely restrained the crowds from offering sacrifice to them.
text.Rotherham: Even these things, saying, scarcely restrained they the multitudes from offering sacrifice unto them.
text.KJV: And with these sayings scarce restrained they the people, that they had not done sacrifice unto them.
text.NABRE: Even with these words, they scarcely restrained the crowds from offering sacrifice to them.
text.DRB: And speaking these things, they scarce appeased the multitudes from sacrificing to them.

text.NRSV: But Jews came there from Antioch and Iconium and won over the crowds. Then they stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing that he was dead.
text.Rotherham: But there came thither, from Antioch and Iconium, Jews, and, persuading the multitudes, and stoning Paul, they dragged him outside the city, supposing him to be dead.
text.KJV: And there came thither certain Jews from Antioch and Iconium, who persuaded the people, and, having stoned Paul, drew him out of the city, supposing he had been dead.
text.NABRE: However, some Jews from Antioch and Iconium arrived and won over the crowds. They stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing that he was dead.
text.DRB: But there came in certain Jews from Antioch and Iconium: and persuading the multitudes, and stoning Paul, they drew him out of the city, thinking him to be dead.

text.NRSV: But when the disciples surrounded him, he got up and went into the city. The next day he went on with Barnabas to Derbe.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, the disciples surrounding him, he rose up, and entered into the city. And, on the morrow, he went forth, with Barnabas, unto Derbe.
text.KJV: Howbeit, as the disciples stood round about him, he rose up, and came into the city: and the next day he departed with Barnabas to Derbe.
text.NABRE: But when the disciples gathered around him, he got up and entered the city. On the following day he left with Barnabas for Derbe.
text.DRB: But the Disciples compassing him round about, he rising up, entered into the city, and the next day he went forth with Barnabas unto Derbe.

text.NRSV: After they had proclaimed the good news to that city and had made many disciples, they returned to Lystra, then on to Iconium and Antioch.
text.Rotherham: Delivering the good news unto that city also, and making a good number of disciples, they returned unto Lystra, and unto Iconium, and unto Antioch,—
text.KJV: And when they had preached the gospel to that city, and had taught many, they returned again to Lystra, and to Iconium, and Antioch,
text.NABRE: End of the First Mission. After they had proclaimed the good news to that city and made a considerable number of disciples, they returned to Lystra and to Iconium and to Antioch.
text.DRB: And when they had evangelized to that city, & had taught many, they returned to Lystra and Iconium, and to Antioch:

text.NRSV: There they strengthened the souls of the disciples and encouraged them to continue in the faith, saying, “It is through many persecutions that we must enter the kingdom of God.”
text.Rotherham: confirming the souls of the disciples, beseeching them to abide in the faith, and [declaring] that, through many tribulations, must we enter into the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: Confirming the souls of the disciples, and exhorting them to continue in the faith, and that we must through much tribulation enter into the kingdom of God.
text.NABRE: They strengthened the spirits of the disciples and exhorted them to persevere in the faith, saying, “It is necessary for us to undergo many hardships to enter the kingdom of God.”
text.DRB: confirming the hearts of the Disciples, and exhorting them to continue in the faith; and that by many tribulations we must enter into the Kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: And after they had appointed elders for them in each church, with prayer and fasting they entrusted them to the Lord in whom they had come to believe.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, appointing unto them by vote, in each assembly, elders, praying with fastings, they commended them unto the Lord on whom they had believed.
text.KJV: And when they had ordained them elders in every church, and had prayed with fasting, they commended them to the Lord, on whom they believed.
text.NABRE: They appointed presbyters for them in each church and, with prayer and fasting, commended them to the Lord in whom they had put their faith.
text.DRB: And when they had ordained to them Priests in every Church, and had prayed with fastings, they commended them to our Lord in whom they believed.

text.NRSV: Then they passed through Pisidia and came to Pamphylia.
text.Rotherham: And, passing through Pisidia, they came into Pamphylia;
text.KJV: And after they had passed throughout Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.
text.NABRE: Then they traveled through Pisidia and reached Pamphylia.
text.DRB: And passing through Pisidia, they came into Pamphylia,

text.NRSV: When they had spoken the word in Perga, they went down to Attalia.
text.Rotherham: and, speaking in Perga the word, they came down unto Attalia;
text.KJV: And when they had preached the word in Perga, they went down into Attalia:
text.NABRE: After proclaiming the word at Perga they went down to Attalia.
text.DRB: and speaking the word of our Lord in Perge, they went down into Attalia:

text.NRSV: From there they sailed back to Antioch, where they had been commended to the grace of God for the work that they had completed.
text.Rotherham: and, from thence, they set sail for Antioch, whence they had been given up unto the favour of God for the work which they had fulfilled.
text.KJV: And thence sailed to Antioch, from whence they had been recommended to the grace of God for the work which they fulfilled.
text.NABRE: From there they sailed to Antioch, where they had been commended to the grace of God for the work they had now accomplished.
text.DRB: and from thence they sailed to Antioch, whence they had been delivered to the grace of God unto the work which they accomplished.

text.NRSV: When they arrived, they called the church together and related all that God had done with them, and how he had opened a door of faith for the Gentiles.
text.Rotherham: And, when they had arrived and gathered together the assembly, they began recounting how many things God had done with them, and that he had opened, unto the nations, a door of faith.
text.KJV: And when they were come, and had gathered the church together, they rehearsed all that God had done with them, and how he had opened the door of faith unto the Gentiles.
text.NABRE: And when they arrived, they called the church together and reported what God had done with them and how he had opened the door of faith to the Gentiles.
text.DRB: And when they were come, and had assembled the Church, they reported what great things God had done with them, & that he had opened a door of faith to the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: And they stayed there with the disciples for some time.
text.Rotherham: And they spent no little time with the disciples.
text.KJV: And there they abode long time with the disciples.
text.NABRE: Then they spent no little time with the disciples.
text.DRB: And they abode no little time with the Disciples.

text.KJV: And certain men which came down from Judea taught the brethren, and said, Except ye be circumcised after the manner of Moses, ye cannot be saved.
text.NABRE: Some who had come down from Judea were instructing the brothers, “Unless you are circumcised according to the Mosaic practice, you cannot be saved.”
text.NRSV: Then certain individuals came down from Judea and were teaching the brothers, “Unless you are circumcised according to the custom of Moses, you cannot be saved.”
text.DRB: And certain coming down from Jewry, taught the brethren: That unless you be circumcised according to the manner of Moyses, you cannot be saved.
text.Rotherham: And, certain persons, coming down from Judea, began to teach the brethren—Except ye be circumcised according to the custom of Moses, ye cannot be saved.

text.KJV: When therefore Paul and Barnabas had no small dissension and disputation with them, they determined that Paul and Barnabas, and certain other of them, should go up to Jerusalem unto the apostles and elders about this question.
text.NABRE: Because there arose no little dissension and debate by Paul and Barnabas with them, it was decided that Paul, Barnabas, and some of the others should go up to Jerusalem to the apostles and presbyters about this question.
text.NRSV: And after Paul and Barnabas had no small dissension and debate with them, Paul and Barnabas and some of the others were appointed to go up to Jerusalem to discuss this question with the apostles and the elders.
text.DRB: No little sedition therefore being risen to Paul and Barnabas against them, they appointed that Paul and Barnabas should go up, & certain others of the rest, to the Apostles and Priests unto Jerusalem, upon this question.
text.Rotherham: And, when Paul and Barnabas had had no little dissension and discussion with them, it was arranged that Paul and Barnabas and certain others from among them should go up unto the Apostles and Elders in Jerusalem concerning this question,

text.KJV: And being brought on their way by the church, they passed through Phenice and Samaria, declaring the conversion of the Gentiles: and they caused great joy unto all the brethren.
text.NABRE: They were sent on their journey by the church, and passed through Phoenicia and Samaria telling of the conversion of the Gentiles, and brought great joy to all the brothers.
text.NRSV: So they were sent on their way by the church, and as they passed through both Phoenicia and Samaria, they reported the conversion of the Gentiles, and brought great joy to all the believers.
text.DRB: They therefore being brought on their way by the Church, passed through Phoenice & Samaria, reporting the conversion of the Gentiles: and they made great joy to all the brethren.
text.Rotherham: They, therefore, being set forward by the Assembly, began passing through Phoenicia and Samaria, fully relating the conversion of them of the nations, and were causing great joy unto all the brethren.

text.KJV: And when they were come to Jerusalem, they were received of the church, and of the apostles and elders, and they declared all things that God had done with them.
text.NABRE: When they arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church, as well as by the apostles and the presbyters, and they reported what God had done with them.
text.NRSV: When they came to Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church and the apostles and the elders, and they reported all that God had done with them.
text.DRB: And when they were come to Jerusalem, they were received of the Church and of the Apostles and Ancients, declaring whatsoever God had done with them.
text.Rotherham: And, having arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the Assembly and the Apostles and the Elders; and they recounted all things God had done with them.

text.KJV: But there rose up certain of the sect of the Pharisees which believed, saying, That it was needful to circumcise them, and to command them to keep the law of Moses.
text.NABRE: But some from the party of the Pharisees who had become believers stood up and said, “It is necessary to circumcise them and direct them to observe the Mosaic law.”
text.NRSV: But some believers who belonged to the sect of the Pharisees stood up and said, “It is necessary for them to be circumcised and ordered to keep the law of Moses.”
text.DRB: And there arose certain of the heresy of the Pharisees that believed, saying, That they must be circumcised; commanded also to keep the law of Moyses.
text.Rotherham: But there [had] stood forth some of those who, from the sect of Pharisees, had believed, saying—It is needful to be circumcising them, also to charge them to be keeping the law of Moses.

text.KJV: And the apostles and elders came together for to consider of this matter.
text.NABRE: The apostles and the presbyters met together to see about this matter.
text.NRSV: The apostles and the elders met together to consider this matter.
text.DRB: And the Apostles and Ancients assembled to consider of this word.
text.Rotherham: And the Apostles and Elders were gathered together to see about this matter.

text.KJV: And when there had been much disputing, Peter rose up, and said unto them, Men and brethren, ye know how that a good while ago God made choice among us, that the Gentiles by my mouth should hear the word of the gospel, and believe.
text.NABRE: After much debate had taken place, Peter got up and said to them, “My brothers, you are well aware that from early days God made his choice among you that through my mouth the Gentiles would hear the word of the gospel and believe.
text.NRSV: After there had been much debate, Peter stood up and said to them, “My brothers, you know that in the early days God made a choice among you, that I should be the one through whom the Gentiles would hear the message of the good news and become believers.
text.DRB: And when there was made a great disputation, Peter rising up said to them: Men brethren, you know that of old days God among us chose, that by my mouth the Gentiles should hear the word of the Gospel, and believe.
text.Rotherham: And, when much discussion had arisen, Peter standing up, said unto them—Brethren! Ye yourselves, well know that, in days long past, amongst you, God chose that, through my mouth, the nations should hear the word of the glad tidings, and believe.

text.KJV: And God, which knoweth the hearts, bare them witness, giving them the Holy Ghost, even as he did unto us;
text.NABRE: And God, who knows the heart, bore witness by granting them the holy Spirit just as he did us.
text.NRSV: And God, who knows the human heart, testified to them by giving them the Holy Spirit, just as he did to us;
text.DRB: And God which knoweth the hearts, gave testimony, giving unto them the Holy Ghost as well as to us,
text.Rotherham: And, the heart-observing God, bare witness,—unto them, giving the Holy Spirit, just as, even unto us;

text.KJV: And put no difference between us and them, purifying their hearts by faith.
text.NABRE: He made no distinction between us and them, for by faith he purified their hearts.
text.NRSV: and in cleansing their hearts by faith he has made no distinction between them and us.
text.DRB: and hath put no difference between us and them, by faith purifying their hearts.
text.Rotherham: and made no distinction at all betwixt us and them, by their faith, purifying their hearts.

text.KJV: Now therefore why tempt ye God, to put a yoke upon the neck of the disciples, which neither our fathers nor we were able to bear?
text.NABRE: Why, then, are you now putting God to the test by placing on the shoulders of the disciples a yoke that neither our ancestors nor we have been able to bear?
text.NRSV: Now therefore why are you putting God to the test by placing on the neck of the disciples a yoke that neither our ancestors nor we have been able to bear?
text.DRB: Now therefore why tempt you God, to put a yoke upon the necks of the Disciples, which neither our fathers nor we have been able to bear?
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, why are ye proving God, that ye should put a yoke upon the neck of the disciples, which, neither our fathers, nor we, have been able to bear.

text.KJV: But we believe that through the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ we shall be saved, even as they.
text.NABRE: On the contrary, we believe that we are saved through the grace of the Lord Jesus, in the same way as they.”
text.NRSV: On the contrary, we believe that we will be saved through the grace of the Lord Jesus, just as they will.”
text.DRB: But by the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ we believe to be saved, in like manner as they also.
text.Rotherham: But, through the favour of the Lord Jesus, we believe we shall be saved, in like manner as, even they.

text.KJV: Then all the multitude kept silence, and gave audience to Barnabas and Paul, declaring what miracles and wonders God had wrought among the Gentiles by them.
text.NABRE: The whole assembly fell silent, and they listened while Paul and Barnabas described the signs and wonders God had worked among the Gentiles through them.
text.NRSV: The whole assembly kept silence, and listened to Barnabas and Paul as they told of all the signs and wonders that God had done through them among the Gentiles.
text.DRB: And all the multitude held their peace: and they heard Barnabas and Paul telling what great signs and wonders God had done among the Gentiles by them.
text.Rotherham: And all the throng held their peace, and began to hearken unto Barnabas and Paul relating how many signs and wonders God had done among the nations, through them.

text.KJV: And after they had held their peace, James answered, saying, Men and brethren, hearken unto me:
text.NABRE: James on Dietary Law. After they had fallen silent, James responded, “My brothers, listen to me.
text.NRSV: After they finished speaking, James replied, “My brothers, listen to me.
text.DRB: And after they held their peace, James answered, saying: Men brethren, hear me.
text.Rotherham: And, after they held their peace, James answered, saying—Brethren! hearken unto me.

text.KJV: Simeon hath declared how God at the first did visit the Gentiles, to take out of them a people for his name.
text.NABRE: Symeon has described how God first concerned himself with acquiring from among the Gentiles a people for his name.
text.NRSV: Simeon has related how God first looked favorably on the Gentiles, to take from among them a people for his name.
text.DRB: Simon hath told how God first visited to take of the Gentiles a people to his name.
text.Rotherham: Symeon, hath fully told how God, first visited, to take out of the nations, a people for his name.

text.KJV: And to this agree the words of the prophets; as it is written,
text.NABRE: The words of the prophets agree with this, as is written:
text.NRSV: This agrees with the words of the prophets, as it is written,
text.DRB: And to this accord the words of the Prophets, as it is written:
text.Rotherham: And, with this, agree the words of the prophets, according as it is written—

text.KJV: After this I will return, and will build again the tabernacle of David, which is fallen down; and I will build again the ruins thereof, and I will set it up:
text.NABRE: ‘After this I shall return and rebuild the fallen hut of David; from its ruins I shall rebuild it and raise it up again,
text.NRSV: ‘After this I will return, and I will rebuild the dwelling of David, which has fallen; from its ruins I will rebuild it, and I will set it up,
text.DRB: After these things I will return, and will re-edify the tabernacle of David which was fallen, and the ruins thereof I will re-edify, and set it up:
text.Rotherham: After these things, will I return, and will rebuild the tent of David that hath fallen, and, the ruins thereof, will I rebuild, and will set it up again:

text.KJV: That the residue of men might seek after the Lord, and all the Gentiles, upon whom my name is called, saith the Lord, who doeth all these things.
text.NABRE: so that the rest of humanity may seek out the Lord, even all the Gentiles on whom my name is invoked. Thus says the Lord who accomplishes these things,
text.NRSV: so that all other peoples may seek the Lord— even all the Gentiles over whom my name has been called. Thus says the Lord, who has been making these things
text.DRB: that the residue of men may seek after the Lord, and all nations upon whom my name is invocated, saith the Lord that doeth these things.
text.Rotherham: That the residues of men may seek out the Lord, and all the nations upon whom my name hath been called, saith the Lord that doeth these things,

text.DRB: To our Lord was his own work known from the beginning of the world.
text.NABRE: known from of old.’
text.KJV: Known unto God are all his works from the beginning of the world.
text.Rotherham: Known from age-past times.
text.NRSV: known from long ago.’

text.KJV: Wherefore my sentence is, that we trouble not them, which from among the Gentiles are turned to God:
text.NABRE: It is my judgment, therefore, that we ought to stop troubling the Gentiles who turn to God,
text.NRSV: Therefore I have reached the decision that we should not trouble those Gentiles who are turning to God,
text.DRB: For the which cause I judge, that they which of the Gentiles are converted to God, are not to be disquieted,
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, I, judge, not to be troubling them who, from the nations, are turning unto God;

text.KJV: But that we write unto them, that they abstain from pollutions of idols, and from fornication, and from things strangled, and from blood.
text.NABRE: but tell them by letter to avoid pollution from idols, unlawful marriage, the meat of strangled animals, and blood.
text.NRSV: but we should write to them to abstain only from things polluted by idols and from fornication and from whatever has been strangled and from blood.
text.DRB: but to write unto them that they refrain themselves from the contaminations of idols, and fornication, and strangled things, and blood.
text.Rotherham: but to write unto them, to abstain from the pollutions of idols, and from fornication, and from what is strangled, and from blood.

text.KJV: For Moses of old time hath in every city them that preach him, being read in the synagogues every sabbath day.
text.NABRE: For Moses, for generations now, has had those who proclaim him in every town, as he has been read in the synagogues every sabbath.”
text.NRSV: For in every city, for generations past, Moses has had those who proclaim him, for he has been read aloud every sabbath in the synagogues.”
text.DRB: For Moyses of old times hath in every city them that preach him in the Synagogues, where he is read every Sabboth.
text.Rotherham: For, Moses, out of ancient generation, hath, in every city, them who proclaim him; seeing, that in the synagogues, every sabbath, he is read.

text.KJV: Then pleased it the apostles and elders, with the whole church, to send chosen men of their own company to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas; namely, Judas surnamed Barsabas, and Silas, chief men among the brethren:
text.NABRE: Letter of the Apostles. Then the apostles and presbyters, in agreement with the whole church, decided to choose representatives and to send them to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas. The ones chosen were Judas, who was called Barsabbas, and Silas, leaders among the brothers.
text.NRSV: Then the apostles and the elders, with the consent of the whole church, decided to choose men from among their members and to send them to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas. They sent Judas called Barsabbas, and Silas, leaders among the brothers,
text.DRB: Then it pleased the Apostles and Ancients with the whole Church, to choose men out of them, & to send to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas, Judas, who was surnamed Barsabas, & Silas, chief men among the Brethren,
text.Rotherham: Then, seemed it good unto the Apostles and the Elders with the whole Assembly to send chosen men from among them unto Antioch, with Paul and Barnabas,—even Judas who is called Barsabbas, and Silas, men taking a lead among the brethren:

text.KJV: And they wrote letters by them after this manner; The apostles and elders and brethren send greeting unto the brethren which are of the Gentiles in Antioch and Syria and Cilicia:
text.NABRE: This is the letter delivered by them: “The apostles and the presbyters, your brothers, to the brothers in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia of Gentile origin: greetings.
text.NRSV: with the following letter: “The brothers, both the apostles and the elders, to the believers of Gentile origin in Antioch and Syria and Cilicia, greetings.
text.DRB: writing by their hands. The Apostles and Ancients, the brethren, to the brethren of the Gentiles that are at Antioch and in Syria and Cilicia, greeting.
text.Rotherham: writing through their hand—The Apostles and the Elder Brethren, unto the brethren throughout Antioch and Syria and Cilicia, who are from among the nations, wish joy!

text.KJV: Forasmuch as we have heard, that certain which went out from us have troubled you with words, subverting your souls, saying, Ye must be circumcised, and keep the law: to whom we gave no such commandment:
text.NABRE: Since we have heard that some of our number [who went out] without any mandate from us have upset you with their teachings and disturbed your peace of mind,
text.NRSV: Since we have heard that certain persons who have gone out from us, though with no instructions from us, have said things to disturb you and have unsettled your minds,
text.DRB: Because we have heard that certain going forth from us have troubled you with words subverting your souls, to whom we gave no commandment:
text.Rotherham: Inasmuch as we had heard that, certain from among us, had troubled you with words, dismantling your souls,—unto whom we had given no instructions,

text.KJV: It seemed good unto us, being assembled with one accord, to send chosen men unto you with our beloved Barnabas and Paul,
text.NABRE: we have with one accord decided to choose representatives and to send them to you along with our beloved Barnabas and Paul,
text.NRSV: we have decided unanimously to choose representatives and send them to you, along with our beloved Barnabas and Paul,
text.DRB: It hath pleased us being gathered in one, to choose out men & to send them unto you with our dearest Barnabas and Paul,
text.Rotherham: It seemed good unto us, coming to be of one accord, that we should choose men and send them unto you, with our beloved Barnabas and Paul,—

text.KJV: Men that have hazarded their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: who have dedicated their lives to the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.NRSV: who have risked their lives for the sake of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: men that have given their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: men who have given up their lives in behalf of the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.KJV: We have sent therefore Judas and Silas, who shall also tell you the same things by mouth.
text.NABRE: So we are sending Judas and Silas who will also convey this same message by word of mouth:
text.NRSV: We have therefore sent Judas and Silas, who themselves will tell you the same things by word of mouth.
text.DRB: We have sent therefore Judas and Silas who themselves also will in words report unto you the same things.
text.Rotherham: We have sent, therefore, Judas and Silas, who also, themselves, by word of mouth, can tell you the same things.

text.KJV: For it seemed good to the Holy Ghost, and to us, to lay upon you no greater burden than these necessary things;
text.NABRE: ‘It is the decision of the holy Spirit and of us not to place on you any burden beyond these necessities,
text.NRSV: For it has seemed good to the Holy Spirit and to us to impose on you no further burden than these essentials:
text.DRB: For it hath seemed good to the Holy Ghost and to us, to lay no further burden upon you than these necessary things:
text.Rotherham: For it hath seemed good unto the Holy Spirit and unto us, no greater burden, to be laying upon you, than these necessary things:—

text.KJV: That ye abstain from meats offered to idols, and from blood, and from things strangled, and from fornication: from which if ye keep yourselves, ye shall do well. Fare ye well.
text.NABRE: namely, to abstain from meat sacrificed to idols, from blood, from meats of strangled animals, and from unlawful marriage. If you keep free of these, you will be doing what is right. Farewell.’”
text.NRSV: that you abstain from what has been sacrificed to idols and from blood and from what is strangled and from fornication. If you keep yourselves from these, you will do well. Farewell.”
text.DRB: That you abstain from the things immolated to idols, and blood, and that which is strangled, and fornication, from the which things keeping yourselves, you shall do well. Fare ye well.
text.Rotherham: To be abstaining from idol sacrifices, and from blood, and from what is strangled, and from fornication,—From which, if ye keep yourselves, ye shall prosper. Fare ye well.

text.KJV: So when they were dismissed, they came to Antioch: and when they had gathered the multitude together, they delivered the epistle:
text.NABRE: Delegates at Antioch. And so they were sent on their journey. Upon their arrival in Antioch they called the assembly together and delivered the letter.
text.NRSV: So they were sent off and went down to Antioch. When they gathered the congregation together, they delivered the letter.
text.DRB: They therefore being dismissed went down to Antioch: and gathering the multitude, delivered the epistle.
text.Rotherham: They, therefore, being let go, came down unto Antioch; and, having gathered together the throng, delivered the letter,

text.KJV: Which when they had read, they rejoiced for the consolation.
text.NABRE: When the people read it, they were delighted with the exhortation.
text.NRSV: When its members read it, they rejoiced at the exhortation.
text.DRB: Which when they had read, they rejoiced upon the consolation:
text.Rotherham: and, when they read it, they rejoiced for the consolation.

text.KJV: And Judas and Silas, being prophets also themselves, exhorted the brethren with many words, and confirmed them.
text.NABRE: Judas and Silas, who were themselves prophets, exhorted and strengthened the brothers with many words.
text.NRSV: Judas and Silas, who were themselves prophets, said much to encourage and strengthen the believers.
text.DRB: but Judas and Silas, themselves also being prophets, with many words comforted the brethren, and confirmed them.
text.Rotherham: And, both Judas and Silas, being themselves also prophets, with much discourse, consoled and confirmed the brethren.

text.KJV: And after they had tarried there a space, they were let go in peace from the brethren unto the apostles.
text.NABRE: After they had spent some time there, they were sent off with greetings of peace from the brothers to those who had commissioned them.
text.NRSV: After they had been there for some time, they were sent off in peace by the believers to those who had sent them.
text.DRB: And having spent some time there, they were with peace dismissed of the brethren unto them that had sent them.
text.Rotherham: And, when they had spent a time, they were let go, in peace, from the brethren, unto them who had sent them.

text.KJV: Notwithstanding it pleased Silas to abide there still.
text.DRB: But it seemed good unto Silas to remain there: and Judas departed alone:

text.KJV: Paul also and Barnabas continued in Antioch, teaching and preaching the word of the Lord, with many others also.
text.NABRE: But Paul and Barnabas remained in Antioch, teaching and proclaiming with many others the word of the Lord.
text.NRSV: But Paul and Barnabas remained in Antioch, and there, with many others, they taught and proclaimed the word of the Lord.
text.DRB: and Paul and Barnabas tarried at Antioch, teaching and evangelizing with many others the word of our Lord.
text.Rotherham: But, Paul and Barnabas, tarried in Antioch, teaching and telling the joyful tidings,—along with many others also,—of the word of the Lord.

text.KJV: And some days after Paul said unto Barnabas, Let us go again and visit our brethren in every city where we have preached the word of the Lord, and see how they do.
text.NABRE: After some time, Paul said to Barnabas, “Come, let us make a return visit to see how the brothers are getting on in all the cities where we proclaimed the word of the Lord.”
text.NRSV: After some days Paul said to Barnabas, “Come, let us return and visit the believers in every city where we proclaimed the word of the Lord and see how they are doing.”
text.DRB: And after certain days, Paul said to Barnabas, let us return and visit our brethren in all cities wherein we have preached the word of our Lord, how they do.
text.Rotherham: And, after certain days, Paul, said unto Barnabas—Let us now return, and visit the brethren in every city in which we have declared the word of the Lord, and see how they are.

text.KJV: And Barnabas determined to take with them John, whose surname was Mark.
text.NABRE: Barnabas wanted to take with them also John, who was called Mark,
text.NRSV: Barnabas wanted to take with them John called Mark.
text.DRB: And Barnabas would have taken with them John also that was surnamed Mark.
text.Rotherham: And, Barnabas, was minded to take with them John also, called Mark;

text.KJV: But Paul thought not good to take him with them, who departed from them from Pamphylia, and went not with them to the work.
text.NABRE: but Paul insisted that they should not take with them someone who had deserted them at Pamphylia and who had not continued with them in their work.
text.NRSV: But Paul decided not to take with them one who had deserted them in Pamphylia and had not accompanied them in the work.
text.DRB: But Paul desired that he (as who had departed from them out of Pamphylia, & had not gone with them to the work) might not be received.
text.Rotherham: but Paul deemed it right, as to him who had withdrawn from them, back from Pamphylia, and had not gone with them unto the work, not to be taking with them, this, man.

text.KJV: And the contention was so sharp between them, that they departed asunder one from the other: and so Barnabas took Mark, and sailed unto Cyprus;
text.NABRE: So sharp was their disagreement that they separated. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus.
text.NRSV: The disagreement became so sharp that they parted company; Barnabas took Mark with him and sailed away to Cyprus.
text.DRB: And there rose a dissension, so that they departed one from another, & that, Barnabas indeed taking Mark sailed to Cypres.
text.Rotherham: And there arose an angry feeling, so that they separated one from the other: and, Barnabas, taking Mark, sailed away unto Cyprus,—

text.KJV: And Paul chose Silas, and departed, being recommended by the brethren unto the grace of God.
text.NABRE: But Paul chose Silas and departed after being commended by the brothers to the grace of the Lord.
text.NRSV: But Paul chose Silas and set out, the believers commending him to the grace of the Lord.
text.DRB: But Paul choosing Silas departed, being delivered of the brethren to the grace of God.
text.Rotherham: whereas, Paul, choosing Silas, went forth, committed unto the favour of the Lord by the brethren,

text.KJV: And he went through Syria and Cilicia, confirming the churches.
text.NABRE: He traveled through Syria and Cilicia bringing strength to the churches.
text.NRSV: He went through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
text.DRB: And he walked through Syria and Cilicia, confirming the Churches: commanding them to keep the precepts of the Apostles & the Ancients.
text.Rotherham: and proceeded to pass through Syria and Cilicia, confirming the assemblies.

text.NRSV: Paul went on also to Derbe and to Lystra, where there was a disciple named Timothy, the son of a Jewish woman who was a believer; but his father was a Greek.
text.Rotherham: And he came even unto Derbe, and unto Lystra; and lo! a certain disciple, was there, by name Timothy, son of a believing Jewish woman, but whose father was a Greek,—
text.KJV: Then came he to Derbe and Lystra: and, behold, a certain disciple was there, named Timotheus, the son of a certain woman, which was a Jewess, and believed; but his father was a Greek:
text.NABRE: He reached [also] Derbe and Lystra where there was a disciple named Timothy, the son of a Jewish woman who was a believer, but his father was a Greek.
text.DRB: And he came to Derbe and Lystra. And behold, there was a certain Disciple there named Timothee, the son of a widow woman that believed, of a father a Gentile.

text.NRSV: He was well spoken of by the believers in Lystra and Iconium.
text.Rotherham: who was well-attested by the brethren in Lystra, and Iconium.
text.KJV: Which was well reported of by the brethren that were at Lystra and Iconium.
text.NABRE: The brothers in Lystra and Iconium spoke highly of him,
text.DRB: To this man the brethren that were in Lystra and Iconium, gave a good testimony.

text.NRSV: Paul wanted Timothy to accompany him; and he took him and had him circumcised because of the Jews who were in those places, for they all knew that his father was a Greek.
text.Rotherham: The same, would Paul have go forth with him, and took and circumcised him, on account of the Jews who were in those places; for they one and all knew that, his father, was, a Greek.
text.KJV: Him would Paul have to go forth with him; and took and circumcised him because of the Jews which were in those quarters: for they knew all that his father was a Greek.
text.NABRE: and Paul wanted him to come along with him. On account of the Jews of that region, Paul had him circumcised, for they all knew that his father was a Greek.
text.DRB: Him Paul would have to go forth with him: and taking him he circumcised him because of the Jews that were in those places. For they all knew that his father was a Gentile.

text.NRSV: As they went from town to town, they delivered to them for observance the decisions that had been reached by the apostles and elders who were in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, as they passed through the cities, they were delivering unto them, for observance, the decrees which had been decided upon by the Apostles and Elders who were in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: And as they went through the cities, they delivered them the decrees for to keep, that were ordained of the apostles and elders which were at Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: As they traveled from city to city, they handed on to the people for observance the decisions reached by the apostles and presbyters in Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And when they passed through the cities, they delivered unto them to keep the decrees that were decreed of the Apostles and Ancients which were at Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: So the churches were strengthened in the faith and increased in numbers daily.
text.Rotherham: The assemblies, therefore, were being confirmed in the faith, and increasing in number, every day.
text.KJV: And so were the churches established in the faith, and increased in number daily.
text.NABRE: Day after day the churches grew stronger in faith and increased in number.
text.DRB: And the Churches were confirmed in faith, and did abound in number daily.

text.NRSV: They went through the region of Phrygia and Galatia, having been forbidden by the Holy Spirit to speak the word in Asia.
text.Rotherham: And they passed through the Phrygian and Galatian country, being forbidden by the Holy Spirit to speak the word in Asia;
text.KJV: Now when they had gone throughout Phrygia and the region of Galatia, and were forbidden of the Holy Ghost to preach the word in Asia,
text.NABRE: Through Asia Minor. They traveled through the Phrygian and Galatian territory because they had been prevented by the holy Spirit from preaching the message in the province of Asia.
text.DRB: And passing through Phrygia and the country of Galatia, they were forbidden by the Holy Ghost to preach the word in Asia.

text.NRSV: When they had come opposite Mysia, they attempted to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus did not allow them;
text.Rotherham: but, coming along Mysia, they were attempting to journey into Bithynia,—and, the Spirit of Jesus, suffered them not;
text.KJV: After they were come to Mysia, they assayed to go into Bithynia: but the Spirit suffered them not.
text.NABRE: When they came to Mysia, they tried to go on into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus did not allow them,
text.DRB: And when they were come into Mysia, they attempted to go into Bithynia, and the Spirit of Jesus permitted them not.

text.NRSV: so, passing by Mysia, they went down to Troas.
text.Rotherham: but, passing by Mysia, they came down unto Troas.
text.KJV: And they passing by Mysia came down to Troas.
text.NABRE: so they crossed through Mysia and came down to Troas.
text.DRB: And when they had passed through Mysia, they went down to Troas:

text.NRSV: During the night Paul had a vision: there stood a man of Macedonia pleading with him and saying, “Come over to Macedonia and help us.”
text.Rotherham: And, a vision, by night, unto Paul appeared:—A man of Macedonia, there was, standing and beseeching him, and saying—Come over into Macedonia, and bring us succour!
text.KJV: And a vision appeared to Paul in the night; There stood a man of Macedonia, and prayed him, saying, Come over into Macedonia, and help us.
text.NABRE: During [the] night Paul had a vision. A Macedonian stood before him and implored him with these words, “Come over to Macedonia and help us.”
text.DRB: and a vision by night was shewed to Paul. There was a certain man of Macedonia standing and beseeching him, and saying: Pass into Macedonia, and help us.

text.NRSV: When he had seen the vision, we immediately tried to cross over to Macedonia, being convinced that God had called us to proclaim the good news to them.
text.Rotherham: Now, when, the vision, he had seen, straightway, we sought to go forth unto Macedonia, concluding that God, had summoned us to tell the glad tidings unto them.
text.KJV: And after he had seen the vision, immediately we endeavoured to go into Macedonia, assuredly gathering that the Lord had called us for to preach the gospel unto them.
text.NABRE: When he had seen the vision, we sought passage to Macedonia at once, concluding that God had called us to proclaim the good news to them.
text.DRB: And as soon as he had seen the vision, forthwith we sought to go into Macedonia, being assured that God had called us to evangelize to them.

text.NRSV: We set sail from Troas and took a straight course to Samothrace, the following day to Neapolis,
text.Rotherham: Setting sail, therefore, from Troas, we ran straight into Samothracia, and, on the morrow, unto New City,
text.KJV: Therefore loosing from Troas, we came with a straight course to Samothracia, and the next day to Neapolis;
text.NABRE: Into Europe. We set sail from Troas, making a straight run for Samothrace, and on the next day to Neapolis,
text.DRB: And sailing from Troas, we came with a straight course to Samothracia, and the day following to Neapolis:

text.NRSV: and from there to Philippi, which is a leading city of the district of Macedonia and a Roman colony. We remained in this city for some days.
text.Rotherham: and, from thence, unto Philippi,—which, indeed, is the first city of the part of Macedonia—a colony. And we were, in this city, spending certain days;
text.KJV: And from thence to Philippi, which is the chief city of that part of Macedonia, and a colony: and we were in that city abiding certain days.
text.NABRE: and from there to Philippi, a leading city in that district of Macedonia and a Roman colony. We spent some time in that city.
text.DRB: and from thence to Philippi, which is the first city of the part of Macedonia, a colonia. And we were in this city certain days, abiding.

text.NRSV: On the sabbath day we went outside the gate by the river, where we supposed there was a place of prayer; and we sat down and spoke to the women who had gathered there.
text.Rotherham: and on the day of rest, we went forth outside the gate, beside a river, where we supposed there was a place of prayer,—and, sitting down, we went on to speak unto the women who had come together.
text.KJV: And on the sabbath we went out of the city by a river side, where prayer was wont to be made; and we sat down, and spake unto the women which resorted thither.
text.NABRE: On the sabbath we went outside the city gate along the river where we thought there would be a place of prayer. We sat and spoke with the women who had gathered there.
text.DRB: And upon the day of the Sabboths, we went forth without the gate beside a river, where it seemed that there was prayer: & sitting we spake to the women that were assembled.

text.NRSV: A certain woman named Lydia, a worshiper of God, was listening to us; she was from the city of Thyatira and a dealer in purple cloth. The Lord opened her heart to listen eagerly to what was said by Paul.
text.Rotherham: And, a certain woman, by name Lydia, a seller of purple, of the city of Thyatira, devout towards God, was hearkening, whose heart, the Lord, fully opened, to be giving heed unto the things being spoken by Paul.
text.KJV: And a certain woman named Lydia, a seller of purple, of the city of Thyatira, which worshipped God, heard us: whose heart the Lord opened, that she attended unto the things which were spoken of Paul.
text.NABRE: One of them, a woman named Lydia, a dealer in purple cloth, from the city of Thyatira, a worshiper of God, listened, and the Lord opened her heart to pay attention to what Paul was saying.
text.DRB: And a certain woman named Lydia, a seller of purple of the city of the Thyatirians, one that worshipped God, did hear: whose heart our Lord opened to attend to those things which were said of Paul.

text.NRSV: When she and her household were baptized, she urged us, saying, “If you have judged me to be faithful to the Lord, come and stay at my home.” And she prevailed upon us.
text.Rotherham: And, when she was immersed, and her house, she besought [us], saying—If ye have judged me to be a believer in the Lord, come into my house, and abide [there]. And she constrained us.
text.KJV: And when she was baptized, and her household, she besought us, saying, If ye have judged me to be faithful to the Lord, come into my house, and abide there. And she constrained us.
text.NABRE: After she and her household had been baptized, she offered us an invitation, “If you consider me a believer in the Lord, come and stay at my home,” and she prevailed on us.
text.DRB: And when she was baptized, & her house, she besought us, saying: If you have judged me to be faithful to our Lord, enter into my house & tarry. And she constrained us.

text.NRSV: One day, as we were going to the place of prayer, we met a slave-girl who had a spirit of divination and brought her owners a great deal of money by fortune-telling.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as we were on our way unto the place of prayer, a certain damsel, having a spirit of Python, met us,—who, indeed, much gain, was presenting unto her masters, by divining.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, as we went to prayer, a certain damsel possessed with a spirit of divination met us, which brought her masters much gain by soothsaying:
text.NABRE: Imprisonment at Philippi. As we were going to the place of prayer, we met a slave girl with an oracular spirit, who used to bring a large profit to her owners through her fortune-telling.
text.DRB: And it came to pass as we went to prayer, a certain wench having a Pythonical spirit, met us, that brought great gain to her masters by divining.

text.NRSV: While she followed Paul and us, she would cry out, “These men are slaves of the Most High God, who proclaim to you a way of salvation.”
text.Rotherham: The same, following after Paul and us, kept crying aloud, saying—These men, are servants of the Most High God,—who, indeed, are declaring unto you a way of salvation.
text.KJV: The same followed Paul and us, and cried, saying, These men are the servants of the most high God, which shew unto us the way of salvation.
text.NABRE: She began to follow Paul and us, shouting, “These people are slaves of the Most High God, who proclaim to you a way of salvation.”
text.DRB: This same following Paul & us, cried saying: These men are the servants of the high God, which preach unto you the way of salvation.

text.NRSV: She kept doing this for many days. But Paul, very much annoyed, turned and said to the spirit, “I order you in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her.” And it came out that very hour.
text.Rotherham: And, this, she continued to do for many days. But Paul, worn out, and turning unto the spirit, said—I charge thee, in the name of Jesus Christ, to come out from her. And it came out the same hour.
text.KJV: And this did she many days. But Paul, being grieved, turned and said to the spirit, I command thee in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her. And he came out the same hour.
text.NABRE: She did this for many days. Paul became annoyed, turned, and said to the spirit, “I command you in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her.” Then it came out at that moment.
text.DRB: And this she did many days. And Paul being sorry, and turning, said to the spirit: I command thee in the name of Jesus Christ to go out from her. And he went out the same hour.

text.NRSV: But when her owners saw that their hope of making money was gone, they seized Paul and Silas and dragged them into the marketplace before the authorities.
text.Rotherham: And, her masters, seeing that their hope of gain had gone out, laying hold on Paul and Silas, dragged them into the market-place, unto the rulers;
text.KJV: And when her masters saw that the hope of their gains was gone, they caught Paul and Silas, and drew them into the marketplace unto the rulers,
text.NABRE: When her owners saw that their hope of profit was gone, they seized Paul and Silas and dragged them to the public square before the local authorities.
text.DRB: But her masters seeing that the hope of their gain was gone, apprehending Paul and Silas, brought them into the market place to the Princes:

text.NRSV: When they had brought them before the magistrates, they said, “These men are disturbing our city; they are Jews
text.Rotherham: and, leading them forward unto the magistrates, said—These men, are exceedingly troubling our city, they, being Jews,
text.KJV: And brought them to the magistrates, saying, These men, being Jews, do exceedingly trouble our city,
text.NABRE: They brought them before the magistrates and said, “These people are Jews and are disturbing our city
text.DRB: and presenting them to the magistrates, they said: These men trouble our city, being Jews:

text.NRSV: and are advocating customs that are not lawful for us as Romans to adopt or observe.”
text.Rotherham: And are declaring customs, which it is not allowable for us either to accept or to observe, being Romans.
text.KJV: And teach customs, which are not lawful for us to receive, neither to observe, being Romans.
text.NABRE: and are advocating customs that are not lawful for us Romans to adopt or practice.”
text.DRB: and they preach a fashion which it is not lawful for us to receive, nor do, being Romans.

text.NRSV: The crowd joined in attacking them, and the magistrates had them stripped of their clothing and ordered them to be beaten with rods.
text.Rotherham: And the multitude rose up together against them, and, the magistrates, rending off them their mantles, were giving orders to beat them with rods;
text.KJV: And the multitude rose up together against them: and the magistrates rent off their clothes, and commanded to beat them.
text.NABRE: The crowd joined in the attack on them, and the magistrates had them stripped and ordered them to be beaten with rods.
text.DRB: And the people ran against them: and the magistrates tearing their coats, commanded them to be beaten with rods.

text.NRSV: After they had given them a severe flogging, they threw them into prison and ordered the jailer to keep them securely.
text.Rotherham: and, laying upon them many stripes, they thrust them into prison, charging the prison-keeper, safely, to be keeping them:
text.KJV: And when they had laid many stripes upon them, they cast them into prison, charging the jailor to keep them safely:
text.NABRE: After inflicting many blows on them, they threw them into prison and instructed the jailer to guard them securely.
text.DRB: And when they had laid many stripes upon them, they did cast them into prison, commanding the keeper that he should keep them diligently.
