text.KJV: And they drew Alexander out of the multitude, the Jews putting him forward. And Alexander beckoned with the hand, and would have made his defence unto the people.
text.NABRE: Some of the crowd prompted Alexander, as the Jews pushed him forward, and Alexander signaled with his hand that he wished to explain something to the gathering.
text.DRB: And of the multitude they drew forth Alexander, the Jews thrusting him forward. But Alexander with his hand desiring silence, would have given the people satisfaction.

text.NRSV: But when they recognized that he was a Jew, for about two hours all of them shouted in unison, “Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!”
text.Rotherham: But, recognizing that he was a Jew, one voice arose from all for about two hours, as they cried aloud—Great, is Diana of the Ephesians!
text.KJV: But when they knew that he was a Jew, all with one voice about the space of two hours cried out, Great is Diana of the Ephesians.
text.NABRE: But when they recognized that he was a Jew, they all shouted in unison, for about two hours, “Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!”
text.DRB: Whom as soon as they perceived to be a Jew, there was made one voice of all, almost for the space of two hours crying out: Great is Diana of the Ephesians.

text.NRSV: But when the town clerk had quieted the crowd, he said, “Citizens of Ephesus, who is there that does not know that the city of the Ephesians is the temple keeper of the great Artemis and of the statue that fell from heaven?
text.Rotherham: Howbeit the town-clerk, having calmed the multitude, saith—Ephesians! why, who is there of mankind that doth not acknowledge the city of Ephesians to be temple-keeper of the Great Diana, and of the [image] that fell from Jupiter?
text.KJV: And when the townclerk had appeased the people, he said, Ye men of Ephesus, what man is there that knoweth not how that the city of the Ephesians is a worshipper of the great goddess Diana, and of the image which fell down from Jupiter?
text.NABRE: Finally the town clerk restrained the crowd and said, “You Ephesians, what person is there who does not know that the city of the Ephesians is the guardian of the temple of the great Artemis and of her image that fell from the sky?
text.DRB: And when the Scribe had appeased the multitudes, he saith: Ye men of Ephesus, for what man is there that knoweth not the city of the Ephesians to be a worshipper of great Diana, and Jupiter's child?

text.NRSV: Since these things cannot be denied, you ought to be quiet and do nothing rash.
text.Rotherham: As these things, then, cannot be denied, it is needful that ye be calmed at once, and, nothing rash, be doing.
text.KJV: Seeing then that these things cannot be spoken against, ye ought to be quiet, and to do nothing rashly.
text.NABRE: Since these things are undeniable, you must calm yourselves and not do anything rash.
text.DRB: Forasmuch therefore as these things cannot be gainsaid, you must be quieted, and do nothing rashly.

text.NRSV: You have brought these men here who are neither temple robbers nor blasphemers of our goddess.
text.Rotherham: For ye have brought these men, neither as temple-robbers, nor as defaming our goddess.
text.KJV: For ye have brought hither these men, which are neither robbers of churches, nor yet blasphemers of your goddess.
text.NABRE: The men you brought here are not temple robbers, nor have they insulted our goddess.
text.DRB: For you have brought these men, being neither sacrilegious, nor blaspheming your goddess.

text.NRSV: If therefore Demetrius and the artisans with him have a complaint against anyone, the courts are open, and there are proconsuls; let them bring charges there against one another.
text.Rotherham: If then, Demetrius, and the craftsmen with him, have against anyone an accusation, courts, are being held, and there are, proconsuls: let them accuse one another!
text.KJV: Wherefore if Demetrius, and the craftsmen which are with him, have a matter against any man, the law is open, and there are deputies: let them implead one another.
text.NABRE: If Demetrius and his fellow craftsmen have a complaint against anyone, courts are in session, and there are proconsuls. Let them bring charges against one another.
text.DRB: But if Demetrius & the artificers that are with him, have matter to say against any man, there are Courts kept in the common place, & there are Proconsuls; let them accuse one another.

text.NRSV: If there is anything further you want to know, it must be settled in the regular assembly.
text.Rotherham: But, if after, something further, ye are seeking, in the regular assembly shall it be settled.
text.KJV: But if ye enquire any thing concerning other matters, it shall be determined in a lawful assembly.
text.NABRE: If you have anything further to investigate, let the matter be settled in the lawful assembly,
text.DRB: And if you ask any other matter, it may be resolved in a lawful assembly.

text.NRSV: For we are in danger of being charged with rioting today, since there is no cause that we can give to justify this commotion.”
text.Rotherham: For we are, even in danger of being accused of riot, concerning this day, no cause at all, existing, by reference to which we shall be able to give a reason for this concourse.
text.KJV: For we are in danger to be called in question for this day’s uproar, there being no cause whereby we may give an account of this concourse.
text.NABRE: for, as it is, we are in danger of being charged with rioting because of today’s conduct. There is no cause for it. We shall [not] be able to give a reason for this demonstration.” With these words he dismissed the assembly.
text.DRB: For we are in danger also to be accused for this day's sedition: whereas there is no man guilty by whom we may give an account of this concourse. And when he had said these things, he dismissed the assembly.

text.NRSV: When he had said this, he dismissed the assembly.
text.Rotherham: And, these things, having said, he dismissed the assembly.
text.KJV: And when he had thus spoken, he dismissed the assembly.

text.NRSV: After the uproar had ceased, Paul sent for the disciples; and after encouraging them and saying farewell, he left for Macedonia.
text.Rotherham: But, after the tumult had ceased, Paul, sending for the disciples and exhorting them, took leave, and went forth to be journeying unto Macedonia.
text.KJV: And after the uproar was ceased, Paul called unto him the disciples, and embraced them, and departed for to go into Macedonia.
text.NABRE: When the disturbance was over, Paul had the disciples summoned and, after encouraging them, he bade them farewell and set out on his journey to Macedonia.
text.DRB: And after that the tumult was ceased, Paul calling the Disciples, and exhorting them, took his leave, and set forward to go into Macedonia.

text.NRSV: When he had gone through those regions and had given the believers much encouragement, he came to Greece,
text.Rotherham: Passing through those parts, however, and exhorting them with much discourse, he came into Greece;
text.KJV: And when he had gone over those parts, and had given them much exhortation, he came into Greece,
text.NABRE: As he traveled throughout those regions, he provided many words of encouragement for them. Then he arrived in Greece,
text.DRB: And when he had walked through those parts, & had exhorted them with much speech, he came to Greece:

text.NRSV: where he stayed for three months. He was about to set sail for Syria when a plot was made against him by the Jews, and so he decided to return through Macedonia.
text.Rotherham: and, spending three months, when a plot was laid against him by the Jews, as he was about to sail to Syria, he determined to turn back through Macedonia.
text.KJV: And there abode three months. And when the Jews laid wait for him, as he was about to sail into Syria, he purposed to return through Macedonia.
text.NABRE: where he stayed for three months. But when a plot was made against him by the Jews as he was about to set sail for Syria, he decided to return by way of Macedonia.
text.DRB: where when he had spent three months, the Jews laid wait for him as he was about to sail into Syria: and he had counsel to return through Macedonia.

text.NRSV: He was accompanied by Sopater son of Pyrrhus from Beroea, by Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, by Gaius from Derbe, and by Timothy, as well as by Tychicus and Trophimus from Asia.
text.Rotherham: Now there were accompanying him, Sopater, son of Pyrrhus a Beroean; and of the Thessalonians, Aristarchus and Secundus; and Gaius of Derbe and Timothy; and, of Asia, Tychicus and Trophimus.
text.KJV: And there accompanied him into Asia Sopater of Berea; and of the Thessalonians, Aristarchus and Secundus; and Gaius of Derbe, and Timotheus; and of Asia, Tychicus and Trophimus.
text.NABRE: Return to Troas. Sopater, the son of Pyrrhus, from Beroea, accompanied him, as did Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, Gaius from Derbe, Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus from Asia
text.DRB: And there accompanied him Sosipater of Pyrrhus, of Beroea: and of Thessalonians, Aristarchus, and Secundus: and Caius of Derbe, and Timothee: and of Asia, Tychicus and Trophimus.

text.NRSV: They went ahead and were waiting for us in Troas;
text.Rotherham: And, these, came and were waiting for us at Troas.
text.KJV: These going before tarried for us at Troas.
text.NABRE: who went on ahead and waited for us at Troas.
text.DRB: These going before, stayed for us at Troas:

text.NRSV: but we sailed from Philippi after the days of Unleavened Bread, and in five days we joined them in Troas, where we stayed for seven days.
text.Rotherham: And, we, sailed forth, after the days of unleavened bread, from Philippi, and came unto them in Troas in five days, where we tarried seven days.
text.KJV: And we sailed away from Philippi after the days of unleavened bread, and came unto them to Troas in five days; where we abode seven days.
text.NABRE: We sailed from Philippi after the feast of Unleavened Bread, and rejoined them five days later in Troas, where we spent a week.
text.DRB: But we sailed after the days of Azymes from Philippi, and came to them unto Troas in five days, where we abode seven days.

text.NRSV: On the first day of the week, when we met to break bread, Paul was holding a discussion with them; since he intended to leave the next day, he continued speaking until midnight.
text.Rotherham: And, on the first of the week, when we were gathered together to break bread, Paul went on to discourse with them, being about to depart on the morrow; and he prolonged his discourse until midnight.
text.KJV: And upon the first day of the week, when the disciples came together to break bread, Paul preached unto them, ready to depart on the morrow; and continued his speech until midnight.
text.NABRE: Eutychus Restored to Life. On the first day of the week when we gathered to break bread, Paul spoke to them because he was going to leave on the next day, and he kept on speaking until midnight.
text.DRB: And in the first of the Sabboth when we were assembled to break bread, Paul disputed with them, being to depart on the morrow: and he continued the sermon until mid-night.

text.NRSV: There were many lamps in the room upstairs where we were meeting.
text.Rotherham: Now there were a good many torches in the upper room, where we were gathered together.
text.KJV: And there were many lights in the upper chamber, where they were gathered together.
text.NABRE: There were many lamps in the upstairs room where we were gathered,
text.DRB: And there were a great number of lamps in the upper chamber where we were assembled.

text.NRSV: A young man named Eutychus, who was sitting in the window, began to sink off into a deep sleep while Paul talked still longer. Overcome by sleep, he fell to the ground three floors below and was picked up dead.
text.Rotherham: And there sat, a certain young man by name Eutychus, in the window, who was getting overpowered by a deep sleep; and, while Paul was discoursing yet further, being overpowered by his sleep, he fell, from the third story, down, and was taken up dead.
text.KJV: And there sat in a window a certain young man named Eutychus, being fallen into a deep sleep: and as Paul was long preaching, he sunk down with sleep, and fell down from the third loft, and was taken up dead.
text.NABRE: and a young man named Eutychus who was sitting on the window sill was sinking into a deep sleep as Paul talked on and on. Once overcome by sleep, he fell down from the third story and when he was picked up, he was dead.
text.DRB: And a certain young man named Eutychus, sitting upon the window, whereas he was oppressed with heavy sleep: (Paul disputing long) driven by sleep, fell from the third loft down, and was taken up dead.

text.NRSV: But Paul went down, and bending over him took him in his arms, and said, “Do not be alarmed, for his life is in him.”
text.Rotherham: Going down, however, Paul fell upon him, and, embracing him, said—Be not making confusion; for, his soul, is, in him.
text.KJV: And Paul went down, and fell on him, and embracing him said, Trouble not yourselves; for his life is in him.
text.NABRE: Paul went down, threw himself upon him, and said as he embraced him, “Don’t be alarmed; there is life in him.”
text.DRB: To whom when Paul was gone down, he lay upon him: and embracing him he said: Be not troubled, for his soul is in him.

text.NRSV: Then Paul went upstairs, and after he had broken bread and eaten, he continued to converse with them until dawn; then he left.
text.Rotherham: And, going up, and breaking the loaf, and tasting,—for a good while also conversing, until dawn, thus, he departed.
text.KJV: When he therefore was come up again, and had broken bread, and eaten, and talked a long while, even till break of day, so he departed.
text.NABRE: Then he returned upstairs, broke the bread, and ate; after a long conversation that lasted until daybreak, he departed.
text.DRB: And going up and breaking bread and tasting, and having talked sufficiently to them until day light, so he departed.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile they had taken the boy away alive and were not a little comforted.
text.Rotherham: And they brought the boy alive, and were comforted beyond measure.
text.KJV: And they brought the young man alive, and were not a little comforted.
text.NABRE: And they took the boy away alive and were immeasurably comforted.
text.DRB: And they brought the lad alive, & were not a little comforted.

text.NRSV: We went ahead to the ship and set sail for Assos, intending to take Paul on board there; for he had made this arrangement, intending to go by land himself.
text.Rotherham: And, we, going forward unto the ship, set sail for Assos, from thence being about to take up Paul; for, so, had he arranged, being about, himself, to go on foot.
text.KJV: And we went before to ship, and sailed unto Assos, there intending to take in Paul: for so had he appointed, minding himself to go afoot.
text.NABRE: Journey to Miletus. We went ahead to the ship and set sail for Assos where we were to take Paul on board, as he had arranged, since he was going overland.
text.DRB: But we going up into the ship, sailed to Asson, from thence meaning to receive Paul; for so he had ordained, himself purposing to journey by land.

text.NRSV: When he met us in Assos, we took him on board and went to Mitylene.
text.Rotherham: And, when he fell in with us in Assos, we took him on board, and came into Mitylene;
text.KJV: And when he met with us at Assos, we took him in, and came to Mitylene.
text.NABRE: When he met us in Assos, we took him aboard and went on to Mitylene.
text.DRB: And when he had found us in Asson, taking him with us we came to Mitylene.

text.NRSV: We sailed from there, and on the following day we arrived opposite Chios. The next day we touched at Samos, and the day after that we came to Miletus.
text.Rotherham: and, from thence sailing away on the morrow, we came over against Chios, and, on the next day, we thrust aside into Samos, and, on the succeeding day, we came into Miletus.
text.KJV: And we sailed thence, and came the next day over against Chios; and the next day we arrived at Samos, and tarried at Trogyllium; and the next day we came to Miletus.
text.NABRE: We sailed away from there on the next day and reached a point off Chios, and a day later we reached Samos, and on the following day we arrived at Miletus.
text.DRB: And sailing thence, the day following we came over against Chios: and the other day we arrived at Samos: and the day following we came to Miletum.

text.NRSV: For Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus, so that he might not have to spend time in Asia; he was eager to be in Jerusalem, if possible, on the day of Pentecost.
text.Rotherham: For Paul had determined to sail past Ephesus, lest he should happen to lose time in Asia; for he hastened, if it were possible for him, against the day of Pentecost, to arrive, in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: For Paul had determined to sail by Ephesus, because he would not spend the time in Asia: for he hasted, if it were possible for him, to be at Jerusalem the day of Pentecost.
text.NABRE: Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus in order not to lose time in the province of Asia, for he was hurrying to be in Jerusalem, if at all possible, for the day of Pentecost.
text.DRB: For Paul had purposed to sail leaving Ephesus, lest any stay should be made him in Asia. For he hastened, if it were possible for him, to keep the day of Pentecost at Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: From Miletus he sent a message to Ephesus, asking the elders of the church to meet him.
text.Rotherham: But, from Miletus, he sent unto Ephesus, and called for the elders of the assembly.
text.KJV: And from Miletus he sent to Ephesus, and called the elders of the church.
text.NABRE: Paul’s Farewell Speech at Miletus. From Miletus he had the presbyters of the church at Ephesus summoned.
text.DRB: And sending from Miletum to Ephesus, he called the Ancients of the Church.

text.NRSV: When they came to him, he said to them: “You yourselves know how I lived among you the entire time from the first day that I set foot in Asia,
text.Rotherham: And, when they were come to him, he said unto them—Ye yourselves, well know, from the first day when I set foot in Asia, in what manner came to be with you all the time,
text.KJV: And when they were come to him, he said unto them, Ye know, from the first day that I came into Asia, after what manner I have been with you at all seasons,
text.NABRE: When they came to him, he addressed them, “You know how I lived among you the whole time from the day I first came to the province of Asia.
text.DRB: Who being come to him, and assembled together, he said to them: You know from the first day that I entered into Asia, in what manner I have been with you all the time,

text.NRSV: serving the Lord with all humility and with tears, enduring the trials that came to me through the plots of the Jews.
text.Rotherham: doing service unto the Lord, with all humility and tears, and temptations which befell me through the plots of the Jews:
text.KJV: Serving the Lord with all humility of mind, and with many tears, and temptations, which befell me by the lying in wait of the Jews:
text.NABRE: I served the Lord with all humility and with the tears and trials that came to me because of the plots of the Jews,
text.DRB: serving our Lord with all humility and tears, and tentations that did chance to me by the conspiracies of the Jews:

text.NRSV: I did not shrink from doing anything helpful, proclaiming the message to you and teaching you publicly and from house to house,
text.Rotherham: in what manner I in nowise shrank from announcing unto you anything that was profitable and teaching you publicly and in your homes;
text.KJV: And how I kept back nothing that was profitable unto you, but have shewed you, and have taught you publickly, and from house to house,
text.NABRE: and I did not at all shrink from telling you what was for your benefit, or from teaching you in public or in your homes.
text.DRB: How I have withdrawn nothing that was profitable, but that I preached it to you, and taught you openly and from house to house,

text.NRSV: as I testified to both Jews and Greeks about repentance toward God and faith toward our Lord Jesus.
text.Rotherham: bearing full witness, both to Jews and to Greeks, as to the repentance due unto God, and as to belief on our Lord Jesus.
text.KJV: Testifying both to the Jews, and also to the Greeks, repentance toward God, and faith toward our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: I earnestly bore witness for both Jews and Greeks to repentance before God and to faith in our Lord Jesus.
text.DRB: testifying to Jews and Gentiles penance toward God, and faith in our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: And now, as a captive to the Spirit, I am on my way to Jerusalem, not knowing what will happen to me there,
text.Rotherham: And, now, lo! I, bound in my spirit, am journeying unto Jerusalem; the things which therein shall befall me, not knowing,—
text.KJV: And now, behold, I go bound in the spirit unto Jerusalem, not knowing the things that shall befall me there:
text.NABRE: But now, compelled by the Spirit, I am going to Jerusalem. What will happen to me there I do not know,
text.DRB: And now behold, being bound by the spirit, I go to Jerusalem; not knowing what things shall befall me in it,

text.NRSV: except that the Holy Spirit testifies to me in every city that imprisonment and persecutions are waiting for me.
text.Rotherham: save that, the Holy Spirit, from city to city, doth bear me full witness, saying that, bonds and tribulations, await me.
text.KJV: Save that the Holy Ghost witnesseth in every city, saying that bonds and afflictions abide me.
text.NABRE: except that in one city after another the holy Spirit has been warning me that imprisonment and hardships await me.
text.DRB: but that the Holy Ghost throughout all cities doth protest to me, saying: that bands and tribulations abide me at Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: But I do not count my life of any value to myself, if only I may finish my course and the ministry that I received from the Lord Jesus, to testify to the good news of God’s grace.
text.Rotherham: But, for no cause whatever, am I making my life dear to myself, so that I may finish my course and the ministry which I have received from the Lord Jesus, to bear full witness as to the good news of the favour of God.
text.KJV: But none of these things move me, neither count I my life dear unto myself, so that I might finish my course with joy, and the ministry, which I have received of the Lord Jesus, to testify the gospel of the grace of God.
text.NABRE: Yet I consider life of no importance to me, if only I may finish my course and the ministry that I received from the Lord Jesus, to bear witness to the gospel of God’s grace.
text.DRB: But I fear none of these things, neither do I make my life more precious than myself, so that I may consummate my course & ministry which I received of our Lord Jesus, to testify the Gospel of the grace of God.

text.NRSV: “And now I know that none of you, among whom I have gone about proclaiming the kingdom, will ever see my face again.
text.Rotherham: And, now, lo! I, know that, no more, shall ye see my face,—ye all, among whom I have gone about proclaiming the kingdom.
text.KJV: And now, behold, I know that ye all, among whom I have gone preaching the kingdom of God, shall see my face no more.
text.NABRE: “But now I know that none of you to whom I preached the kingdom during my travels will ever see my face again.
text.DRB: And now behold I do know, that you shall no more see my face all you, through whom I have passed preaching the Kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: Therefore I declare to you this day that I am not responsible for the blood of any of you,
text.Rotherham: Wherefore I take you to witness, on this very day, that, pure am I from the blood of all;
text.KJV: Wherefore I take you to record this day, that I am pure from the blood of all men.
text.NABRE: And so I solemnly declare to you this day that I am not responsible for the blood of any of you,
text.DRB: Wherefore I take you to witness this present day that I am clear from the blood of all.

text.NRSV: for I did not shrink from declaring to you the whole purpose of God.
text.Rotherham: for I have not shrunk from announcing all the counsel of God unto you.
text.KJV: For I have not shunned to declare unto you all the counsel of God.
text.NABRE: for I did not shrink from proclaiming to you the entire plan of God.
text.DRB: For I have not spared to declare unto you all the counsel of God.

text.NRSV: Keep watch over yourselves and over all the flock, of which the Holy Spirit has made you overseers, to shepherd the church of God that he obtained with the blood of his own Son.
text.Rotherham: Be taking heed unto yourselves, and unto all the little flock in which the Holy Spirit hath set, you, as overseers,—to be shepherding the assembly of God which he hath acquired through means of the blood of his own.
text.KJV: Take heed therefore unto yourselves, and to all the flock, over the which the Holy Ghost hath made you overseers, to feed the church of God, which he hath purchased with his own blood.
text.NABRE: Keep watch over yourselves and over the whole flock of which the holy Spirit has appointed you overseers, in which you tend the church of God that he acquired with his own blood.
text.DRB: Take heed to yourselves and to the whole flock wherein the Holy Ghost hath placed you bishops, to rule the Church of God which he hath purchased with his own blood.

text.NRSV: I know that after I have gone, savage wolves will come in among you, not sparing the flock.
text.Rotherham: I, know, that there will enter, after my departure, grievous wolves into your midst, not sparing the little flock;
text.KJV: For I know this, that after my departing shall grievous wolves enter in among you, not sparing the flock.
text.NABRE: I know that after my departure savage wolves will come among you, and they will not spare the flock.
text.DRB: I know that after my departure there will ravening wolves enter in among you, not sparing the flock.

text.NRSV: Some even from your own group will come distorting the truth in order to entice the disciples to follow them.
text.Rotherham: and, from among your own selves, will arise men speaking distorted things, to draw away the disciples after themselves.
text.KJV: Also of your own selves shall men arise, speaking perverse things, to draw away disciples after them.
text.NABRE: And from your own group, men will come forward perverting the truth to draw the disciples away after them.
text.DRB: And out of your own selves shall arise men speaking perverse things, to draw away Disciples after themselves.

text.NRSV: Therefore be alert, remembering that for three years I did not cease night or day to warn everyone with tears.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, be on the watch, remembering that, for three years, night and day, I gave myself no rest, with tears, admonishing each one.
text.KJV: Therefore watch, and remember, that by the space of three years I ceased not to warn every one night and day with tears.
text.NABRE: So be vigilant and remember that for three years, night and day, I unceasingly admonished each of you with tears.
text.DRB: For the which cause be vigilant, keeping in memory that for three years night & day I ceased not with tears to admonish every one of you.

text.NRSV: And now I commend you to God and to the message of his grace, a message that is able to build you up and to give you the inheritance among all who are sanctified.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, I commend you unto the Lord, and unto his word of favour,—which is able to build up and give the inheritance among all the hallowed ones.
text.KJV: And now, brethren, I commend you to God, and to the word of his grace, which is able to build you up, and to give you an inheritance among all them which are sanctified.
text.NABRE: And now I commend you to God and to that gracious word of his that can build you up and give you the inheritance among all who are consecrated.
text.DRB: And now I commend you to God and to the word of his grace, who is able to edify, and to give inheritance in all the sanctified.

text.NRSV: I coveted no one’s silver or gold or clothing.
text.Rotherham: The silver or gold or apparel of no one, did I covet:
text.KJV: I have coveted no man’s silver, or gold, or apparel.
text.NABRE: I have never wanted anyone’s silver or gold or clothing.
text.DRB: No man's silver and gold or garment have I coveted.

text.NRSV: You know for yourselves that I worked with my own hands to support myself and my companions.
text.Rotherham: yourselves, acknowledge that, for my necessities and for those who were with me, hard wrought these hands!
text.KJV: Yea, ye yourselves know, that these hands have ministered unto my necessities, and to them that were with me.
text.NABRE: You know well that these very hands have served my needs and my companions.
text.DRB: Yourselves know that for such things as were needful for me and them that are with me, these hands have ministered.

text.NRSV: In all this I have given you an example that by such work we must support the weak, remembering the words of the Lord Jesus, for he himself said, ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive.’”
text.Rotherham: In all things, I gave you to understand, that, thus toiling, it behoves to be helping the weak, also to be keeping in mind the words of the Lord Jesus, that, he himself, said—Happy is it, rather to give than to receive!
text.KJV: I have shewed you all things, how that so labouring ye ought to support the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, how he said, It is more blessed to give than to receive.
text.NABRE: In every way I have shown you that by hard work of that sort we must help the weak, and keep in mind the words of the Lord Jesus who himself said, ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive.’”
text.DRB: I have shewed you all things, that so labouring, you must receive the weak, and remember the word of our Lord Jesus, because he said: It is a more blessed thing to give rather than to take.

text.NRSV: When he had finished speaking, he knelt down with them all and prayed.
text.Rotherham: And, these things saying, kneeling down with them all, he prayed.
text.KJV: And when he had thus spoken, he kneeled down, and prayed with them all.
text.NABRE: When he had finished speaking he knelt down and prayed with them all.
text.DRB: And when he had said these things, falling on his knees he prayed with all them.

text.NRSV: There was much weeping among them all; they embraced Paul and kissed him,
text.Rotherham: And they all wept much, and, falling upon Paul’s neck, they were tenderly kissing him;
text.KJV: And they all wept sore, and fell on Paul’s neck, and kissed him,
text.NABRE: They were all weeping loudly as they threw their arms around Paul and kissed him,
text.DRB: And there was great weeping made of all; and falling upon the neck of Paul, they kissed him,

text.NRSV: grieving especially because of what he had said, that they would not see him again. Then they brought him to the ship.
text.Rotherham: being most distressed for the word which he had said,—That, no more, should they, his face, behold. And they accompanied him unto the ship.
text.KJV: Sorrowing most of all for the words which he spake, that they should see his face no more. And they accompanied him unto the ship.
text.NABRE: for they were deeply distressed that he had said that they would never see his face again. Then they escorted him to the ship.
text.DRB: being sorry most of all for the word which he had said, that they should see his face no more. And they brought him going unto the ship.

text.NRSV: When we had parted from them and set sail, we came by a straight course to Cos, and the next day to Rhodes, and from there to Patara.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when we set sail, having torn ourselves from them, running a straight course, we came unto Cos, and, on the next day, unto Rhodes,—and from thence unto Patara;
text.KJV: And it came to pass, that after we were gotten from them, and had launched, we came with a straight course unto Coos, and the day following unto Rhodes, and from thence unto Patara:
text.NABRE: When we had taken leave of them we set sail, made a straight run for Cos, and on the next day for Rhodes, and from there to Patara.
text.DRB: And when it came to pass that we sailed, being carried from them, with a straight course we came to Coos, and the day following to Rhodes, and from thence to Patara.

text.NRSV: When we found a ship bound for Phoenicia, we went on board and set sail.
text.Rotherham: and, finding a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, going on board, we set sail.
text.KJV: And finding a ship sailing over unto Phenicia, we went aboard, and set forth.
text.NABRE: Finding a ship crossing to Phoenicia, we went on board and put out to sea.
text.DRB: And when we had found a ship that passed over to Phoenice, going up into it we sailed.

text.NRSV: We came in sight of Cyprus; and leaving it on our left, we sailed to Syria and landed at Tyre, because the ship was to unload its cargo there.
text.Rotherham: And, sighting Cyprus, and leaving it behind to the left, we held on our voyage to Syria, and landed at Tyre; for, there, the ship was to discharge her cargo.
text.KJV: Now when we had discovered Cyprus, we left it on the left hand, and sailed into Syria, and landed at Tyre: for there the ship was to unlade her burden.
text.NABRE: We caught sight of Cyprus but passed by it on our left and sailed on toward Syria and put in at Tyre where the ship was to unload cargo.
text.DRB: And when we were in the sight of Cypres, leaving it on the left hand, we sailed into Syria, and came to Tyre: for there the ship was to discharge her load.

text.NRSV: We looked up the disciples and stayed there for seven days. Through the Spirit they told Paul not to go on to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, finding up the disciples, we remained there seven days, and they, unto Paul, began to say, through the Spirit, that he would gain no footing in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: And finding disciples, we tarried there seven days: who said to Paul through the Spirit, that he should not go up to Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: There we sought out the disciples and stayed for a week. They kept telling Paul through the Spirit not to embark for Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And finding Disciples, we tarried there seven days: who said to Paul by the Spirit, that he should not go up to Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: When our days there were ended, we left and proceeded on our journey; and all of them, with wives and children, escorted us outside the city. There we knelt down on the beach and prayed
text.Rotherham: And, when it came to pass that we had completed the days, we went forth, and continued our journey, all of them accompanying us, with wives and children, as far as outside the city; and, kneeling down on the beach in prayer,
text.KJV: And when we had accomplished those days, we departed and went our way; and they all brought us on our way, with wives and children, till we were out of the city: and we kneeled down on the shore, and prayed.
text.NABRE: At the end of our stay we left and resumed our journey. All of them, women and children included, escorted us out of the city, and after kneeling on the beach to pray,
text.DRB: And the days being expired, departing we went forward, all bringing us on the way, with their wives and children, till we were out of the city: and falling upon our knees on the shore, we prayed.

text.NRSV: and said farewell to one another. Then we went on board the ship, and they returned home.
text.Rotherham: we tare ourselves from each other, and we went on board the ship, while, they, returned unto their homes.
text.KJV: And when we had taken our leave one of another, we took ship; and they returned home again.
text.NABRE: we bade farewell to one another. Then we boarded the ship, and they returned home.
text.DRB: And when we had bid one another farewell, we went up into the ship; and they returned unto their own.

text.NRSV: When we had finished the voyage from Tyre, we arrived at Ptolemais; and we greeted the believers and stayed with them for one day.
text.Rotherham: And, we, finishing the voyage from Tyre, reached Ptolemais, and, saluting the brethren, abode one day with them;
text.KJV: And when we had finished our course from Tyre, we came to Ptolemais, and saluted the brethren, and abode with them one day.
text.NABRE: Arrival at Ptolemais and Caesarea. We continued the voyage and came from Tyre to Ptolemais, where we greeted the brothers and stayed a day with them.
text.DRB: But we having ended the navigation, from Tyre came down to Ptolomais: and saluting the brethren, we tarried one day with them.

text.NRSV: The next day we left and came to Caesarea; and we went into the house of Philip the evangelist, one of the seven, and stayed with him.
text.Rotherham: and, on the morrow departing, we came to Caesarea, and, entering into the house of Philip the evangelist, who was of the seven, we abode with him.
text.KJV: And the next day we that were of Paul’s company departed, and came unto Cesarea: and we entered into the house of Philip the evangelist, which was one of the seven; and abode with him.
text.NABRE: On the next day we resumed the trip and came to Caesarea, where we went to the house of Philip the evangelist, who was one of the Seven, and stayed with him.
text.DRB: And the next day departing, we came to Cæsarea. And entering into the house of Philip the Evangelist, who was one of the seven, we tarried with him.

text.NRSV: He had four unmarried daughters who had the gift of prophecy.
text.Rotherham: Now—this man, had four virgin daughters, who used to prophesy.
text.KJV: And the same man had four daughters, virgins, which did prophesy.
text.NABRE: He had four virgin daughters gifted with prophecy.
text.DRB: And he had four daughters virgins, that did prophesy.

text.NRSV: While we were staying there for several days, a prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.
text.Rotherham: And, as we stayed on many days, there came down a certain man from Judaea, a prophet, by name Agabus;
text.KJV: And as we tarried there many days, there came down from Judea a certain prophet, named Agabus.
text.NABRE: We had been there several days when a prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.
text.DRB: And as we abode there for certain days, there came a certain Prophet from Jewry, named Agabus.

text.NRSV: He came to us and took Paul’s belt, bound his own feet and hands with it, and said, “Thus says the Holy Spirit, ‘This is the way the Jews in Jerusalem will bind the man who owns this belt and will hand him over to the Gentiles.’”
text.Rotherham: and, coming unto us, and taking Paul’s girdle, he bound his own feet and hands, and said—Thus, saith the Holy Spirit, the man whose this girdle is, shall the Jews thus bind in Jerusalem, and deliver up into the hands of Gentiles.
text.KJV: And when he was come unto us, he took Paul’s girdle, and bound his own hands and feet, and said, Thus saith the Holy Ghost, So shall the Jews at Jerusalem bind the man that owneth this girdle, and shall deliver him into the hands of the Gentiles.
text.NABRE: He came up to us, took Paul’s belt, bound his own feet and hands with it, and said, “Thus says the holy Spirit: This is the way the Jews will bind the owner of this belt in Jerusalem, and they will hand him over to the Gentiles.”
text.DRB: He, when he was come to us, took Paul's girdle: and binding his own feet & hands, he said: Thus saith the Holy Ghost: The man whose girdle this is, so shall the Jews bind in Jerusalem, and shall deliver him into the hands of the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: When we heard this, we and the people there urged him not to go up to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, when we heard these things, both, we, and they of the place began beseeching him, not to go up unto Jerusalem.
text.KJV: And when we heard these things, both we, and they of that place, besought him not to go up to Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: When we heard this, we and the local residents begged him not to go up to Jerusalem.
text.DRB: Which when we had heard, we and they that were of the same place, desired him that he would not go up to Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Then Paul answered, “What are you doing, weeping and breaking my heart? For I am ready not only to be bound but even to die in Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus.”
text.Rotherham: Then, answered Paul—What are ye doing, weeping and breaking my heart? For, I, not only to be bound, but to die in Jerusalem, am ready, in behalf of the name of the Lord Jesus.
text.KJV: Then Paul answered, What mean ye to weep and to break mine heart? for I am ready not to be bound only, but also to die at Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus.
text.NABRE: Then Paul replied, “What are you doing, weeping and breaking my heart? I am prepared not only to be bound but even to die in Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus.”
text.DRB: Then Paul answered, and said: What do you, weeping and afflicting my heart? for I am ready not only to be bound, but to die also in Jerusalem for the name of our Lord Jesus.

text.NRSV: Since he would not be persuaded, we remained silent except to say, “The Lord’s will be done.”
text.Rotherham: And, as he was not to be persuaded, we ceased, saying—the Lord’s, will be done!
text.KJV: And when he would not be persuaded, we ceased, saying, The will of the Lord be done.
text.NABRE: Since he would not be dissuaded we let the matter rest, saying, “The Lord’s will be done.”
text.DRB: And when we could not persuade him, we ceased, saying: The will of our Lord be done.

text.NRSV: After these days we got ready and started to go up to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, after these days, making ready what we had, we started to go up unto Jerusalem;
text.KJV: And after those days we took up our carriages, and went up to Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: Paul and James in Jerusalem. After these days we made preparations for our journey, then went up to Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And after these days, being prepared, we went up to Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Some of the disciples from Caesarea also came along and brought us to the house of Mnason of Cyprus, an early disciple, with whom we were to stay.
text.Rotherham: and there went certain also of the disciples from Caesarea, along with us, who were to introduce us unto one with whom we might be entertained, one Mnason of Cyprus, an early disciple.
text.KJV: There went with us also certain of the disciples of Cesarea, and brought with them one Mnason of Cyprus, an old disciple, with whom we should lodge.
text.NABRE: Some of the disciples from Caesarea came along to lead us to the house of Mnason, a Cypriot, a disciple of long standing, with whom we were to stay.
text.DRB: And there came also of the Disciples from Cæsarea with us, bringing with them one Jason a Cyprian (with whom we should lodge) an old Disciple.

text.NRSV: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the brothers welcomed us warmly.
text.Rotherham: Now, when we came to Jerusalem, the brethren, gladly welcomed us.
text.KJV: And when we were come to Jerusalem, the brethren received us gladly.
text.NABRE: When we reached Jerusalem the brothers welcomed us warmly.
text.DRB: And when we were come to Jerusalem, the brethren received us gladly.

text.NRSV: The next day Paul went with us to visit James; and all the elders were present.
text.Rotherham: And, on the next day, Paul went in with us unto James, and, all the elders, were present.
text.KJV: And the day following Paul went in with us unto James; and all the elders were present.
text.NABRE: The next day, Paul accompanied us on a visit to James, and all the presbyters were present.
text.DRB: And the day following Paul went in with us to James, and all the Ancients were assembled.

text.NRSV: After greeting them, he related one by one the things that God had done among the Gentiles through his ministry.
text.Rotherham: And, saluting them, he went on to narrate, one by one, each of the things which God had wrought among the nations through his ministry.
text.KJV: And when he had saluted them, he declared particularly what things God had wrought among the Gentiles by his ministry.
text.NABRE: He greeted them, then proceeded to tell them in detail what God had accomplished among the Gentiles through his ministry.
text.DRB: Whom when he had saluted, he told particularly what God had done among the Gentiles by his ministry.

text.NRSV: When they heard it, they praised God. Then they said to him, “You see, brother, how many thousands of believers there are among the Jews, and they are all zealous for the law.
text.Rotherham: And, they, having heard, began glorifying God; and they said to him—Thou observest, brother, how many myriads there are among the Jews who have believed, and, all, are zealous for the law.
text.KJV: And when they heard it, they glorified the Lord, and said unto him, Thou seest, brother, how many thousands of Jews there are which believe; and they are all zealous of the law:
text.NABRE: They praised God when they heard it but said to him, “Brother, you see how many thousands of believers there are from among the Jews, and they are all zealous observers of the law.
text.DRB: But they hearing it, magnified God, and said to him: Thou seest (brother) how many thousands there are among the Jews that have believed: and all are zelatours of the Law.

text.NRSV: They have been told about you that you teach all the Jews living among the Gentiles to forsake Moses, and that you tell them not to circumcise their children or observe the customs.
text.Rotherham: Now they have heard it rumoured concerning thee, that, an apostacy, art thou teaching, from Moses, unto all the Jews, who are among the nations, telling them not to be circumcising their children, nor, by the customs, to be walking.
text.KJV: And they are informed of thee, that thou teachest all the Jews which are among the Gentiles to forsake Moses, saying that they ought not to circumcise their children, neither to walk after the customs.
text.NABRE: They have been informed that you are teaching all the Jews who live among the Gentiles to abandon Moses and that you are telling them not to circumcise their children or to observe their customary practices.
text.DRB: But they have heard of thee that thou doest teach those Jews that are among the Gentiles, to depart from Moyses: saying that they ought not to circumcise their children, nor walk according to the custom.

text.NRSV: What then is to be done? They will certainly hear that you have come.
text.Rotherham: What, then, is it? at all events, they will hear that thou hast come.
text.KJV: What is it therefore? the multitude must needs come together: for they will hear that thou art come.
text.NABRE: What is to be done? They will surely hear that you have arrived.
text.DRB: What is it then? needs must the multitude assemble: for they will hear that thou art come.

text.NRSV: So do what we tell you. We have four men who are under a vow.
text.Rotherham: This, then, do, which unto thee, we say:—We have four men who have a vow upon themselves.
text.KJV: Do therefore this that we say to thee: We have four men which have a vow on them;
text.NABRE: So do what we tell you. We have four men who have taken a vow.
text.DRB: Do this therefore which we tell thee. There are with us four men, that have a vow on them.

text.NRSV: Join these men, go through the rite of purification with them, and pay for the shaving of their heads. Thus all will know that there is nothing in what they have been told about you, but that you yourself observe and guard the law.
text.Rotherham: Taking these unto thee, be purified with them, and spend something upon them, that they may shave their head; and all will get to know, that the things which they have heard rumoured concerning thee, are, nothing,—on the contrary, thou thyself, dost keep the ranks, guarding the law.
text.KJV: Them take, and purify thyself with them, and be at charges with them, that they may shave their heads: and all may know that those things, whereof they were informed concerning thee, are nothing; but that thou thyself also walkest orderly, and keepest the law.
text.NABRE: Take these men and purify yourself with them, and pay their expenses that they may have their heads shaved. In this way everyone will know that there is nothing to the reports they have been given about you but that you yourself live in observance of the law.
text.DRB: Taking these unto thee, sanctify thyself with them; and bestow on them, that they may shave their heads: and all shall know that the things which they heard of thee, are false: but that thyself also walkest keeping the Law.

text.NRSV: But as for the Gentiles who have become believers, we have sent a letter with our judgment that they should abstain from what has been sacrificed to idols and from blood and from what is strangled and from fornication.”
text.Rotherham: But, concerning them of the nations who have believed, we ourselves, sent, deciding that they should be guarding themselves, both as to idol sacrifice, and blood, and what is strangled, and fornication.
text.KJV: As touching the Gentiles which believe, we have written and concluded that they observe no such thing, save only that they keep themselves from things offered to idols, and from blood, and from strangled, and from fornication.
text.NABRE: As for the Gentiles who have come to believe, we sent them our decision that they abstain from meat sacrificed to idols, from blood, from the meat of strangled animals, and from unlawful marriage.”
text.DRB: But concerning them that believe of the Gentiles, we have written, decreeing that they should refrain themselves from the immolated to idols, and blood, and suffocated, and fornication.

text.NRSV: Then Paul took the men, and the next day, having purified himself, he entered the temple with them, making public the completion of the days of purification when the sacrifice would be made for each of them.
text.Rotherham: Then Paul, taking unto him the men, on the next day, with them, being purified, began entering into the temple to declare the filling up of the days of the purification—until, the offering, had been presented for each one of them.
text.KJV: Then Paul took the men, and the next day purifying himself with them entered into the temple, to signify the accomplishment of the days of purification, until that an offering should be offered for every one of them.
text.NABRE: So Paul took the men, and on the next day after purifying himself together with them entered the temple to give notice of the day when the purification would be completed and the offering made for each of them.
text.DRB: Then Paul taking the men unto him, the next day being purified with them entered into the temple, shewing the accomplishment of the days of the purification, until an oblation was offered for every one of them.

text.NRSV: When the seven days were almost completed, the Jews from Asia, who had seen him in the temple, stirred up the whole crowd. They seized him,
text.Rotherham: When, however, the seven days were on the point of being concluded, the Jews from Asia, observing him in the temple, began to urge-on all the multitude, and thrust upon them their hands,
text.KJV: And when the seven days were almost ended, the Jews which were of Asia, when they saw him in the temple, stirred up all the people, and laid hands on him,
text.NABRE: Paul’s Arrest. When the seven days were nearly completed, the Jews from the province of Asia noticed him in the temple, stirred up the whole crowd, and laid hands on him,
text.DRB: But whiles the seven days were a finishing, those Jews that were of Asia, when they had seen him in the temple, stirred up all the people, and laid hands upon him,

text.NRSV: shouting, “Fellow Israelites, help! This is the man who is teaching everyone everywhere against our people, our law, and this place; more than that, he has actually brought Greeks into the temple and has defiled this holy place.”
text.Rotherham: crying out—Israelites! be giving help! This, is the man who, against the people, and the law, and this place, is teaching, all men everywhere; furthermore, even Greeks, hath he brought into the temple, and hath profaned this holy place.
text.KJV: Crying out, Men of Israel, help: This is the man, that teacheth all men every where against the people, and the law, and this place: and further brought Greeks also into the temple, and hath polluted this holy place.
text.NABRE: shouting, “Fellow Israelites, help us. This is the man who is teaching everyone everywhere against the people and the law and this place, and what is more, he has even brought Greeks into the temple and defiled this sacred place.”
text.DRB: crying: Ye men of Israel, help: this is the man that against the people and the Law and this place teaching all men every-where, hath also moreover brought in Gentiles into the temple, and hath violated this holy place.

text.NRSV: For they had previously seen Trophimus the Ephesian with him in the city, and they supposed that Paul had brought him into the temple.
text.Rotherham: For they had before seen Trophimus, the Ephesian, in the city along with him, whom they were supposing, Paul, had brought, into the temple.
text.KJV: (For they had seen before with him in the city Trophimus an Ephesian, whom they supposed that Paul had brought into the temple.)
text.NABRE: For they had previously seen Trophimus the Ephesian in the city with him and supposed that Paul had brought him into the temple.
text.DRB: (For they had seen Trophimus the Ephesian in the city with him, whom they supposed that Paul had brought into the temple.)

text.NRSV: Then all the city was aroused, and the people rushed together. They seized Paul and dragged him out of the temple, and immediately the doors were shut.
text.Rotherham: And the whole city was set in motion, and there took place a running together of the people, and, laying hold of Paul, they proceeded to drag him outside the temple, and, straightway, the doors were made fast.
text.KJV: And all the city was moved, and the people ran together: and they took Paul, and drew him out of the temple: and forthwith the doors were shut.
text.NABRE: The whole city was in turmoil with people rushing together. They seized Paul and dragged him out of the temple, and immediately the gates were closed.
text.DRB: And the whole city was in an uproar: and there was made a concourse of the people. And apprehending Paul, they drew him forth of the temple: and immediately the doors were shut.

text.NRSV: While they were trying to kill him, word came to the tribune of the cohort that all Jerusalem was in an uproar.
text.Rotherham: When, also, they were seeking to slay him, there was carried up information, unto the captain of the band, that all Jerusalem was in confusion:—
text.KJV: And as they went about to kill him, tidings came unto the chief captain of the band, that all Jerusalem was in an uproar.
text.NABRE: While they were trying to kill him, a report reached the cohort commander that all Jerusalem was rioting.
text.DRB: And as they sought to kill him, it was told the Tribune of the band, That all Jerusalem is in a confusion.

text.NRSV: Immediately he took soldiers and centurions and ran down to them. When they saw the tribune and the soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
text.Rotherham: who, instantly taking unto him soldiers and centurions, ran down upon them; and, they, seeing the captain and the soldiers, left off striking Paul.
text.KJV: Who immediately took soldiers and centurions, and ran down unto them: and when they saw the chief captain and the soldiers, they left beating of Paul.
text.NABRE: He immediately took soldiers and centurions and charged down on them. When they saw the commander and the soldiers they stopped beating Paul.
text.DRB: Who forthwith taking unto him soldiers and Centurions, ran down to them. Who, when they had seen the Tribune and the soldiers, ceased to strike Paul.

text.NRSV: Then the tribune came, arrested him, and ordered him to be bound with two chains; he inquired who he was and what he had done.
text.Rotherham: Then, the captain, drawing near, laid hold of him, and ordered him to be bound with two chains,—and began to enquire, who he might be, and what he had done;
text.KJV: Then the chief captain came near, and took him, and commanded him to be bound with two chains; and demanded who he was, and what he had done.
text.NABRE: The cohort commander came forward, arrested him, and ordered him to be secured with two chains; he tried to find out who he might be and what he had done.
text.DRB: Then the Tribune coming near apprehended him, and commanded him to be bound with two chains: and he demanded who he was, and what he had done.

text.NRSV: Some in the crowd shouted one thing, some another; and as he could not learn the facts because of the uproar, he ordered him to be brought into the barracks.
text.Rotherham: but, others, were calling out, something else, in the multitude; and so, as he could not get to know the certainty, because of the tumult, he ordered him to be brought into the castle.
text.KJV: And some cried one thing, some another, among the multitude: and when he could not know the certainty for the tumult, he commanded him to be carried into the castle.
text.NABRE: Some in the mob shouted one thing, others something else; so, since he was unable to ascertain the truth because of the uproar, he ordered Paul to be brought into the compound.
text.DRB: And some cried one thing, some another, in the multitude. And whereas he could not know the certainty for the tumult, he commanded him to be led into the castle.

text.NRSV: When Paul came to the steps, the violence of the mob was so great that he had to be carried by the soldiers.
text.Rotherham: And, when he came unto the stairs, so it was that he was borne along by the soldiers, because of the force of the multitude;
text.KJV: And when he came upon the stairs, so it was, that he was borne of the soldiers for the violence of the people.
text.NABRE: When he reached the steps, he was carried by the soldiers because of the violence of the mob,
text.DRB: And when he was come to the stairs, it chanced that he was carried of the soldiers because of the violence of the people.

text.NRSV: The crowd that followed kept shouting, “Away with him!”
text.Rotherham: for the throng of the people was following, crying out—Away with him!
text.KJV: For the multitude of the people followed after, crying, Away with him.
text.NABRE: for a crowd of people followed and shouted, “Away with him!”
text.DRB: For the multitude of the people followed, crying: Away with him.

text.NRSV: Just as Paul was about to be brought into the barracks, he said to the tribune, “May I say something to you?” The tribune replied, “Do you know Greek?
text.Rotherham: But, when he was on the point of being taken into the castle, Paul saith unto the captain—Is it allowed me, to say somewhat unto thee? And he said—With Greek, art thou acquainted?
text.KJV: And as Paul was to be led into the castle, he said unto the chief captain, May I speak unto thee? Who said, Canst thou speak Greek?
text.NABRE: Just as Paul was about to be taken into the compound, he said to the cohort commander, “May I say something to you?” He replied, “Do you speak Greek?
text.DRB: And when Paul began to be brought into the castle, he saith to the Tribune: is it lawful for me to speak something to thee? Who said: Canst thou speak Greek?

text.NRSV: Then you are not the Egyptian who recently stirred up a revolt and led the four thousand assassins out into the wilderness?”
text.Rotherham: Not, then, art, thou, the Egyptian, who, before these days, stirred up to sedition and led out into the wilderness the four-thousand men of the Assassins?
text.KJV: Art not thou that Egyptian, which before these days madest an uproar, and leddest out into the wilderness four thousand men that were murderers?
text.NABRE: So then you are not the Egyptian who started a revolt some time ago and led the four thousand assassins into the desert?”
text.DRB: Art not thou the Ægyptian that before these days did raise a tumult, and didst lead forth into the desert four thousand men that were murderers?

text.NRSV: Paul replied, “I am a Jew, from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of an important city; I beg you, let me speak to the people.”
text.Rotherham: And Paul said—I, indeed, am a Jew, of Tarsus in Cilicia,—a citizen, of no obscure city; but I beseech thee, give me leave to speak unto the people!
text.KJV: But Paul said, I am a man which am a Jew of Tarsus, a city in Cilicia, a citizen of no mean city: and, I beseech thee, suffer me to speak unto the people.
text.NABRE: Paul answered, “I am a Jew, of Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of no mean city; I request you to permit me to speak to the people.”
text.DRB: And Paul said to him: I am a man truly a Jew of Tarsus, a citizen not of an obscure city of Cilicia. And I desire thee, permit me to speak to the people.

text.NRSV: When he had given him permission, Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people for silence; and when there was a great hush, he addressed them in the Hebrew language, saying:
text.Rotherham: And, when he had given leave, Paul, standing upon the stairs, waved with his hand unto the people; and, when great silence was secured, he addressed them in the Hebrew language, saying—
text.KJV: And when he had given him licence, Paul stood on the stairs, and beckoned with the hand unto the people. And when there was made a great silence, he spake unto them in the Hebrew tongue, saying,
text.NABRE: When he had given his permission, Paul stood on the steps and motioned with his hand to the people; and when all was quiet he addressed them in Hebrew.
text.DRB: And when he had permitted him, Paul standing on the stairs, beckoned with his hand to the people. And great silence being made, he spake unto them in the Hebrew tongue, saying.

text.NRSV: “Brothers and fathers, listen to the defense that I now make before you.”
text.Rotherham: Brethren and fathers! Hear ye, the defence, which I now make unto you:—
text.KJV: Men, brethren, and fathers, hear ye my defence which I make now unto you.
text.NABRE: “My brothers and fathers, listen to what I am about to say to you in my defense.”
text.DRB: Men brethren and fathers, hear what account I do render now unto you.

text.NRSV: When they heard him addressing them in Hebrew, they became even more quiet. Then he said:
text.Rotherham: And, when they heard that, in the Hebrew language, he had begun to address them, they kept the more quiet. And he saith—
text.KJV: (And when they heard that he spake in the Hebrew tongue to them, they kept the more silence: and he saith,)
text.NABRE: When they heard him addressing them in Hebrew they became all the more quiet. And he continued,
text.DRB: (And when they had heard that he spake to them in the Hebrew tongue, they did the more keep silence.

text.NRSV: “I am a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia, but brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, educated strictly according to our ancestral law, being zealous for God, just as all of you are today.
text.Rotherham: I, am a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but nurtured in this city, at the feet of Gamaliel,—trained after the strictness of our ancestral law; being jealous for God just as, all ye, are this day;
text.KJV: I am verily a man which am a Jew, born in Tarsus, a city in Cilicia, yet brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, and taught according to the perfect manner of the law of the fathers, and was zealous toward God, as ye all are this day.
text.NABRE: “I am a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia, but brought up in this city. At the feet of Gamaliel I was educated strictly in our ancestral law and was zealous for God, just as all of you are today.
text.DRB: And he saith:) I am a man a Jew, born at Tarsus in Cilicia, but brought up in this city, at the feet of Gamaliel instructed according to the verity of the law of the fathers, an emulator of the Law as also all you are this day:

text.NRSV: I persecuted this Way up to the point of death by binding both men and women and putting them in prison,
text.Rotherham: and, this way, I persecuted unto the death, binding and delivering up into prisons both men and women:—
text.KJV: And I persecuted this way unto the death, binding and delivering into prisons both men and women.
text.NABRE: I persecuted this Way to death, binding both men and women and delivering them to prison.
text.DRB: who persecuted this way unto death, binding & delivering into custodies men & women,

text.NRSV: as the high priest and the whole council of elders can testify about me. From them I also received letters to the brothers in Damascus, and I went there in order to bind those who were there and to bring them back to Jerusalem for punishment.
text.Rotherham: as, even the High-priest, beareth me witness, and all the Eldership,—from whom, letters also, accepting to the brethren, unto Damascus, was I journeying, to bring them who were there bound unto Jerusalem, that they might be punished.
text.KJV: As also the high priest doth bear me witness, and all the estate of the elders: from whom also I received letters unto the brethren, and went to Damascus, to bring them which were there bound unto Jerusalem, for to be punished.
text.NABRE: Even the high priest and the whole council of elders can testify on my behalf. For from them I even received letters to the brothers and set out for Damascus to bring back to Jerusalem in chains for punishment those there as well.
text.DRB: as the high Priest doth give me testimony, and all the Ancients. Of whom receiving letters also to the brethren, I went to Damascus, that I might bring them thence bound to Jerusalem, to be punished.

text.NRSV: “While I was on my way and approaching Damascus, about noon a great light from heaven suddenly shone about me.
text.Rotherham: But it befell me, as I was journeying and drawing nigh unto Damascus, that, about mid-day—suddenly—out of heaven, there flashed a great light all around me;
text.KJV: And it came to pass, that, as I made my journey, and was come nigh unto Damascus about noon, suddenly there shone from heaven a great light round about me.
text.NABRE: “On that journey as I drew near to Damascus, about noon a great light from the sky suddenly shone around me.
text.DRB: And it came to pass as I was going, and drawing nigh to Damascus at mid-day, suddenly from Heaven there shone round about me much light:

text.NRSV: I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’
text.Rotherham: I fell also to the ground, and heard a voice saying unto me—Saul! Saul! Why, me, art thou persecuting?
text.KJV: And I fell unto the ground, and heard a voice saying unto me, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?
text.NABRE: I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’
text.DRB: and falling on the ground, I heard a voice saying to me: Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?

text.NRSV: I answered, ‘Who are you, Lord?’ Then he said to me, ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth whom you are persecuting.’
text.Rotherham: And, I, answered—Who art thou, Lord? And he said unto me—I, am Jesus the Nazarene, whom, thou, art persecuting!
text.KJV: And I answered, Who art thou, Lord? And he said unto me, I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom thou persecutest.
text.NABRE: I replied, ‘Who are you, sir?’ And he said to me, ‘I am Jesus the Nazorean whom you are persecuting.’
text.DRB: And I answered: Who are thou Lord? And he said to me: I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom thou persecutest.

text.NRSV: Now those who were with me saw the light but did not hear the voice of the one who was speaking to me.
text.Rotherham: Now, they who were with me, beheld, indeed, the light, but heard not, the voice, of him that was speaking with me
text.KJV: And they that were with me saw indeed the light, and were afraid; but they heard not the voice of him that spake to me.
text.NABRE: My companions saw the light but did not hear the voice of the one who spoke to me.
text.DRB: And they that were with me, saw the light indeed, but the voice they heard not of him that spake with me.

text.NRSV: I asked, ‘What am I to do, Lord?’ The Lord said to me, ‘Get up and go to Damascus; there you will be told everything that has been assigned to you to do.’
text.Rotherham: And said—What shall I do, Lord? And, the Lord, said unto me—Arise, and be going thy way into Damascus, and, there, shall it be told thee of all things which are appointed for thee to do.
text.KJV: And I said, What shall I do, Lord? And the Lord said unto me, Arise, and go into Damascus; and there it shall be told thee of all things which are appointed for thee to do.
text.NABRE: I asked, ‘What shall I do, sir?’ The Lord answered me, ‘Get up and go into Damascus, and there you will be told about everything appointed for you to do.’
text.DRB: And I said: What shall I do Lord? And our Lord said to me: Arise and go to Damascus; and there it shall be told thee of all things that thou must do.

text.NRSV: Since I could not see because of the brightness of that light, those who were with me took my hand and led me to Damascus.
text.Rotherham: But, as I could not see clearly owing to the glory of that light, being led by the hand of them who were with me, I came into Damascus.
text.KJV: And when I could not see for the glory of that light, being led by the hand of them that were with me, I came into Damascus.
text.NABRE: Since I could see nothing because of the brightness of that light, I was led by hand by my companions and entered Damascus.
text.DRB: And whereas I did not see for the brightness of that light, being led of my companions by the hand, I came to Damascus.

text.NRSV: “A certain Ananias, who was a devout man according to the law and well spoken of by all the Jews living there,
text.Rotherham: And, one Ananias, a man devout according to the law, well-attested by all the Jews that dwelt there,
text.KJV: And one Ananias, a devout man according to the law, having a good report of all the Jews which dwelt there,
text.NABRE: “A certain Ananias, a devout observer of the law, and highly spoken of by all the Jews who lived there,
text.DRB: And one Ananias, a man according to the Law, having testimony of all the Jews' inhabitants,

text.NRSV: came to me; and standing beside me, he said, ‘Brother Saul, regain your sight!’ In that very hour I regained my sight and saw him.
text.Rotherham: coming unto me, and standing over me, said—Saul, brother! look up. And, I, in that very hour, looked up on him.
text.KJV: Came unto me, and stood, and said unto me, Brother Saul, receive thy sight. And the same hour I looked up upon him.
text.NABRE: came to me and stood there and said, ‘Saul, my brother, regain your sight.’ And at that very moment I regained my sight and saw him.
text.DRB: coming to me, and standing by me, said to me: Brother Saul, look up. And I the self-same hour looked upon him.

text.NRSV: Then he said, ‘The God of our ancestors has chosen you to know his will, to see the Righteous One and to hear his own voice;
text.Rotherham: And he said—The God of our fathers, hath chosen thee to get to know his will, and to see the Righteous One,—and to hear a voice out of his mouth.
text.KJV: And he said, The God of our fathers hath chosen thee, that thou shouldest know his will, and see that Just One, and shouldest hear the voice of his mouth.
text.NABRE: Then he said, ‘The God of our ancestors designated you to know his will, to see the Righteous One, and to hear the sound of his voice;
text.DRB: But he said: The God of our fathers hath preordained thee, that thou shouldest know his will, and see the just one, and hear a voice from his mouth:

text.NRSV: for you will be his witness to all the world of what you have seen and heard.
text.Rotherham: Because thou shalt be a witness to him unto all men, of the things which thou hast seen and heard.
text.KJV: For thou shalt be his witness unto all men of what thou hast seen and heard.
text.NABRE: for you will be his witness before all to what you have seen and heard.
text.DRB: because thou shalt be his witness to all men, of those things which thou hast seen and heard.

text.NRSV: And now why do you delay? Get up, be baptized, and have your sins washed away, calling on his name.’
text.Rotherham: And, now, what art thou going to do? Arise, and get thyself immersed, and have thy sins bathed away, calling upon his name.
text.KJV: And now why tarriest thou? arise, and be baptized, and wash away thy sins, calling on the name of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Now, why delay? Get up and have yourself baptized and your sins washed away, calling upon his name.’
text.DRB: And now what tarriest thou? Rise up, and be baptized, and wash away thy sins invocating his name.

text.NRSV: “After I had returned to Jerusalem and while I was praying in the temple, I fell into a trance
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when had returned unto Jerusalem, and was praying in the temple, that I came to be in a trance,
text.KJV: And it came to pass, that, when I was come again to Jerusalem, even while I prayed in the temple, I was in a trance;
text.NABRE: “After I had returned to Jerusalem and while I was praying in the temple, I fell into a trance
text.DRB: And it befell me returning into Jerusalem, and praying in the temple, that I was in a trance,

text.NRSV: and saw Jesus saying to me, ‘Hurry and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not accept your testimony about me.’
text.Rotherham: and saw him, saying unto me—Haste thee, and go forth speedily out of Jerusalem, inasmuch as they will not accept thy witness concerning me.
text.KJV: And saw him saying unto me, Make haste, and get thee quickly out of Jerusalem: for they will not receive thy testimony concerning me.
text.NABRE: and saw the Lord saying to me, ‘Hurry, leave Jerusalem at once, because they will not accept your testimony about me.’
text.DRB: and saw him saying unto me: Make haste, and depart quickly out of Jerusalem: because they will not receive thy testimony of me.

text.NRSV: And I said, ‘Lord, they themselves know that in every synagogue I imprisoned and beat those who believed in you.
text.Rotherham: And, I, said—Lord! they themselves, well know that I was imprisoning and beating in every synagogue them who were believing on thee.
text.KJV: And I said, Lord, they know that I imprisoned and beat in every synagogue them that believed on thee:
text.NABRE: But I replied, ‘Lord, they themselves know that from synagogue to synagogue I used to imprison and beat those who believed in you.
text.DRB: And I said, Lord, they know that I did cast into prison and beat in every Synagogue them that believed in thee.

text.NRSV: And while the blood of your witness Stephen was shed, I myself was standing by, approving and keeping the coats of those who killed him.’
text.Rotherham: And, when the blood of Stephen thy witness was being shed, even I myself, was standing by, and approving, and guarding the mantles of them who were slaying him.
text.KJV: And when the blood of thy martyr Stephen was shed, I also was standing by, and consenting unto his death, and kept the raiment of them that slew him.
text.NABRE: And when the blood of your witness Stephen was being shed, I myself stood by giving my approval and keeping guard over the cloaks of his murderers.’
text.DRB: And when the blood of Steven thy witness was shed, I stood by and consented, and kept the garments of them that killed him.

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, ‘Go, for I will send you far away to the Gentiles.’”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto me—Be taking thy journey; because, I, unto nations afar off, will send thee.
text.KJV: And he said unto me, Depart: for I will send thee far hence unto the Gentiles.
text.NABRE: Then he said to me, ‘Go, I shall send you far away to the Gentiles.’”
text.DRB: And he said to me: Go, for into the Gentiles a far will I send thee.

text.NRSV: Up to this point they listened to him, but then they shouted, “Away with such a fellow from the earth! For he should not be allowed to live.”
text.Rotherham: And they hearkened unto him as far as this word, and lifted up their voice, saying—Away from the earth, with such a man as this, for it is not fit that he should live.
text.KJV: And they gave him audience unto this word, and then lifted up their voices, and said, Away with such a fellow from the earth: for it is not fit that he should live.
text.NABRE: Paul Imprisoned. They listened to him until he said this, but then they raised their voices and shouted, “Take such a one as this away from the earth. It is not right that he should live.”
text.DRB: And they heard him until this word, and they lifted up their voice, saying: Away with such an one from the earth: for it is not meet he should live.

text.NRSV: And while they were shouting, throwing off their cloaks, and tossing dust into the air,
text.Rotherham: Now, as they were both making an outcry and tearing their mantles,—dust, also were throwing into the air,
text.KJV: And as they cried out, and cast off their clothes, and threw dust into the air,
text.NABRE: And as they were yelling and throwing off their cloaks and flinging dust into the air,
text.DRB: And when they cried out, and threw off their garments, and cast dust into the air,

text.NRSV: the tribune directed that he was to be brought into the barracks, and ordered him to be examined by flogging, to find out the reason for this outcry against him.
text.Rotherham: the captain ordered him to be brought into the castle, saying, that, with scourging, he should be put to the test,—that he might find out, for what cause they were, thus, clamouring against him.
text.KJV: The chief captain commanded him to be brought into the castle, and bade that he should be examined by scourging; that he might know wherefore they cried so against him.
text.NABRE: the cohort commander ordered him to be brought into the compound and gave instruction that he be interrogated under the lash to determine the reason why they were making such an outcry against him.
text.DRB: the Tribune commanded him to be carried into the castle, and to be beaten with whips, & that he should be tormented: to know for what cause they did so cry at him.

text.NRSV: But when they had tied him up with thongs, Paul said to the centurion who was standing by, “Is it legal for you to flog a Roman citizen who is uncondemned?”
text.Rotherham: But, when they had stretched him out with straps, Paul said unto the by-standing centurion—A Roman, and uncondemned, is it allowed you to be scourging?
text.KJV: And as they bound him with thongs, Paul said unto the centurion that stood by, Is it lawful for you to scourge a man that is a Roman, and uncondemned?
text.NABRE: But when they had stretched him out for the whips, Paul said to the centurion on duty, “Is it lawful for you to scourge a man who is a Roman citizen and has not been tried?”
text.DRB: And when they had bound him very straight with thongs, Paul saith to the Centurion standing by him: Is it lawful for you to whip a man that is a Roman and uncondemned?

text.NRSV: When the centurion heard that, he went to the tribune and said to him, “What are you about to do? This man is a Roman citizen.”
